title: [EPIC] FLAIR architecture re-ratification: heresy purge + grading/goldens/mechanism-policy/region-spine ADRs (full operational GUI)
description: OPERATOR DIRECTIVE 2026-06-21 (comprehensive, orchestrated): deal with the FLAIR architecture comprehensively NOW so we build the right things. Triggered by the grading audit (workflow wf_a094680b-fce) which proved: (1) the live FLAIR oracle grades 'BY CONSTRUCTION' -- ppm_flair_check computes expected RGBs from the SAME palette.h the kernel renders from, so a wrong palette is invisible (the WL-0053 'very wrong' palette passed every gate); (2) the decomp goldens (../system7-decomp, ../win31-decomp) are NOT a live golden master -- only test-clut diffs one 256-entry CLUT, and clut.h is not even rendered with; win31-decomp has ZERO build wiring; (3) FLAIR renders with preview.webp MOCK-UP samples (operator: 'very wrong'); (4) SSIM is unimplemented (harness/ssim.c absent, make ssim is stub_fail); (5) NO behaviour is graded on the booted OS (no event loop; flair_tick_advance has zero callers; kbd ISR feeds the DOS ring not the FLAIR ring); (6) color policy is smeared across 5 duplicated index->RGB switches (chrome/control/dialog/render/palette) = the mechanism/policy tangle. SCOPE: (A) HERESY PURGE -- hard-revoke (management decree) every ratified committee decision that contradicts the locked principles (e.g. ADR-0004 OD-4 seafoam; the re30.4-palette-source committee ruling; the by-construction grading premise). (B) ARCHITECTURE COMMITTEES -- ratify new/amended ADRs for: mechanism/policy(decoration) split; ATKINSON as the single shared dual-heritage spine (QuickDraw Rgn + GDI HRGN); grade-against-INDEPENDENT-decomp-golden (kill by-construction; wire system7-decomp + win31-decomp as live masters, test-clut pattern); the Initech color canon (teal #8DDCDC + decomp-sourced, NOT preview.webp); behavioural grading + the booted-OS event loop + a System-7 behavioural reference; SSIM implement-or-formally-defer; era-layering (Sys7 now / Sys8 Platinum accretes). LOCKED PRINCIPLES (no relitigation, operator): see bd memories 'ratified-operator-directive-2026-06-21' and 'initech-color-canon'. END STATE: a ratified design-doc set that leads to a FULL OPERATIONAL GUI in accordance with these principles. Children: initech-28kn (mech/policy + region-spine amendment) + committee-output ADRs/beads filed as the orchestration runs.
notes: STEP 7 (5l5z) CORE DELIVERED: the live interactive FLAIR desktop (drag + menus) ships on the emulated 386 -- the re-flair epic END STATE 'a FULL OPERATIONAL GUI' is substantially reached. ALSO this session: the window-chrome FIDELITY arc (umbrella initech-hmll, CLOSED) landed all 6 elements graded vs the independent system7-decomp goldens (drop-shadow/box/scrollbar/title-bevel + the prior phase/title). Remaining 5l5z follow-ons are filed polish (menu close-restore+CombineRgn, mouse-ACK, Bochs mouse probe, arena ASLR, live drop-shadow). The 7-step re-flair roadmap steps 1-7 are now all materially complete.

title: Latent: one baked-program output 'build' is dropped deterministically (reproduces on KVM)
description: While building the test-datetime baked program (beads initech-yv9), found that exactly ONE field-build in a multi-field output sequence is dropped/garbled deterministically per build (the dropped field shifts with code layout: MIN, HOUR, DOW observed). NOT a TCG artifact -- it reproduces under -accel kvm (real hardware) AND -accel tcg,one-insn-per-tb=on. The INT 21h handlers return CORRECT values (host oracle test-int21 + the values that DO appear confirm this); the corruption is in the GUEST program's instruction/data stream during execution. Symptoms: a cat-string loop appends nothing (string's first byte reads as NUL) or a store writes to a shifted address, at a position-dependent code site. Hypothesis: a kernel/loader memory-layout interaction corrupts the running program's code/data page during INT 21h processing (the PSP/DTA/env or staging copy region near 0x20000-0x20300). Worked around in os/milton/datetime_program.asm by emitting every field TWICE (one clean copy always survives the single per-run drop). Repro: build the datetime image, boot with -rtc base=...,clock=vm; observe one DT-* field missing without the duplicate-emit. Needs root-cause: instrument the program's code/data page for writes during a multi-INT-21h-write run; check loader staging/PSP/DTA overlap with PROGRAM_IMAGE (0x20100).
notes: ROOT-CAUSED by orchestrator (was mis-hypothesized as guest-exec corruption). The LOADER corrupts the program: load_program (os/milton/loader.c ~L224-232) does loader_memcpy(PROGRAM_IMAGE=0x20100, image, image_len) to copy the whole .COM, THEN writes the 2-byte empty env block at ENV_BLOCK=0x20200 = PROGRAM_IMAGE + 0x100. So for ANY program > 256 bytes, the env-zero OVERWRITES the program's own bytes at file offset 0x100-0x101 -> deterministic 2-byte corruption (reads as NUL). Matches every symptom (position-dependent, reproduces on KVM, 'first byte NUL'). Small programs (<256B, e.g. GREET/type/dir) are unaffected (env-zero lands past them); the resident shell is unaffected (not loader-loaded). CRITICAL: blocks InitechBase + Turbo Initech (both >256B). FIX: relocate ENV_BLOCK out of the program-image arena (memory_map.h is LOCKED spec -- deliberate change per Rule 8 + this issue) and remove the datetime_program.asm emit-twice WORKAROUND so the gate honestly verifies the fix. Mutation-prove: a program with a string at offset 0x100 must round-trip clean.
close_reason: ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED (was masked by an emit-twice workaround). Cause: load_program wrote the 2-byte env block at ENV_BLOCK=0x20200=PROGRAM_IMAGE+0x100 after the image copy, zeroing 2 bytes of any .COM >256B. Fix: relocated ENV_BLOCK to 0x5F000 (dedicated region below the stack, outside the image arena; PROGRAM_IMAGE_MAX capped to ENV_BLOCK). Workaround removed; test-datetime now emits each field once and is green -- proving the fix. Verified full vector green under load.

title: Screendump QEMU keystones flake on fixed wall-clock timeout (non-deterministic gate)
description: test-tracer-boot, test-boot, test-fs (and rarely test-shell) grab a framebuffer at a fixed --timeout-ms (~6s) and assert painted pixels via ppm_text_check. Under back-to-back QEMU boots (e.g. inside 'make test'/'make test-emu') the guest often has not rendered the banner/desktop within the wall clock, so the screendump is blank (fg=0) and the gate goes RED -- a wall-clock race, not a logic failure (they pass when the box is idle, confirmed: 3/3 tracer-boot, 2/2 boot, 2/2 fs standalone; RED inside the aggregate). A non-deterministic gate is worse than none -- it trains us to ignore red and makes 'make test' (initech-4mc) unusable as the rock-solid signal. FIX: replace the fixed-wall-clock screendump with poll-until-rendered (screendump-on-marker, or retry/grow the deadline until a serial 'rendered' marker appears, or screenshot when the guest signals paint-complete over serial). Must stay deterministic (Rule 11) and not weaken what ppm_text_check asserts (Law 2).
acceptance_criteria: 'make test' (host+emu, all 44 gates) passes deterministically 5x in a row on a loaded box; the screendump is captured only after the guest has actually painted (marker-driven, not fixed wall clock); ppm_text_check assertions unchanged; no sleeps that just paper over the race.
close_reason: Fixed: added --screendump-after MARKER; screendump now waits for the guest paint-complete serial marker (BANNER/DIR-OK) before grabbing, with the wall-clock as backstop and honest-RED fall-through. Verified: make test GREEN 2x under 6-core load (previously RED), negative control (NEVERPAINT) terminates at backstop and fails honestly, no hang.

title: Aggregate 'make test' gate: wire the full green oracle vector (replace M0 stub)
description: make test is still the M0 stub_fail. There is no single command that runs the whole green oracle vector; the ~22-gate sweep (WL-0009) is run by hand. 'Rock solid' is unassertable without one green gate. Wire a real aggregate: test-unit (all host unit oracles + their mutant gates) + test-emu (all in-emulator QEMU keystones) + test = both. Milestone stubs (test-region M3, test-dbase M6, test-compiler M7, selfhost/ddc M8) stay OUT of the green aggregate. Fail loud on first red.
acceptance_criteria: 'make test' runs every currently-green host oracle + mutant + QEMU keystone in one invocation and exits 0 only if all pass; flipping one branch in any gated subsystem makes 'make test' exit nonzero; milestone stubs are excluded (or in a separate allowed-to-fail target); ASCII-clean; no new nondeterminism.
close_reason: make test now runs the full green vector (31 host + 13 emu = 44 gates), fails loud on first red, mutation-proven to bite; deterministically GREEN under load after initech-3pe. Milestone stubs (region/dbase/compiler/selfhost/ddc) and Bochs excluded.

title: stage2 -> C kernel handoff: boot_info struct + ROM font capture + flat kernel load
description: The tracer ends by filling the LFB seafoam and halting in asm; there is no C-kernel handoff. To boot InitechDOS to text we need: (1) a flat C kernel binary (os/milton, ADR-0003 DEC-08) linked + loaded from the disk image; (2) stage2 to capture the VGA ROM 8x16 font (INT 10h AX=1130h BH=06h -> ES:BP, 256*16 bytes) in real mode and stash it; (3) a boot_info struct (lfb_addr/pitch/bpp/width/height + font ptr) passed from stage2 to the C kernel entry; (4) the far jump into the C kernel in 32-bit flat mode. Foundational for yqb (console) + bea (banner). PRD Sec 5; ADR-0003 DEC-08 (flat kernel handed off by stage2).
acceptance_criteria: Boot reaches the C kernel entry (distinct serial marker, e.g. KERNEL), no triple-fault, boot_info fields readable in C; reproducible image build.

title: Diagnostic-message catalogue enforcement (ADR-0003 DEC-13 / App C)
description: All kernel diagnostic output references spec/dos_messages.json ONLY (no inline literals). 16-message controlled vocabulary MSG-DOS-0001..0016 verbatim. Oracle: extract strings from the image, diff vs spec; any added/removed/reworded message fails. DEC-13.
acceptance_criteria: Emitted messages match spec/dos_messages.json verbatim; oracle fails on any divergence.
close_reason: DEC-13 controlled-vocabulary enforcement landed: spec/dos_messages.json -> generated build/dos_messages.h codegen (single source of truth); command.c references MSG_DOS_* macros (no inline literals); new mutation-proven oracle test-dosmsg (consistency + image-presence + source-purity teeth) + test-dosmsg-mutant. Full vector green 43 host + 18 emu = 61 gates. Orchestrator independently mutation-proved both teeth bite. Unblocks 509.8 + 509.10.

title: PREREQ: install emulator + asm toolchain (qemu-system-x86, nasm; later bochs/86Box)
description: Environment is missing QEMU (no qemu-system-i386/x86_64), nasm, and bochs. gcc/clang/ld/mtools/objcopy present; 32-bit freestanding builds work. Everything that boots in an emulator is blocked until QEMU lands. Needs sudo (no passwordless sudo in this env) -> the USER must run the install. bochs + 86Box can come at M1 (tri-emulator gate); QEMU + nasm are needed now for the tracer bullet.
acceptance_criteria: qemu-system-i386 (or x86_64) and nasm on PATH; 'make run' can launch QEMU. bochs/86Box deferred to M1.
notes: Done by user. qemu-system-i386/x86_64 8.2.2, nasm 2.16.01, bochs all on PATH. Unblocks f2s (harness), f8v.1 (smoke), f8v.2 (tracer boot), f8v.4 (keystone), x0i (bochs harness). 86Box still TBD for M1.

title: Tracer bullet keystone: boot->draw->screendump->SSIM-guide + serial milestone, green end-to-end
description: The bullet flies. Compose tracer boot + tracer draw, take a screendump, score it with the SSIM tool (m0-4) against a tiny single-window fixture crop (m0-6), and assert the serial milestones. This green signal is the gate that unblocks fleshing out M1-M8. PRD §3, §8, §11 M0.5.
acceptance_criteria: One command produces: boot-stage serial milestones + a screendump + an SSIM score for the single-window/one-glyph render, all green.
notes: RE-POINT (DOS-first, ADR-0003): the tracer KEYSTONE is now the DOS slice end-to-end: boot -> banner -> COMMAND.COM -> DIR over a FAT volume -> TYPE a file -> serial milestone markers + ECH differential. Replaces the GUI/SSIM keystone (deferred to ADR-0004).

title: Tracer draw: one window-rect + one hardcoded Chicago glyph blitted to the LFB (minimal region clip)
description: The thinnest REAL draw path (shared with M3; M3 fleshes out the full region algebra/blitter/font strikes). Draw a single System-7-ish window rectangle and blit ONE Chicago glyph at a fixed position, clipped by a minimal region. Uses the v0 glyph+palette from m0-5. PRD §6.2, §6.3, §11 M3.
acceptance_criteria: A window rect with one legible Chicago glyph renders to the LFB at a known position.
notes: RE-POINT (DOS-first, ADR-0003): the tracer DRAW step is replaced by the DOS slice. New scope: boot -> 'InitechDOS Version 3.30' banner (App D.1) -> COMMAND.COM prompt. GUI glyph/region work deferred to ADR-0004 (M3/M4).

title: Tracer boot: minimal MBR->stage2->32-bit protected/flat->VESA LFB, fill screen with the seafoam palette
description: The thinnest REAL boot path (shared with M1; M1 fleshes out modes/robustness/Bochs+86Box). Custom MBR loads stage2; known-good A20/GDT/PE/far-jump; set a VBE 2.0 LFB at 640x480; clear it to the seafoam background color from the palette sheet. PRD §5, §11 M1.
acceptance_criteria: QEMU shows a seafoam framebuffer; serial marks each boot stage; no triple-fault under -d cpu_reset.
notes: DONE (verified). Real boot chain os/boot/mbr.asm + stage2.asm: MBR(INT13h CHS)->stage2(VBE2 640x480 LFB @0xFD000000 24bpp, A20 via 0x92, flat GDT, CR0.PE, far jump)->32-bit fill LFB seafoam RGB(111,160,142)->hlt-loop live. build/tracer_boot.img (32K, reproducible). Gate test-tracer-boot: serial markers S1..OK + tools/ppm_seafoam_check (81 samples PASS) + no triple-fault. Independently confirmed center pixel=111,160,142. Break (seafoam RGB / null far-jump selector) -> RED, restored GREEN. Regression green. ASCII-clean. Bochs not needed.

title: Smoke test: harness boots a seed-built freestanding binary, asserts serial marker, screendumps, returns CI exit code
description: The heartbeat. Compile a trivial Pascal program with the seed cross-compiler (m0-2), boot it under the QEMU harness (m0-3), assert a known serial marker, capture a QMP screendump, and return a pass/fail exit code. Threads the compiler + boot + harness layers minimally. PRD §8, §11 M0.5.
acceptance_criteria: `make smoke` exits 0 on a good binary and non-zero when the serial marker is absent or a triple-fault occurs.
notes: DONE (verified). Real 'make smoke' gate: builds build/seed_smoke.elf, boots via build/qemu_harness, PASS iff serial marker 'InitechOS seed OK' found AND no triple-fault AND no hang. Verified: make smoke exit 0 (18 serial bytes, marker yes); triple_fault fixture w/ that marker -> exit 1; wrong-marker -> non-zero. Screendump best-effort/skipped per bead initech-xcg (seed guest clean-exits ~0.25s) -- serial+fault is the authoritative signal; visual screendump is f8v.4. Full regression green (test-seed/-codegen/-harness/smoke). ASCII-clean.

description: C-hosted single-pass Pascal front end skeleton: lexer/parser for a minimal subset; stack-machine x86 emit; produce a freestanding flat binary that prints over serial. The bootstrap seed (PRD §4, §7).
notes:
Step A DONE (verified by orchestrator): seed/ lexer+parser+AST (token/lexer/ast/parser/initechc .c), zero-dep test harness. make factory builds build/initechc; make test-seed REAL gate green (54+23 checks); independent mutation OP_ADD->OP_MUL -> red, restored -> green; .c/.h ASCII-clean. Grammar: program/var integer/begin-end/assign/write(ln)/int-expr with correct precedence. Remaining: Step B codegen (AST->freestanding x86, serial write), Step C verify booting in QEMU (now unblocked).
DONE (verified). Step B codegen: seed/codegen.{h,c} stack-machine AST-walk -> nasm Intel asm; seed/rt/ multiboot1 runtime + serial helpers + isa-debug-exit; initechc --emit-asm; pipeline -> build/seed_smoke.elf. ACCEPTANCE MET: build/seed_smoke.elf boots under build/qemu_harness and emits exactly 'InitechOS seed OK\n' (18 bytes). make test-seed-codegen REAL gate green w/ exact-output arith corpus (R=14,R=20,D=3 M=2,N=-5); independent imul->add mutation -> RED, restored -> GREEN. ASCII-clean. Subset growth + fpc-differential + UART-init/tri-emu deferred to filed beads.
RE-CONTEXT (post ADR-0003): this seed Pascal->x86 compiler is the GENESIS OF TURBO INITECH (ADR-0007), not the OS bootstrap. The C OS is built by the factory toolchain (host gcc interim, CDR-0001; i686-elf target, ADR-0002).

description: ADR-0013 Sec 8. Host-buildable Layer-5 dispatcher: FlairApp/FlairAppProcs (Sec 3.1), flair_app_dispatch (refCon demux + activateEvt deactivate/activate-pair synthesis in task context), FlairProcess_launch/_terminate, child sub-arena (flair_alloc block -> flair_heap_init child -> one-shot free). title: FLAIR live compositor ERASES the modal dialog + menu bars when a window is dragged across them (non-WindowMgr layers not damage-tracked)
description:
SYMPTOM (operator, make run-flair): corrupted redraws when windows are moved past each other.

ROOT CAUSE: the live drag repaint desktop_paint_damage (os/flair/desktop.c:171) composites ONLY the WindowMgr document windows + the desktop background. The live scene ALSO contains the modal FILE COPY dialog (os/flair/dialog.h:420, rect x[140,500] y[200,280], drawn ON TOP) and TWO stacked menu bars (os/flair/shell.h:99-101, y[0,40]) -- both painted ONCE by shell_render and NOT participants in the WindowMgr damage/z-order model. When a document window is dragged across them, the vacated area falls through to wm->desktop_update and is filled with seafoam (desktop.c:197-200), ERASING the modal/bar; and a dragged window's visible region is not clipped against them (window.c fronts_union considers only WindowMgr windows), so it can OVERPAINT them.

EMPIRICAL (flair_live.img; drag W1 left 260px across W0 + modal): the modal right-half x[366,498] y[206,278] went from 85.5% white + 14.5% black (the dBoxProc dialog box + text) BEFORE to 100% teal AFTER -- the modal erased to desktop background. Window-over-window compositing is CLEAN and the cursor save-under is CLEAN; the defect is specifically the non-WindowMgr layers.

WHY IT ESCAPED: test-flair-drag's pixel oracle (tools/ppm_flair_drag_check.c) deliberately probes "right of window 0" and "above the modal", AVOIDING the modal/bar region; the region gate (test-interact) grades math, not composited pixels over these layers.

FIX OPTIONS: integrate the always-on-top layers (menu bars + modal) into the live compositor so they survive a damage repaint. Interim: after desktop_paint_damage, re-blit the bar + modal layers over the damaged region. Clean: make the bars + modal topmost participants in the z-order (windows clip against them via fronts_union; they repaint in the damage pass). Same incompleteness family as initech-z1f5 (live menu panel restore).

Oracle-first: depends on the independent modal/bar survival oracle.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging any document window across the modal and the menu bars leaves them intact (no teal hole, no overpaint), verified by an independent pixel oracle the fix turns RED->GREEN; window-over-window compositing stays clean; reproducible build green.

title: FLAIR mouse: INDEPENDENT physical-direction oracle (PS/2 up -> cursor v DECREASES) + EVENT_MUTATE_FLIP_SIGN mutant
description:
The mouse-Y inversion (sibling bug) escaped every gate because all mouse-Y oracles grade the cursor by RECOMPUTING expected where.v from the SAME "cursor_v += dy" rule the artifact uses (harness/proptest/test_event.c manufactures dy=+5 and asserts v=240+5), and the only mouse mutant EVENT_MUTATE_STALE_WHERE drops the update (magnitude) but never tests SIGN. This is the HER-02 by-construction heresy on the sign axis (ADR-0010, Law 2).

Build an INDEPENDENT oracle that encodes the GROUND-TRUTH polarity from the hardware contract (a real PS/2 mouse-up packet: byte0 Y-sign bit + byte2 magnitude, +Y = physical up) and asserts that after the producer cooks it into the ring, the screen cursor v DECREASES (moves up). Mutation-prove with a new EVENT_MUTATE_FLIP_SIGN that inverts the producer sign and MUST drive this oracle RED. Optional EMU leg on the interactive image: inject a known physical-up motion, assert the cursor sprite rose.

This oracle is the RED half of the producer fix and must exist FIRST (Red->Green, CLAUDE.md Rule 1/6).
acceptance_criteria: A host oracle anchored to the PS/2 packet convention (NOT cook_raw's += rule) asserts physical-up -> screen v decreases; RED against the current inverted producer, GREEN after the negation fix; EVENT_MUTATE_FLIP_SIGN bites it.

title: FLAIR live cursor: vertical mouse axis INVERTED -- producer packs RAW PS/2 dy, violating the locked ring-payload Y contract
description:
SYMPTOM (operator, make run-flair): the mouse moves inverted in the VERTICAL axis -- physical-up moves the cursor/window DOWN; horizontal is correct.

ROOT CAUSE: a producer-side sign error that VIOLATES the locked ring-payload contract. spec/event_model.h:333-335 defines FLAIR_RAW_MOUSE payload bits 16..23 as "signed delta Y ... Y axis inverted from PS/2 convention to screen-down-positive" -- the PS/2->screen flip MUST happen BEFORE the value enters the ring. But the producer flair_live_mouse_post (os/milton/kmain.c:1024-1026) packs the RAW PS/2 dy (mouse.c:222 "+dy = up") with NO inversion. Downstream cook_raw (os/flair/event.c:441) correctly ADDS what the contract guarantees is an already-flipped screen-down delta (g_cursor_v += dy), so physical-up raises g_cursor_v and the cursor descends. cook_raw and test_event.c are CORRECT per spec; the bug is solely the producer. The misleading comment is mouse.c:225-227 ("the pump cooks the Y flip"), contradicting the locked spec.

EMPIRICAL (flair_live.img, QMP rel-mouse): rel-y +40 -> window top 120->80 (UP); rel-y -40 -> 120->160 (DOWN) => physical-up -> window-down. Reproduced.

WHY IT ESCAPED: the producer is __attribute__((unused)) on host (no host oracle) and runs only in the bare-metal live build; the EMU drag/mouse gate goldens BAKED IN the inverted behavior (Makefile:11199 "rel +y -> cursor up"; test-flair-mouse where.v calibrated to the inverted cook). No oracle establishes an INDEPENDENT physical-direction ground truth (HER-02 / ADR-0010 by-construction pattern, on the sign axis).

FIX: negate dy at the producer (kmain.c:1026) BEFORE narrowing into bits 16..23, in int width -- CRITICAL: -(int8)-128 = +128 is not int8-representable; negate in int, or saturate -128->+127. Update the stale ISR comment mouse.c:225-227. Leave cook_raw (event.c:441) and test_event.c UNCHANGED. Then RE-BASELINE the EMU gate goldens that encoded the inversion (test-flair-drag Makefile ~11193-11224 trace+expected window pos; test-flair-mouse Makefile:8396/8423 expected where.v) to the physical-correct direction -- do NOT revert the fix to keep them green.

Oracle-first: depends on the independent physical-direction oracle.
acceptance_criteria: make run-flair: physical-up moves the cursor/window UP. The independent direction oracle is RED before the producer fix and GREEN after. EMU drag/mouse gates re-baselined to physical-correct direction. test_event.c unchanged (correct per spec). Reproducible build green; ASCII-clean.

title: ADR-0004 amendment: lock in mechanism/policy(decoration) split + ATKINSON as the dual-heritage (QuickDraw Rgn + GDI HRGN) shared spine
description: RATIFIED OPERATOR DIRECTIVE 2026-06-21 (no relitigation; operator authority, NOT committee -- do not convene one). Lock in the two design choices stolen from the X Window System factoring. (1) MECHANISM/POLICY SPLIT: the imaging/window MECHANISM (surface/GrafPort draw verbs, ATKINSON region clip, Window/Event geometry: strucRgn/contRgn/updateRgn/drag/z-order/DiffRgn) is kept SEPARATE from POLICY/DECORATION (chrome look, control look, dialog look, COLORS, patterns, metrics). Mechanism is policy-free and takes color/pattern/metric as PARAMETERS; decoration is a layer on top. (2) ATKINSON = THE SHARED SPINE: os/flair/atkinson (scanline inversion-list set algebra) is the SAME machinery behind BOTH QuickDraw Rgn AND GDI HRGN; BOTH heritages (System-7 chrome + Win-3.1/Photoshop chrome -- both present in the Office Space frame) clip through the ONE engine; NEITHER heritage owns it. GAP ANALYSIS: ADR-0004 D-1 (5-layer stack) + D-2 (one surface module/GrafPort) already give the LAYERING; ADR-0005 already gives a clean-room, heritage-neutral region engine. But (a) ADR-0004 does NOT make mechanism-vs-decoration a first-class invariant -- the Managers (layer 3) currently fold geometry-mechanism AND appearance-policy together, and color is smeared across chrome.c/control.c/dialog.c (the 5 duplicated index->RGB switches -- the exact tangle that let the preview.webp palette leak in unnoticed, WL-0053); (b) ADR-0005 frames the engine as System-7/QuickDraw-only (Rect field order, UnionRgn/SectRgn names) and never acknowledges it as the GDI-HRGN spine too. ACTIONS: write an operator-ratified AMENDMENT to ADR-0004 (no committee) making both binding invariants; cross-ref a companion note on ADR-0005 (the engine is the dual-heritage Rgn+HRGN spine). The amendment's current-state/migration/forward-obligation sections must be grounded in the verified FLAIR structure from the running grading audit (Law 1). IMPLICATIONS for the verb track: re30.4 (one palette module, kill the 5 switches) is the FIRST mechanism/policy cut -- extract color into a POLICY module the mechanism reads as params, not a dedup'd lookup baked into decoration; re30.5 (GrafPort verbs) lands the imaging mechanism policy-free. Memory: bd memories 'ratified-operator-directive-2026-06-21'. Relates: initech-hfeg (palette canon), initech-re30.4 (palette module), initech-re30.5 (verbs).

title: FLAIR palette/chrome must derive from + grade against the system7-decomp/win31-decomp canon (NOT preview.webp samples)

The CANONICAL color schemes are in the painstakingly-built sister repos: ../system7-decomp (System 7: clut.h ROM CLUT [already canon], specs/chrome, specs/desktop, quickdraw color-quickdraw, the wctb window color table, research/raw/s7-chrome.md, golden screenshots/captures) and ../win31-decomp (Win 3.1 accents: specs/gdi/system-colors.md, specs/gdi/palette.md, specs/chrome/color-scheme.md, re/win31-color-measurements.md, refs default-colors, oracles/osfree-win16 syscolor). FLAIR is GRADED AGAINST these goldens/specs. System 7 + Win 3.1 accents are canonical NOW; System 8 / Platinum accretes and becomes canonical LATER (era-layered). ACTIONS: (1) re-derive FLAIR's chrome/desktop/menu/dialog/pinstripe/scrollbar colors from the decomp canon (System 7 indexed-8 colors ARE clut.h entries; Win accents from win31-decomp); (2) re-point the FLAIR palette module (re30.4) to the canon, NOT palette.h preview-samples; (3) re-calibrate the FLAIR oracles (ppm_flair_check, test-chrome/control/dialog, golden_resolves) to grade against the decomp canon; (4) keep the preview.webp samples only as the frame-mockup reference they are (NOT chrome canon). This SUPERSEDES the committee re30.4-palette-source ruling (which wrongly preserved the preview.webp seafoam on a now-invalid 'OD-4 ratified' premise). Re-run the re30.4 design from the canon.
notes: DEFINITIVE color canon (operator 2026-06-21): Initech teal #8DDCDC (VIC-20 cyan, screen/phosphor; #57B19F pigment for printed props only). RULE: System 7/8 golden vs decomp goldens, EXCEPT replace lavender tinge -> #8DDCDC AND desktop bg = #8DDCDC; Win31 for the additional Windows bits. Supersedes ADR-0004 OD-4 seafoam. See bd memory 'initech-color-canon'.

title: stage2: add 640x480x8 (VESA 0x101) LFB fallback + an oracle leg that REACHES the kmain 8bpp DAC present wrapper
description: COMMITTEE RULING (re30.3 chair, 2026-06-21): the FLAIR desktop's indexed-8 present path (kmain.c flair_desktop_present, bpp==8 branch: programs the VGA DAC for FLAIR CLUT indices 0..8 then index-copies row-by-row honoring lfb_pitch) is CORRECT, period-authentic, ratified-architecture code (ADR-0004 OD-2/AM-7: indexed-8 == the authentic early-90s VGA/SVGA backing-store depth, VBE 0x101) but is UNREACHED in-tree: os/boot/stage2.asm vbe_setup accepts ONLY 640x480x32/24, so the only 8bpp the boot yields is mode-0x13 320x200 which the kmain 640x480 size guard correctly rejects before present is called. FIX: (a) stage2 requests a 640x480x8 LFB (VESA mode 0x101) as a priority tier BELOW 32/24bpp and ABOVE the mode-0x13 floor; (b) add an emulator oracle leg that DRIVES a real 640x480x8 LFB so the kmain 8bpp DAC+row-copy WRAPPER is mutation-proven REACHED (NOT merely the separate host render.c:121 8bpp branch -- that is a different reimplementation; the live wrapper has ZERO coverage today, a real Rule-6 gap). Sequence before/with q0gy (86Box) so the period-authentic 8bpp desktop lands before the M4 app freeze / period-authenticity sign-off (DEC-04/FO-D). Display-mode strategy of record (committee A): dual-depth, request priority 32->24->8(0x101)->fail-loud floor; OD-2 offscreen stays indexed-8; AM-6 host-model-per-mode preserved.
notes: Committee ruling re30.3-chair. Cite: kmain.c flair_desktop_present 8bpp branch; os/boot/stage2.asm vbe_setup (640x480x32/24 only); ADR-0004 OD-2/AM-7/D-2/AM-6/D-DISSENT-1; PRD Sec 5 line 105 (640x480 x8/x32 both canonical). Soft input to initech-fgs1 (486+/period-SVGA): if 486+ SVGA is ratified and 640x480x8 becomes PRIMARY, revisit the tier ORDER.

title: stage2 640x480x8 VESA 0x101 LFB fallback + REACH the kmain 8bpp present path (period-authentic indexed-8 desktop)
description: RULING re30.3-chair (2026-06-21): stage2 vbe_setup (stage2.asm:388-394) accepts ONLY 32/24bpp at 640x480; the period-authentic 640x480x8 (VESA mode 0x101/0x103, ADR-0004 OD-2/AM-7) is NEVER requested, so the kmain.c:716-737 8bpp DAC-program+pitch-honoring-index-copy present path is UNREACHABLE in-tree (the only 8bpp the boot path yields is 320x200 mode-0x13, rejected by the kmain.c:769 size guard before present). SCOPE: (1) extend vbe_setup mode-walk to ALSO accept a 640x480x8 LFB mode (VESA 0x101) as an explicit priority tier BELOW 32/24bpp (truecolor preferred = QEMU dev-loop; 8bpp accepted = period SVGA / 86Box); record lfb_bpp=8 with the 640x480 geometry so the size guard passes and flair_desktop_present takes the 8bpp branch. (2) Add an emulator/host oracle leg that DRIVES a 640x480x8 LFB and MUTATION-PROVES the kmain 8bpp present is REACHED and correct -- NOT merely the host render.c:121/test_chrome.c:77 8bpp BRANCHES (those are a separate host reimplementation; the live-LFB DAC+row-copy WRAPPER at kmain.c:716-737 has zero coverage today; Rule 6 = a path no oracle reaches is decoration). (3) Until landed, kmain.c:716 carries a pointer comment naming this bead + 'reached when a 640x480x8 LFB exists (VESA 0x101 / 86Box)'. Priority order of record (RULING A): (1) 640x480x32 LFB, (2) 640x480x24 LFB, (3) 640x480x8 LFB == VESA 0x101 (period-authentic), (4) mode-0x13 320x200x8 = the fail-loud floor the kmain guard rejects. SEQUENCE: land this BEFORE or WITH initech-q0gy (86Box) so the authentic 8bpp desktop exists before apps freeze at M4. Refs: PRD Sec 5 line 105 (VBE LFB 640x480 x8/x32); ADR-0004 OD-2/AM-7/D-2/D-DISSENT-1; AM-6 (host-model-per-mode frozen); Makefile:10116 (the in-tree escalation marker this resolves).

title: FLAIR Phase 4 -- App Contract (ADR-0013): tenant/event/launch/yield/exit + multi-app co-residency
description: RATIFIED as ADR-0013 (2026-06-27, ADR-by-committee wf_42352963-a57 + adversarial verify; was mis-numbered 'ADR-0010', which is the unrelated Grading-and-Goldens ADR). The FLAIR App Contract: a tenant = a FlairApp record of C entry-points + a child arena, bound to its windows via WindowRecord.refCon; the shell inverts WaitNextEvent and demuxes by refCon through a HOST-BUILDABLE Layer-5 dispatcher (os/flair/process.{c,h}) called by both kmain and the test-process oracle (E-D2 single-spine). Delivers MULTI-APP CO-RESIDENCY (MultiFinder model) over the existing cooperative pump + WindowMgr z-order + FLAIR heap, within flat-32 + no-isolation. Native windowed apps via FlairProcess_launch; character-mode tenants (SAMIR/Turbo Initech) keep the unchanged single-program loader (suspend-desktop/save-under/rebuild-on-return). Native-vs-text fork RULED: Initech123/InitechWord are native Toolbox tenants. First cut (ADR-0013 Sec 8): process.c dispatcher + activateEvt synthesis + raise SHELL_MAX_WINDOWS + reference 'hello window' tenant + a 2nd co-resident tenant + migrate SAMIR as first text-tenant; oracles O-1..O-8. Phase 4.5 (initech-49ez) platform services build on this. See docs/adr/ADR-0013-FLAIR-App-Contract.md.

title: Route AH=09h/02h/06h CON output through the redirectable JFT handle 1 (hsct prereq)
description: WL-0045 BLOCKER 1: COMMAND.COM builtins emit most output via AH=09h (dos_print -> do_puts -> con_putc DIRECT), bypassing JFT handle 1, so DUP2(file,1) does NOT redirect them -- 'echo HELLO > file' would print to CON and leave the file empty. Real DOS routes AH=02h/09h/06h to STDOUT (handle 1), so they ARE redirectable. FIX: route the AH=09h/02h/06h CON-output path through the redirectable handle 1 (interacts with p96i's con_putc ANSI hook + g_sink). Prereq for hsct (I/O redirection) redo. Kernel-window room now exists (o0td delivered +32 KiB).
close_reason: Duplicate of initech-k36g (pre-existing, same AH=09h/02h/06h CON->handle-1 routing prereq for hsct). I missed it on create; consolidating on k36g.

title: Rule-8: raise PROGRAM_BASE 0x30000->0x38000 for kernel BSS headroom (ADR-by-committee ruling)
description: The shell kernel outgrew the 128 KiB window [0x10000,0x30000) -- _kernel_end=0x30fc0 overruns PROGRAM_BASE by ~4.3 KiB after the p96i ANSI wiring. ADR-by-committee (memory-architect/period-purist/north-star lenses) RULED: raise PROGRAM_BASE 0x30000->0x38000 (the sanctioned mechanism, precedent 5pe; a growing resident kernel pushing the TPA up is the authentic DOS model). LOCKED-SPEC change (Rule 8). BLAST RADIUS (committee-found): (1) spec/memory_map.h PROGRAM_BASE 0x30000->0x38000 (PROGRAM_IMAGE etc. auto-compute); (2) 15 .asm fixtures hard-code org 0x00030100 -> 0x00038100 (incl. os/samir/boot/samir_crt0.asm + samir.ld); (3) SECONDARY data-page addresses 0x31000/0x32000 in datetime/vect/absdisk programs must shift +0x8000 -> 0x39000/0x3A000; (4) test_hardware_spec.c hard-coded CHECK(PROGRAM_BASE==0x30000) + spec/hardware.json literal must update; (5) re-verify the full EXEC gate-set + emu + Bochs (Rule 5). Verify program region [0x38000,0x70000) stays ample (PROGRAM_IMAGE_MAX ~155KiB > SAMIR ~107KiB -- committee-verified).
notes: WL-0042 Wave 4 blocker. Committee ruling 2026-06-20. Companion follow-up: merge g_fat/g_load_fat via fileio_fat_get_cache (B, the headroom bead).
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by option B (FAT-cache share). The ADR-by-committee ratified raising PROGRAM_BASE 0x30000->0x38000, but on implementation+grading the raise BROKE SAMIR: it shrank the disjoint AH=48h heap arena ([PROGRAM_IMAGE+image+BSS_RESERVE, ENV_BLOCK)) from ~48 KiB to ~16 KiB -> SAMIR's REPL failed to start (test-samir-boot RED). The committee's own endorsed option B is strictly better: the loader now SHARES the int21 file backend's whole-FAT buffer (fileio_fat_fat_buffer) instead of its own 6 KiB g_load_fat copy -> frees 6 KiB kernel .bss so the kernel fits at the ORIGINAL 0x30000 (_kernel_end 0x30fc0->0x2f7a0) WITH p96i, preserving SAMIR's full arena. The PROGRAM_BASE raise is no longer needed (kept available as a future option if the kernel outgrows even with B). Implemented in loader.c/loader.h/kmain.c + fileio_fat_fat_buffer reuse. Caught by Law-4 orchestrator grading (verify the report; the committee analysis missed the heap-arena consequence).

title: Kernel-window headroom: raise PROGRAM_BASE or share the two 6 KiB FAT buffers (g_fat/g_load_fat)
description: The shell kernel .bss is repeatedly bumping the program-load window guard (_kernel_end vs PROGRAM_BASE=0x30000, limit 0x2ff00) as the kernel grows. Across this session each wave (Wave 1 env/mz/batch, Wave 2 mvg crit_blockdev, Wave 3 device-chain) pushed it; mitigated tactically by trimming BATCH_FILE_MAX 4096->2048 (a .BAT size cut). The PRINCIPLED fix: either (a) raise PROGRAM_BASE (e.g. 0x30000->0x34000) -- a LOCKED-SPEC change (spec/memory_map.h, Rule 8) with wide blast radius (every .COM org 0x30100, the MCB arena window [0x30000,0x70000), PROGRAM_IMAGE, the absdisk seam -- needs an ADR amendment + every .asm fixture's org updated), OR (b) share ONE whole-FAT buffer between fat12.c (g_fat, 6144) and loader.c (g_load_fat, 6144) -- saves 6 KiB, but couples the loader's load_program_from_fat FAT cache to fat12's. Restore BATCH_FILE_MAX to 4096 once headroom exists.
notes: RATIFIED (committee 2026-06-20, gridlock=false) + ORCHESTRATOR AMENDMENT: PATH 2 -- whole-map shift UP by delta=0x8000. The chair OVERRULED a 3-lens PATH-1 majority on measured arithmetic: PATH-1A (reserve 0xC000) left only 736 B between arena_base and SAMIR .bss end (re-creates y206); PATH-1B (reserve 0x10000, arena shrinks to 31.2 KiB) PANICS SAMIR -- pal_milton.c PAL_MILTON_HEAP_PARAS=2048 requests EXACTLY 0x8000=32768 B via AH=48h with milton_panic on CF=1, and 31968<32768. PATH 2 keeps SAMIR's arena byte-identical (47.2 KiB), preserves disjointness, gains +32 KiB kernel window by reclaiming half the dead [0x90000,0xA0000) gap. FINAL CONSTANTS (spec/memory_map.h): PROGRAM_BASE 0x30000->0x38000, PROGRAM_IMAGE 0x30100->0x38100, ENV_BLOCK 0x5F000->0x67000, PROGRAM_STACK_BOT 0x60000->0x68000, PROGRAM_STACK_TOP 0x6FFFC->0x77FFC, PROGRAM_ALLOC_END 0x70000->0x78000, LOAD_STAGING_BASE 0x70000->0x78000; PROGRAM_BSS_RESERVE HELD at 0x10000 (covers measured 47.3 KiB .bss, 16.7 KiB slack). ORCHESTRATOR AMENDMENT (Law 4 -- the chair's 'kstart ESP unchanged' was a FLAW): staging at [0x78000,0x88000) would overlap the kernel stack [0x80000,0x90000); FIX = move kstart.asm ESP 0x8FFFC->0x97FFC so the kernel stack moves to [0x88000,0x98000), disjoint from staging, 32 KiB gap to VGA. boot_info@0x500 + FONT_STASH@0x1000 are low-mem (untouched); stage2 anchor 0x90000 dead after handoff. Plus 13 .asm orgs->0x38100 + ALL program scratch/result equs +0x8000 (vect BOGUS_24/R_CRITAL, datetime full R_* block, absdisk PATBUF/RDBUF/R_AX -- caught by self-audit), samir.ld+crt0, BATCH_FILE_MAX 2048->4096. GRADED GREEN: kernel-end guard re-bound to 0x38000 (_kernel_end=0x300e0 OK); SAMIR relinked @0x38100 .bss still 0xBD20 __bss_end 0x57040; test-loader 29/0, test-arena-disjoint 1321/0, test-samir-boot PASS (the y206 oracle). Full make test + Bochs in flight.
close_reason: DELIVERED (PATH 2, committee-ratified + orchestrator kstart amendment). PROGRAM_BASE 0x30000->0x38000 whole-map shift +0x8000; kernel window [0x10000,0x38000)=160 KiB (+32 KiB). PROGRAM_BSS_RESERVE held 0x10000 (the prescribed 32 KiB cut was disproven: SAMIR .bss measured 47.3 KiB not 26 KiB). SAMIR arena preserved 47.2 KiB (test-samir-boot PASS). Graded GREEN: 240 host + 39 emu + Bochs (triple_fault=0). Commit bae46d4, WL-0046. Caught 2 blast-radius misses the suite surfaced (test_exec.c latent 0x5F000; test_hardware_spec.c + hardware.json factory regression guards). Restored BATCH_FILE_MAX 4096.

The FILE COPY modal IS visible/legible (its 7px black dBoxProc border makes 'Saving tables to disk...' + progress bar stand out) -- but it reads gray-on-gray-ish rather than the frame's white box. title: Extend harness/emu/bochs.c with RFB FramebufferUpdate pixel capture (~50 LOC)

title: InitechDOS loader: FAT-load .COM direct into PROGRAM_IMAGE (lift 64KiB LOAD_STAGING_MAX cap to PROGRAM_IMAGE_MAX)
description: load_program_from_fat reads the .COM into LOAD_STAGING [0x70000,0x80000)=64KiB then copies to PROGRAM_IMAGE; it rejects file_size>LOAD_STAGING_MAX. SAMIR.COM is 77KiB (>64KiB) so EXEC SAMIR.COM returns LOADER_ERR_TOO_BIG. Staging cannot grow (kernel stack at 0x80000). Fix: read the FAT file DIRECTLY into PROGRAM_IMAGE (0x30100) and build the PSP in place -- no staging copy -- so the cap becomes PROGRAM_IMAGE_MAX (188KiB). Also fixes the stale loader comment claiming 'staging cap >= PROGRAM_IMAGE_MAX'. New InitechDOS work (operator-authorized); blocks the S8.2 emu gate. Benefits all future large apps (TPS, the Pascal compiler).
acceptance_criteria: EXEC of a >64KiB .COM from FAT loads + runs under QEMU; existing toy-program EXEC gates (test-exec/test-shell/test-fs) stay green; mutation-proven; PROGRAM_IMAGE_MAX remains the only size bound.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix .dbt arena-mark: dbt_close reset-to-base freed live interp ctx (memo tables)
description: dbt_open/dbt_create stored arena_mark=NULL so dbt_close reset the PAL arena to BASE, freeing the caller's prior allocations -- incl. a live xb_interp/ctx when a memo-bearing table is closed (surfaced by 7az.5: hook chain broke after close+reopen). title: In-kernel FAT16 mount: windowed/streaming FAT-sector read (g_fat[12*512] too small for a FAT16 FAT)
description: z01 landed the FAT16 DECODE layer (fat16_next_cluster + fat_type dispatch) + a host-side 3-way differential oracle (test-fat16) proving the decode over real FAT16 fixtures (incl. a 2.5MB volume whose chain crosses cluster 0xFF8). BUT the kernel cannot yet mount a real FAT16 volume: the whole-FAT in-memory buffer g_fat[12*512] (~6KB, sized for a 1.44MB FAT12 floppy) is far smaller than a FAT16 FAT (up to ~128KB). A real FAT16 mount currently fails loud (no corruption). To make FAT16 actually readable IN-KERNEL/IN-EMULATOR, replace the whole-FAT load with a windowed/streaming FAT-sector read (read only the FAT sector(s) needed for the current cluster's entry, like the streaming chain walk dao added for the data region). This is the remaining work for true FAT16 HDD read; it should be a dependency of the 40oq capstone (FAT16-green criterion).
close_reason: Landed: windowed/streaming FAT-sector read so a real FAT16 volume mounts in-kernel (mode-aware bind: FAT12 keeps whole-FAT g_fat for the WRITE path, FAT16 installs the 2-sector windowed reader READ-only). FAT12 12-bit straddle byte-identical; cache amortizes consecutive clusters. test-d27i + 3 mutants (off-by-one sector / no-straddle / no-window-dispatch) mutation-proven. I caught+fixed an orphaned-gate-wiring gap (test-d27i was not in TEST_UNIT_GATES). Commit 5a6361a; full gate 99 host + 27 emu GREEN. In-EMULATOR FAT16 mount leg filed as a 40oq-blocking followup.

title: Lock FLAIR heap = extended-memory region (DEC-03) in spec/memory_map.h + boot_info ext_mem_kb + stage2 INT15h probe + bump/free allocator
description: DEC-03 (committee-ratified, wf_573c1cf5-537): Lock the FLAIR Toolbox heap as a DEDICATED extended-memory region -- NOT the per-program MCB arena (rebound on every EXEC -> would destroy GUI state). FLAIR_HEAP_BASE=0x00100000, FLAIR_HEAP_SIZE=0x00400000 (4 MiB), window [0x100000,0x500000), FLAIR_HEAP_MIN=0x00400000. FIXED + spec-locked window (Rule 11 determinism). stage2 PROBES installed RAM via INT 15h E820/E801/88h into a NEW boot_info_t.ext_mem_kb field; kernel PANICS loud (Rule 2, PC LOAD LETTER) if probed RAM < FLAIR_HEAP_MIN -- probe gates boot, never alters the map. No LFB collision (lfb_addr is VBE PhysBasePtr aperture or 0xA0000 fallback). ONE Rule 8 act touches exactly: boot_info.h (+ext_mem_kb), spec/memory_map.h (+FLAIR_HEAP_BASE/SIZE/MIN + commentary), spec/hardware.json (flair_heap block). NO existing memory_map constant changes. THEN the allocator: one flat FLAIR-owned arena, bump-pointer + typed free-list per class (region storage / indexed-8 offscreens / Manager handles / NFNT strikes), no per-row malloc, fail-loud on exhaustion (PRD Sec 5). FO-G: a test/mutant must confirm the panic-below-min path fires.
notes: SPEC-LOCK + PROBE DELIVERABLE DONE (committed 033e2fd): boot_info.ext_mem_kb + stage2 INT15h E820/E801/88h probe; spec/memory_map.h FLAIR_HEAP_BASE/SIZE/MIN (4 MiB @0x100000, fixed window); spec/hardware.json flair_heap block; kmain.c panic-below-min; FO-G oracle test_flair_heap_ram + mutant. REMAINING: the bump+typed-free-list ALLOCATOR .c (os/flair/heap.{c,h}) over [0x100000,0x500000) -- next wave (Wave 2), needed by the render skeleton + offscreen bitmaps + Manager records.

title: CAPSTONE: Kernel feature-complete certificate (Appendix-A coverage oracle)
description: Defines and proves 'kernel 100% feature complete' for the DOS-3.3 controlled scope. A coverage oracle walks ah_is_listed()/spec/int21h_register.json and asserts EVERY listed Appendix-A AH reaches a real handler -- ZERO functions remain in the not-yet-impl arm (int21.c:2022-2049). Plus: subdirs (ti8), FAT16 (z01), MCB wired (509.6), absolute disk (k-absdisk), partitions+multivol, INT 24h wired (mvg), break (4tw), wildcard (80k) all green. FCB 0F-24h (509.9) is the one Legacy class deprioritized vs the north-star: it must be either dispatched OR formally waived by the operator for a true 100% -- this cert surfaces that go/no-go.
notes: CAPSTONE ARTIFACT DELIVERED + GREEN (WL-0044, commit 4e60bd5): test_40oq.c certifies the Appendix-A INT 21h FUNCTIONAL surface is feature-complete -- 55 dispatched Core+Resident AHs, ZERO unwaived not-yet-impl gaps (the AH=03/04/05 AUX/PRN gap was closed this wave), FCB(0x0F-0x24) operator-WAIVED. 265 checks + mutant; make test-40oq green; 240 host + emu green. The bead stays OPEN (NOT --force-closed) because its literal-total deps kzfs (MBR partition) + slvd (multi-volume) are OPERATOR-DEFERRED (2026-06-15, 'no in-tranche consumer; revisit when FAT16-HDD/multi-volume land') -- the cert SURFACES this go/no-go honestly rather than hiding it. To reach literal-100%: lift the kzfs/slvd deferral (+ optionally dispatch FCB 509.9), then extend the cert. The DOS-3.3 personality is functionally complete for the north-star (Turbo Initech + InitechBase needs all green).

The blockdev/ATA layer (ata.c LBA28 read+write) already exists -- this is the INT 25h/26h front door over it, with the DOS stack-leaves-flags convention. Out of the literal ADR-0003 Appendix A INT 21h scope but a kernel sector service the util layer needs; confirm scope note in the cert.
notes: Operator 2026-06-15: AMEND ADR-0003 NOW (new DEC adding INT 25h/26h vectors; DEC-04a/DEC-10 ceremony). ADR-by-committee deliberation launched. Implement 4mq7 AFTER the amendment ratifies. Lock: drive numbering (AL=0=A:), I/O + invalid-drive error-code set, the INT 25h/26h stack-flags quirk, and where the 0x25/0x26 contract is recorded (int21h_calling_convention.json is INT-21h-scoped -> sibling stanza/new spec file).
close_reason: INT 25h/26h absolute disk R/W per ADR-0003 DEC-15: 0x8F trap-gate vectors + isr stubs (byte-identical to emu-proven int24 template) + int21 dispatch/reentrancy guard + kmain-bound blockdev seam (mounted-only, fail-loud unbound) + documented-and-omitted FLAGS-stack wart; AL=0=A:, separate AL/AH hardware-error space, bounds-checked-before-IO. test-absdisk + test-spec[6/6] + 8 DEC-15 mutants RED. make clean && make test GREEN 85+26 (geometry unchanged). Adversarial CLEAN. 2 P3 nits filed (952e019).

title: MBR partition-table parse + hidden_sectors LBA offset
description: Today the disk is mounted as an unpartitioned superfloppy: mount reads the BPB at LBA0 and hidden_sectors is parsed into bpb_t (dos_structs.h:210) but NEVER added to any LBA. A real partitioned HDD would mount at the wrong offset. Parse the MBR partition table, locate the active/first FAT partition, and add its start LBA (and hidden_sectors) to every disk access. Prereq for FAT16 HDD, multi-volume, and FDISK/FORMAT.
notes: DEFERRED by operator 2026-06-15: blocked on initech-t1on (DEC-07 amendment). One parallel-safe bead (disjoint fat12.c mount/device seam) but broadens the on-disk contract with no in-tranche consumer; revisit when FAT16-HDD/multi-volume land.

Add AL=00 get-attributes, AL=01 set-attributes against the FAT dir entry. North-star: InitechBase + a real ATTRIB util need this.
close_reason: AH=43h CHMOD get/set file attributes: DOS-faithful SET reject set (operator-approved, dir/vol-label target + Directory/VolLabel CX bits -> 0x0005; deviation recorded in initech-5o6o), attribute-byte-only RMW (mtime/mdate preserved, Rule 11), all 8 vtable literals updated, 3-way FAT differential (fat12_get_attr/python/mtools) + 7 mutants RED. Adversarial review found + FIXED a GET-on-directory fidelity bug (c7bffac: GET now returns CF=0/CX=0x10 per RBIL 4300h). make clean && make test GREEN 80+26 (76c4c8f + c7bffac).

title: INT 21h AH=56h RENAME: directory-entry rename
description: Listed (int21.c:284) but undispatched. No dir-entry rename path. EDX=old ASCIIZ, EDI/flat=new ASCIIZ. Root-dir rename first; cross-directory move once ti8 lands. North-star: InitechBase rewrites .dbf via rename; the shell REN built-in (s-ren) sits on this.
close_reason: AH=56h RENAME same-dir dir-entry rename: EDX/EDI two-pointer contract, same-dir-only (cross-dir->0x0011, move deferred to ycb3), dir/vol-label-source + dest-exists -> 0x0005, name-field-only RMW (chain/size/dates/FAT untouched, Rule 11), all 9 vtable slot-15 literals updated, mren differential + 6 mutants RED. Adversarial review CLEAN (self/case-only rename verified DOS-faithful vs mtools mren). make clean && make test GREEN 83+26 (0869ca0). P3 coverage follow-up: initech-isil.

title: INT 21h AH=5Bh CREATNEW: create, fail if file exists
description: Listed (int21.c:287) but undispatched. CREAT(3Ch) truncates an existing file; CREATNEW must instead fail with error 50h (file exists). Small delta over do_creat (int21.c:859) -- add the exists-check before allocating the SFT slot.
close_reason: AH=5Bh CREATNEW: implemented + oracle-green (test-fileio) + 4 mutants RED; merged (5a8f78f); adversarial-verified, no blocker; coverage follow-ups initech-nmpo/glsw filed.

title: INT 21h AH=57h FILETIME: get/set file date/time by handle
description: Listed (int21.c:285) but undispatched. AL=00 get / AL=01 set the packed DOS date/time of an open handle's dir entry. Pairs with the live RTC (AH=2Ah-2Dh already wired) for stamping on write. North-star: InitechBase + faithful DIR timestamps.
close_reason: AH=57h FILETIME GET/SET by handle: verbatim-pack contract, immediate-flush set_time vtable seam (all 6 initializers updated), inverted FAT differential (--stat-path-time) + 6 mutants RED; make clean && make test GREEN 78+26 AND make test-boot-bochs PASS (KERNEL_SECTORS 128->144 geometry obligation met, Rule 5); adversarial-verified clean (b0c4e2d). P3 stale comment fixed.

title: INT 21h AH=44h IOCTL: AL=00 get-device-info + AL=01/06/07/08 minors
description: Listed (int21.c:276) but ZERO AL-subfunction handling -- the whole IOCTL family falls to not-yet-impl. Add at least AL=00 (get device info: is-handle-a-device, CON/NUL flags), AL=01 (set), AL=06/07 (input/output status), AL=08 (removable-media check). Chains to the 509.7 device model for the device-info bits.
close_reason: AH=44h IOCTL AL=00 get-device-info: done+green (76+26)+adversarial-verified behavior-correct (03e67ae). Removed the over-conservative dep on 509.7 (resident device-driver model): the AL=00 landing uses the EXISTING SFT CON kind (handles 0/1/2), NOT 509.7 device headers -- consistent with epic bsy build order (group H IOCTL precedes group I 509.7). Minors + bit-name label fix -> initech-4nbn.

title: INT 21h AH=39h/3Ah/3Bh MKDIR/RMDIR/CHDIR: dispatch + handlers
description: Recognized-not-dispatched today: ah_is_listed() lists 39/3A/3B (int21.c:267) but the dispatch switch (int21_dispatch_body, int21.c:1898-2018) has no case, so they fall to not-yet-impl CF=1/AX=0001. Add real handlers that create/remove/change directories on the FAT volume. Blocked on ti8 (the FS has no subdirectory model yet -- root-dir 8.3 only). Pairs with the dispatched-but-stub GETCWD (47h) which currently always returns root (int21.c:2007, do_getcwd 1677).
notes:
GROUNDED PLAN (wf_426c388f, this session; bead's stale line nums corrected: ah_is_listed@384, dispatch switch@2303-2436, do_getcwd@2080/case0x47@2426). TWO LANDINGS, same bead, serial on current branch:
LANDING 1 = CHDIR(3Bh) ALONE (zero fat12-write dep; de-risks the shared resolve-base). Needs: NEW backend member resolve_dir (FULL-path-to-directory; existing resolve_dir_path@938 returns the leaf's PARENT, NOT the target -> using it for CD is a silent bug) wrapping fat12_resolve_path (validate final entry DIR_ATTR_DIRECTORY else 0x0003, return its start_cluster 0=>root, emit canonical root-relative text); FIX fat_resolve's (void)cwd_start@fileio_fat.c:422 so RELATIVE multi-component resolve seeds the descend cursor from cwd_start (mutant m5 = revert this); do_chdir near 2080 (write g_cwd_path/g_cwd_start_cluster@int21.c:65-66, ENFORCE INT21_CWD_MAX on WRITE -> overlength fail-loud 0x0003; CD .. at root stays root); case 0x3B; 3 host mocks.
LANDING 2 = MKDIR(39h)+RMDIR(3Ah) for ROOT-PARENT ONLY (non-root parent -> 0x0003, deferred to zs24). NEW public fat12_mkdir/fat12_rmdir wrapping existing static find_free/free_chain/scan_root/write_dirent + public set_entry/flush_fats + a dotdir-cluster writer. EMPIRICAL '.'/'..' (mtools 4.0.43, triple-confirmed): '.'={0x2E,0x20*10} attr0x10 start=OWN cluster; '..'={0x2E,0x2E,0x20*9} attr0x10 start=PARENT (ROOT=0, NOT self/1); slot[2]=0x00 end; FAT entry=0xFFF EOC; FIXED_MTIME/MDATE (Rule 11). mkdir/rmdir vtable members + fat_mkdir/fat_rmdir (dir_start!=0 -> 0x0003) + do_mkdir/do_rmdir + cases 0x39/0x3A.
PINNED DOS CODES: CHDIR missing/non-dir 0x0003; MKDIR-exists 0x0005; RMDIR-non-empty 0x0005; RMDIR-of-CWD 0x0010 (NEW #define INT21_ERR_CURRENT_DIR 0x0010u + do_geterr 0x10 arm); RMDIR-of-root reject; GETCWD already conformant.
ORACLE RED-first: host units (do_chdir/mkdir/rmdir via mock) + DIFFERENTIAL test_fat12_mkdir vs mmd golden (normalize mtime). Mutants: m1 chdir skips DIR_ATTR; m2 '..'=self (mmd diff bites = THE '..'-rule mutant); m3 forget EOC; m4 rmdir skips empty-check; m5 revert cwd_start fix. Nested-dir creation (MD \SUB\NEW) = zs24.
close_reason: DONE + merged (67c143c CHDIR + 91062055 MKDIR/RMDIR). AH=3Bh CHDIR (new resolve_dir backend member; fixed fat_resolve's ignored cwd_start so relative resolution honors CWD), AH=39h MKDIR + AH=3Ah RMDIR for ROOT-parent dirs (fat12_mkdir/rmdir + dotdir writer; '..' start=parent/0=root, empirically pinned + mmd-differential-gated). DOS codes: MKDIR-exists/RMDIR-non-empty 0x0005, RMDIR-of-CWD 0x0010 (new INT21_ERR_CURRENT_DIR). 5 mutants mutation-proven. make test 68 host + 22 emu GREEN + Bochs boot PASS (KERNEL_SECTORS 112->128, geometry-guarded). Nested-dir creation (MD \SUB\NEW) deferred to zs24 (fail-loud 0x0003).

title: [epic] DOS 3.3 feature-parity push (MILTON): close the Appendix-A INT 21h + shell gap
description: Drive InitechDOS (MILTON layer) to DOS 3.3 feature parity within ADR-0003 Appendix A scope. Grounded gap-map (this session): the INT 21h dispatch switch (int21.c:1862) implements 39 functions; MKDIR/RMDIR/CHDIR(39/3A/3B), CHMOD(43), IOCTL(44), ALLOC/FREE/SETBLOCK(48/49/4A), RENAME(56), FILETIME(57), CREATNEW(5B), KEEP(31), and FCB ops(0F-24) are RECOGNIZED by ah_is_listed() but NOT dispatched (fall through to not-yet-impl CF=1/AX=1). The shell has 7 built-ins (DIR/TYPE/CD/CLS/VER/ECHO/EXIT); COPY/DEL/REN/MD/RD/SET/DATE/TIME/PROMPT/PATH + batch + AUTOEXEC + a real env store are missing. SCOPE = MILTON only (kernel/INT21/FS/shell/loader/mem/devices); EXCLUDES FLAIR Toolbox/GUI (M3/M4). GOVERNING: ADR-0003 Appendix A is the controlled INT21 scope (no new funcs without amendment); DEC-08 defers MZ .EXE (dtw); FCB(509.9) + INT2Fh net redirector(part of 509.7) deprioritized per north-star (flag to user before closing). North-star filter: Turbo Initech (heap via 48h, EXEC+args+env, subdirs, handle I/O) and InitechBase (random-access lseek, RENAME, date/time, FINDFIRST) and Office-Space shell feel.
design: SEQUENCED BUILD ORDER (conflict-group serialized, oracle-gated, each RED->GREEN->mutation-proven->make test): A) dww (FINDFIRST DTA real-DOS offsets) -> 62m (kbd emits CR) -> 456 (cmd-tail args). B) 509.6 (MCB arena 48/49/4A in new mcb.c). C) dao (streaming cluster walk) -> z01 (FAT16). D) ti8 (subdir traversal+real GETCWD) -> NEW MKDIR/RMDIR/CHDIR 39/3A/3B + MD/RD/CD. E) NEW env-store + per-proc copy + EXEC inherit -> NEW SET. F) 80k (wildcard glob + DIR honors arg) -> NEW COPY -> NEW DEL/ERASE -> NEW DATE/TIME. G) xw1 (batch .BAT) -> NEW PROMPT -> atf (PATH+COMSPEC) -> 509.10 (mint Appendix-D boot vol + AUTOEXEC runs). H) NEW RENAME 56h+REN -> NEW IOCTL 44h AL=00 -> NEW CON handle-read 3Fh h0 -> NEW CHMOD43/FILETIME57/CREATNEW5B. I) bcg.12 (ATA BSY/DRDY+error codes) -> mvg (INT24h wiring) -> 509.7 (NUL/CON open-by-name; net redirector OUT) -> bcg.13 (msg-catalogue source-scan oracle). DEFER/FLAG: dtw(DEC-08), 509.9(FCB - ADR mandates, flag user), kod(authenticity-last), we2(ADR-0007 tripwire).
acceptance_criteria: Every Appendix-A Core/Resident INT 21h function dispatched with a real handler + oracle; shell has the DOS-feel built-ins (COPY/DEL/REN/MD/RD/SET/DATE/TIME/PROMPT/PATH) + batch + AUTOEXEC; MCB heap, subdirs, FAT16, env store all green; make test stays green throughout; each feature mutation-proven. FCB(509.9) decision surfaced to user.

title: FAT16 volume mounts OK but all decode/encode is 12-bit-only: silent garbage on FAT16 mount
description: fat12_mount returns FAT12_OK for a volume it classifies FAT_TYPE_FAT16 (fat12.c:132), but next_cluster/set_entry/walk_chain and the EOC/bad predicates are hard-wired 12-bit + FAT12 thresholds (header even warns FAT16 OUT OF SCOPE). fat_type is set but never checked by any op. A FAT16 mount + read mis-decodes every entry (16-bit treated as straddled 12-bit) -> garbage chains / corrupt data, no fail-loud. Audit: fat12 fat12-fat16-decode-mismatch.
acceptance_criteria: Either reject FAT16 at mount (FAT12_ERR_UNSUPPORTED when total_clusters>=FAT12_MAX_CLUSTERS) so 12-bit code never runs on 16-bit FAT, or assert vol->fat_type==FAT_TYPE_FAT12 at every decode/encode entry and fail loud; test mounts a FAT16 image and asserts the loud rejection (mutation-proven); make test green.
close_reason: fat12_mount rejects FAT16 (FAT12_ERR_UNSUPPORTED) since all decode/encode is 12-bit-only. RED->GREEN (synthetic FAT16 boot sector via in-test memory blockdev, total_sectors bumped past 4085), mutation-proven. make test 55h+19e GREEN.

title: FAT12 read path has no upper-cluster-bound: out-of-range cluster maps to in-image LBA, silent wrong data
description: fat12_next_cluster/walk_chain/read_file/read_partial only bounds-check the FAT BUFFER (off+1 < fat_len), never the cluster against the last valid data cluster. fat12_last_cluster() exists (fat12.c:973) but is write-path-only. An in-FAT link value above last-valid with (N*3/2+1)<fat_len is accepted and fed to BPB_CLUSTER_LBA. On 1.44MB geometry it happens to land >=2880 and is caught only by the file-backed blockdev EOF short-read -- a coincidence, not an invariant. With sectors_per_cluster>1 or FAT slack it maps to a VALID in-image LBA and silently returns the WRONG sector. Fuzzer (fat12_corrupt_fuzz.c:340-356) documents reliance on that coincidence. Audit: fat12 fat12-no-upper-cluster-bound + fat12-no-eoc-min-data-value-overlap-test.
acceptance_criteria: Explicit predicate cluster in [FAT12_FIRST_DATA_CLUSTER .. fat12_last_cluster(vol)] applied at every data-cluster consumer; returns FAT12_ERR_CLUSTER past last data cluster; fuzz/diff leg on a multi-sectors-per-cluster image pokes an in-FAT out-of-range link mapping to an in-image LBA and asserts fail-loud (RED today); make test green.
close_reason: Added fat12_cluster_in_range(vol,c) upper-bound predicate; hardened all 5 chain-walk sites (walk_chain + read_partial x2 + write_partial x3) so an out-of-range in-chain cluster is rejected (FAT12_ERR_CLUSTER) instead of mapped to a wrong/out-of-volume LBA. RED->GREEN (synthetic out-of-range link), write path hardened too (Rule 3). make test 55h+19e GREEN.

title: INT 21h AH=59h GET EXTENDED ERROR returns stale data after most failures
description: do_geterr (AH=59h) reports g_last_error, but int21_note_error() is called from only TWO sites (do_open :805, do_getcwd). Every other failing handler (do_write/read/close/lseek/dup/dup2/creat/unlink/exec/findfirst/findnext) sets CF+AX WITHOUT int21_note_error, so 59h returns the previous/zero error after the common 'call failed -> query 59h' idiom. Spec: DOS 3.3 PRM AH=59h keeps the most-recent INT 21h error. Test gap: test_int21_edge.c:542-579 hand-seeds the error via int21_note_error directly, never via a real failing dispatch -- which is why this is invisible. Audit: int21 geterr-not-populated-on-most-failures + geterr-test-gap.
acceptance_criteria: Centralize a set_error(f,code) helper (set_ax+cf_set+int21_note_error) used at every cf_set error return; dispatcher-driven oracle: fail OPEN/READ/WRITE through int21_dispatch then AH=59h asserts the just-returned code; mutation-proven; make test green.
close_reason: Single dispatch-choke capture in int21_dispatch: on CF=1, int21_note_error(AX). Covers every current+future failing handler; do_geterr (CF=0) never clobbers; success leaves last error intact. RED->GREEN, mutation-proven. make test 55 host + 19 emu GREEN.

title: INT 21h AH=40h WRITE rejects read/write-mode (AL=2) handles
description: do_write (os/milton/int21.c:653) gates FILE writes on e->open_mode != SFT_MODE_WRITE (==1). A handle opened AH=3Dh AL=2 (SFT_MODE_RDWR, sft.h:75) therefore returns ACCESS_DENIED on every WRITE, though read/write mode must permit writing. Breaks any program that opens a file mode-2 to update in place -- notably InitechBase .dbf record updates (the named consumer). do_read does NOT gate on mode (only kind), so read side is fine. Test gap: test_fileio.c only ever uses AL=0/AL=1 (never AL=2), so the bug is invisible to the oracle. VERIFIED against code 2026-06-13. Audit: fileio-sft rdwr-open-write-denied + rdwr-write-test-gap.
acceptance_criteria: do_write permits FILE write when open_mode==SFT_MODE_WRITE||SFT_MODE_RDWR; new test_fileio case: OPEN AL=2, WRITE at off 0, LSEEK 0, READ back == written; mutation-proven (revert gate -> RED); full make test green.
close_reason: do_write permits FILE write for SFT_MODE_WRITE||SFT_MODE_RDWR (int21.c). RED->GREEN, mutation-proven both directions (missing-RDWR + loosened-gate negative). make test: 55 host + 19 emu GREEN.

title: harness/emu/bochs.c driver + wire test-boot to dual-emulator differential
description: Build a C Bochs driver (generated bochsrc, headless display, serial capture, structured result) parallel to qemu.c; wire test-boot to assert QEMU==Bochs. Front D. Relates to initech-x0i / initech-bea.
notes: DE-RISKED + ready to build (6pj done). Working Bochs config (bd memory bochs-boot-solved-initech-6pj): romimage=/usr/share/bochs/BIOS-bochs-legacy + vgaromimage=/usr/share/bochs/VGABIOS-lgpl-latest + cpu:model=pentium + vga:extension=vbe + ata0-master cyl=2,heads=2,spt=32 + com1 mode=file. This Bochs 2.7 is a DEBUGGER build: drive headless with -rc containing 'c'. The RFB display plugin blocks ~30s for a client; the trivial RFB 3.3 unblock handshake (connect:5900, recv 12-byte version, send 'RFB 003.003\n', recv 4-byte sectype, send shared-flag 0x01, hold) must be implemented IN C in bochs.c (Law 3 -- harness is C-only; harness/emu/rfb_unblock.py is the reference, NOT shippable). bochs-term does NOT work headless (stalls on the graphics mode-set). GATE ASSERTS ON SERIAL, NOT a screendump: Bochs RFB cannot display VGA mode 0x13 (see bd memory bochs-rfb-display-does-not-render-vga-mode -- even a pristine real-mode fill shows black; OS render is correct, RFB display is the limitation). Expected Bochs serial: S1 JMP2 S2 VBE-ENOMODE VGA13 VBE FONT KLOAD A20 GDT PM LFB OK KERNEL BI-OK ... CONSOLE BANNER-BEGIN/InitechDOS Version 3.30/BANNER-END BANNER ... no triple-fault (grep Bochs log for '3rd (13)' / 'IDT.limit = 0x0' = 0). Model bochs.c on qemu.c (866 lines): generate bochsrc, spawn bochs, run the C RFB-unblock thread, capture the serial file, scan markers, detect triple-fault via the log. Then wire test-boot to a QEMU==Bochs serial differential. OPEN Q for the visual leg: mode 0x12 (640x480x16 planar) MIGHT render on RFB (untested) -- would need a planar console renderer; defer.
close_reason: C Bochs oracle (harness/emu/bochs.{c,h} + bochs_main.c) + test-boot-bochs gate landed (commit 4a0c38c). RFB unblock implemented in C (Law 3); generates the legacy-BIOS bochsrc, drives the debugger build headless, captures serial, scans for triple-fault. Gate boots the tracer under Bochs and asserts the mode-0x13 fallback (VBE-ENOMODE+VGA13) + the SAME kernel markers as QEMU (the differential) + no triple-fault; mutation-proven (latest BIOS->triple_fault=1->RED; bogus marker->RED). SERIAL-only by necessity (Bochs RFB can't display mode 0x13, bd memory). Not in default make test (env-specific + ~45s); run via 'make test-boot-bochs'. Full suite 55 host + 19 emu still GREEN. Optional follow-ups: a combined test-boot-tri target; promote into make test once stable; a visual leg would need mode 0x12 + planar renderer.

title: stage2 VBE mode-set fix for Bochs (ERR-VBE)
description: stage2.asm VBE 640x480 LFB mode-set fails on the Bochs VGA BIOS, blocking Bochs gating. Diagnose + add a fallback/compat path. Prereq for dual-emulator. Front D. Relates to initech-x0i.
design: SOLVED + path chosen. Bochs has no usable VBE LFB (legacy BIOS: VBE-ENOMODE; latest BIOS: VBE-E00 and won't POST -- CMOV/IDT.limit=0). Standard VGA mode 0x13 (320x200x256 linear @0xA0000) is CONFIRMED working on Bochs legacy-BIOS+pentium (framebuffer live, readback verified). Plan (4 commits): (1) console.c 8bpp linear renderer + host oracle; (2) stage2 vbe_setup falls back to INT10h mode 0x13 on VBE failure, boot_info gains lfb_width/height vars + bpp=8; (3) kmain DAC palette (port 0x3C8/9) + 8bpp seafoam fill; (4) Bochs boot gate (legacy BIOS+pentium+rfb_unblock) asserting serial markers -- feeds initech-564. QEMU path unchanged (VBE succeeds there).
notes: Native-BIOS attempt (bochsbios + bochs-term installed): BIOS-bochs-latest does NOT fix it -- the guest faults EARLY (interrupt with IDT.limit=0x0 at t~363k), never reaching stage2 (no S1). So neither path boots clean on Bochs: SeaBIOS reaches stage2 but VBE-E00 (no VBE), native BIOS crashes pre-stage2 (likely the i440FX/ACPI/USB env or the tiny 2/2/32 geometry vs what the native BIOS expects). CONCLUSION: Bochs boot is a dedicated multi-step effort (try: standard disk geometry for the native BIOS; or term-headless to remove RFB timing; or a stage2 non-LFB fallback for the SeaBIOS path), NOT a quick win. Headless mechanism (RFB-unblock client) proven. Deferring per effort; QEMU remains the binding boot gate.
close_reason: Standard-VGA mode-0x13 fallback implemented + verified (3 commits: console 8bpp renderer, stage2 VBE->mode-0x13 fallback, kmain 8bpp boot_info + DAC palette). Bochs (legacy BIOS + pentium) now boots end-to-end with ZERO faults; QEMU VBE path unchanged. Locked by committed green oracles: test-console (host, 8bpp section mutation-proven) + test-tracer-boot (QEMU). Full suite 55 host + 19 emu GREEN. The committed Bochs differential GATE is tracked in initech-564 (de-risked, ready to build). Diagnosis: no VBE LFB on Bochs (legacy: ENOMODE, latest: E00 + won't POST due to CMOV/IDT.limit); standard VGA mode 0x13 is the portable answer.

close_reason: Done in 4020728: test-kernel-repro + test-kernel-repro-mutant, wired into TEST_UNIT_GATES (47 host gates green). kernel.bin confirmed reproducible across a clean rebuild.
