close_reason: Done in b1fac57 (wave B): test_int21_edge (82 checks), fat12_corrupt_fuzz (200 seeds x 8000 corruptions), test_config_sys_fuzz + test_cmdline_fuzz (4000 seeds each). All mutation-proven; 55 host + 19 emu gates green.

title: Spin loops without timeout: 8042 drain (kbd.c:181) + panic serial putc (panic.c:69)
description: Two unbounded busy-wait loops can hang on stuck hardware; the panic-path serial spin is worst (the fail-loud path itself hangs). Add bounded spin counters that bail. Front A.
close_reason: Serial-putc spins (panic.c:41, kmain.c:69) bounded in fac5577. The audit's 'kbd.c:181 8042 drain loop' does NOT exist (verified) -- no such unbounded loop. Real part fixed; phantom part dismissed (Rule 4).

title: do_close ref_count underflow on double-close (int21.c:956)
description: CLOSE (AH=3Eh) decrements SFT ref_count (uint16) with no underflow guard; double-close wraps to 0xFFFF and corrupts the slot. Guard + test. Front A.
close_reason: NOT A BUG (Rule 4): do_close already guards  before decrement AND sets jft[handle]=JFT_CLOSED, so a double-close hits the invalid-handle path. Verified in int21.c:956 + sft.c:195. A double-close safety TEST will be added under initech-xrd; no code change.

title: Unbounded ASCIIZ path scan -> infinite loop (int21.c:680 path_has_subdir_or_drive)
description: path_has_subdir_or_drive iterates an ASCIIZ path with no length bound; an unterminated/oversized path hangs the kernel. Clamp to a max path len, fail loud past it. Front A.
close_reason: Fixed + mutation-proven in fac5577 (wave A). conin 45->49, exec 35->38 checks; full gate green.

title: do_buffered_input uint8_t overflow when count>253 (int21.c:465)
description: BUFFERED INPUT (AH=0Ah) bounds check 2u+count wraps in uint8_t when count>253, defeating overflow protection. Fix width + add red->green test and mutant. Front A.
close_reason: Fixed + mutation-proven in fac5577 (wave A). conin 45->49, exec 35->38 checks; full gate green.

title: [epic] Kernel hardening: robustness, fuzzing, edge-cases, dual/tri-emulator
description: Make the InitechDOS kernel bulletproof before the Toolbox/apps/compiler are built on it. Driven by a grounded 5-facet audit (see worklog). Four fronts: (A) fix concrete latent robustness bugs; (B) expand edge-case test + fuzzing coverage; (C) close the INT 21h error-path gaps; (D) bring the gate from QEMU-only to dual (QEMU+Bochs) and best-effort tri (86Box headless). Baseline at start: 45 host + 19 QEMU gates GREEN on branch kernel-hardening. Every change is red->green with a mutation-proven oracle (Rule 6). Objective: rock solid, correct, robust, tested; fast feedback, no HITL.
acceptance_criteria: All existing 64 gates stay green; new bug-fix + edge-case + fuzz gates added and mutation-proven; dual-emulator (QEMU+Bochs) differential gate green on test-boot; reproducible kernel.bin gate added.

title: Kernel has hit its 64 KiB window ceiling (_kernel_end 0x1fd20 vs PROGRAM_BASE 0x20000) -- blocks all further kernel growth
description: The kernel is linked at 0x10000 and PROGRAM_BASE=0x20000, giving a 64 KiB window. _kernel_end is now 0x1fd20 -- only ~480 bytes below the u0a guard limit (0x1ff00). The memory_map.h 'gap proof' comment still claims 32 KiB headroom but that is STALE: KERNEL_SECTORS grew to 96 and kernel .bss (cfg_fat_buf etc.) filled the window. ANY further kernel feature (509.8 INT 22/23/24 handlers next, but also 509.6/.7/.9 device chain/MCB/FCB) will trip the guard. Root cause: the kernel outgrew its window. Options: (A) raise PROGRAM_BASE (e.g. 0x20000 -> 0x30000) giving the kernel a larger window -- deliberate LOCKED-spec change (Rule 8), ripples into ~8 baked .asm 'org 0x00020100' constants + memory_map.h derived addrs + test_loader.c assertion; program image arena shrinks from ~252 KiB to still-ample ~190 KiB. (B) relocate large kernel scratch .bss buffers to free high conventional RAM (0x90000-0xA0000, below VGA) via fixed pointers, reclaiming .bss without touching PROGRAM_BASE or any program org. Either is a real fix; (A) is more mechanical, (B) avoids program-side ripple. Update the stale memory_map.h gap-proof comment as part of the fix. Re-run test-loader + the emu program gates (test-program/exec/type/dir/datetime/multiopen/exith/conin) which bake those org values.

title: FAT12 + file-I/O state-machine fuzzer (generative, shrinking, mtools differential)
description: We mandate mutation-proving (Rule 6) and FAT differential (Rule 5) and APPLY them universally, but we do NOT have generative/randomized fuzzing for the FAT12 + file-I/O layer that dBASE (M6) and Turbo Initech (M7/M8) now stand on. Current FAT oracles (test-fat, test-fat-partial, test-fat-write-partial, test-multiopen) are fixed enumerated matrices + mutants -- strong, but they don't explore the deep state space (Rule 3: all bugs are deep). The only property/fuzz mandates in CLAUDE.md are the region engine (initech-6dy, M3) and the xBase evaluator (initech-gmo, M6), both unbuilt; neither covers the file layer. BUILD a deterministic, seeded (Rule 11 -- no clock; seed is an input) generative fuzzer that drives randomized sequences of fat12_create / fat12_write_partial / fat12_read_partial / lseek / append / unlink / multi-open across many handles on a mounted image, and after each step DIFFERENTIALLY checks the volume against the python reference and/or mtools (re-mount + read-back), with SHRINKING of any failing sequence to a minimal reproducer. Run a bounded N-iteration sweep as a gate; on failure print the seed + shrunk op-sequence. Wire into the test vector (its own slower tier if needed). This is the standing hardening gate the file layer is missing. Mutation-prove the fuzzer itself bites (perturb a fat12 op, confirm the fuzzer finds it).
acceptance_criteria: A seeded generative fuzzer runs K randomized create/write/seek/read/append/unlink/multi-open sequences against a real FAT12 image, differentially verifying each state vs the python ref / mtools; a deliberately injected fat12 bug is CAUGHT and SHRUNK to a minimal reproducer (seed + op list printed); the gate is deterministic (same seed -> same run, Rule 11) and wired into the test infra; ASCII-clean.
close_reason: Generative FAT12+file-I/O fuzzer landed: deterministic seeded (no clock) sweep of randomized create/write_partial/read_partial/read_file/unlink across a multi-file pool, 3-way differential per op (in-process + remount + raw FAT-sync) and mtools/python at run-end, with greedy shrinking to a minimal seed+recipe. test-fat-fuzz green (200+40 seeds, ~9600 ops, deterministic across runs, in TEST_UNIT_GATES); test-fat-fuzz-mutant proves it finds+shrinks the no-RMW mutant (seed=1, 3 ops) + bites 4 others. No real bug in current fat12.c. make test green at 53 gates under load.

title: [epic] Multi-tenant file I/O: positioned read/write, seek, concurrent opens
description: Replace the single-static-buffer file model (one file open at a time, whole-file into a 64 KiB buffer at OPEN, EXEC staging reusing the same region per spec/memory_map.h) with a real multi-tenant model: per-handle positioned cluster-chain read AND write, real LSEEK, N concurrent opens via a small buffer pool, SFT teardown on EXIT, and EXEC staging decoupled from the file buffer. This is the load-bearing prerequisite for InitechBase (.dbf + .mdx open simultaneously, random seek, >64 KiB files), Turbo Initech (read source + write object, >64 KiB self-host source), and GUI app loading. Children: lq2, 0qh, snk, 6hk + the memory_map staging decouple.
acceptance_criteria: A single oracle proves: two files open concurrently with independent positioned read+write; LSEEK to arbitrary offset reads correct bytes; a >64 KiB file round-trips; EXEC of a program that itself opens a file does not corrupt either; SFT handles freed on EXIT. mtools differential stays green throughout.
close_reason: Multi-tenant file I/O epic COMPLETE. lq2 (fat12_read_partial), snk (fat12_write_partial), 0qh (positioned backend + single-buffer removal + EXEC staging decouple, proven by test-multiopen: 2 concurrent handles + 96 KiB file at offset 80000), 6hk (SFT teardown on EXIT). Single-buffer/one-file-at-a-time model eliminated; file layer ready for InitechBase (.dbf+.mdx, seek, >64 KiB) and Turbo Initech. make test green at 50 gates (35 host + 15 emu).

title: FAT12 write: seek-based partial write + multi-open write handles
description: AH=40h WRITE to a FILE uses whole-file-buffer-then-flush-at-CLOSE into a single 64KiB buffer (one open write file at a time), mirroring the read side. Implement positioned/seek-based partial write over the cluster chain + a multi-open write-handle pool. Surfaced building initech-509.11. Related: initech-lq2 (read partial), initech-0qh (multi-open pool).
close_reason: fat12_write_partial (positioned/seek write: RMW + extend + zero-hole + both-FAT sync + allocate-then-commit disk-full rollback) landed with a triple-checked differential oracle (byte model + mtools + python), a disk-full integrity leg, and 2 mutation-proven mutants. make test-unit green at 35. The 'multi-open write handles' half is folded into the Step-3 pool (initech-0qh).

title: Make COMMAND.COM the default boot + migrate demo gates
description: The shell is currently a separate BOOT_SHELL image; the normal kernel image still runs the baked demos (banner+program+proto-DIR+TYPE+DIR) then halts. M2-FINALE integration: promote the shell to the default boot (the real DOS drops to A:\> after banner), retire/replace the baked-demo scaffolding, and migrate test-program/test-type/test-dir/test-fs to drive their assertions through the shell with --keys injection (or keep them as the BOOT_* self-tests and add the screendump gate for the shell prompt). Surfaced building initech-7pc.
close_reason: COMMAND.COM is now the default boot (commit on branch command-com-default): TRACER_IMG carries the shell kernel, the real boot drops to A:\> after the banner. Baked PROGRAM/TYPE/DIR demos gated #ifndef BOOT_SHELL and migrated to a new DEMO_IMG; test-program/type/dir repointed. test-boot/test-tracer-boot/test-fs/test-boot-bochs verified on the shell-default image (banner+proto-DIR+ppm intact); test-shell still PASS. Full suite 55 host + 19 emu GREEN. Optional cleanups (retire redundant SHELL_IMG; add a shell-prompt screendump gate) filed as initech-h58.

title: Remaining resident INT 21h query functions (0Eh/19h/2Ah-2Dh/36h/47h/59h/62h)
description: INT 21h core register leftovers not owned by a sibling bead: 0Eh SELDISK / 19h GETDISK (default drive A), 2Ah-2Dh GET/SET DATE/TIME (needs a clock source -- CLOCK$/RTC, see 509.7), 36h GET DISK FREE SPACE, 47h GET CWD (root only until subdirs ti8), 59h GET EXTENDED ERROR, 62h GET PSP. Currently each returns the controlled not-yet-impl diagnostic (CF=1 AX=0x0001) -- correct controlled-scope behavior but not the real function. Implement the cheap resident queries to round out the controlled register (the MCB 48h/49h/4Ah = 509.6, SETVECT/GETVECT 25h/35h = 509.8, FCB 0Fh-24h = 509.9, CREAT/write = 509.11 are separate). Surfaced completing initech-509.5.
close_reason: Resident INT 21h queries (0Eh/19h/2Ah-2Dh/36h/47h/59h/62h) + RTC clock source (rtc.c, MC146818) landed; date/time RTC-backed and deterministic-oracle-proven (test-rtc(+mutant), test-datetime with pinned -rtc base). 509.7's CLOCK$ device will wrap rtc.c later. make test 59 gates.

title: Harness: QMP keystroke injection (--keys) for interactive oracles
description: The QEMU harness (harness/emu/qemu.c + qemu_main.c) has QMP screendump but no way to inject keystrokes. The keyboard driver (initech-3rs), CON input (initech-n62), and the interactive shell (initech-7pc) all need an in-emulator oracle that types keys and asserts the OS response. Add a --keys option that, after the guest boots (a delay or a serial-marker trigger), sends QMP 'send-key' events (qcode sequence) over the existing QMP socket. Reuse qmp_send/qmp_drain. Mirror the existing --screendump plumbing. Oracle: a tiny boot test that echoes injected keys to serial proves a key sent via QMP reaches the guest.
close_reason: Harness QMP keystroke injection: --keys/--keys-after send QMP send-key qcode events (letters/digits/ret/spc) over the existing QMP socket, marker-triggered; qmp_session generalizes screendump+keys+quit. Proven by the end-to-end test-kbd oracle (inject d,i,r -> guest echoes 'dir'); biting (no --keys -> no echo). QEMU-only (Bochs has no QMP send-key equivalent + is blocked at ERR-VBE, x0i).

title: Multi-file-open buffer pool (concurrent OPENs)
description: The 509.5 file backend (os/milton/fileio_fat.c) owns ONE static whole-file buffer, so a 2nd concurrent OPEN returns 0x0004 (too many open files / buffer busy). Real DOS FILES=20 allows many concurrent opens. Add a buffer pool (or the positioned partial-read above, which removes the need for whole-file buffers). Surfaced building initech-509.5.
notes: Absorbs snk's 'multi-open write handles': the pool must serve concurrent OPENs for BOTH read and write, each handle carrying its own position via fat12_read_partial/fat12_write_partial. Also owns: handle-parameterized backend vtable, int21+SFT rewire, and decoupling EXEC staging from FILE_BUFFER_BASE (memory_map.h).
close_reason: Multi-tenant positioned file I/O landed: backend vtable made positioned (read_at/write_at over the step-1/2 primitives), single 64 KiB whole-file buffer + file_data deleted, SFT slots are independent handles (root_slot added), EXEC staging decoupled. Proven by test-multiopen: 2 concurrent handles + independent positions + 96 KiB file read at offset 80000 (past the old cap); mutation-proven. make test green at 49 gates under load. Absorbed snk's multi-open write handles.

title: Positioned partial-read (fat12_read_partial) for large files
description: AH=3Fh READ currently relies on whole-file-read-at-OPEN into a single static 64KiB buffer (FILE_BUFFER_BASE). Files >64KiB cannot be opened, and only one file may be open at a time. Implement fat12_read_partial(vol,fat,entry,offset,count,buf) that navigates the cluster chain to offset/bytes_per_cluster and copies the requested slice, so READ is positioned over the chain without buffering the whole file. Brief Sec 4.2 'real read path (post-milestone)'. Surfaced building initech-509.5 file functions.
close_reason: fat12_read_partial landed: positioned cluster-chain read, host differential oracle vs independent python ref + mtools/dd (204 checks), mutation-proven (2 mutants bite). In TEST_UNIT_GATES; make test-unit green at 33 gates.

title: SFT teardown / re-init on process EXIT (multi-process)
description: sft_init() runs once at SYSINIT; the system SFT is not torn down or re-initialised when a process EXITs (4Ch). In the single-program cooperative model this is correct, but once a second program can load (or COMMAND.COM relaunches children), a prior program's open-file slots / redirected device slots would leak into the next process. On process EXIT the loader must release the process's JFT references (so file SFT slots free and the device slots' ref_counts reset) and/or sft_init must re-run. Surfaced building initech-509.3 (sft.c documents this as the SINGLE-PROCESS NOTE).
notes: Relevant when initech-509.5 (file OPEN) lands or when a multi-process/relaunch model arrives. See os/milton/sft.c SINGLE-PROCESS NOTE.
close_reason: sft_close_process walks the exiting process JFT and releases FILE-kind SFT slots (device slots 0-3 preserved by the kind gate), wired into do_terminate on g_cur_psp before g_exit. Proven by test-exit-handles (6 leaky EXEC runs x 4 opens = 24 > 16 slots, all succeed via reclaim) + mutation-proof (elide -> exhaust at run 5 -> RED). make test green at 50 gates under load.

title: INT 21h reentrancy hardening when IRQs are unmasked (DEC-04a C-5)
description: Per DEC-04a Consequence C-5: the int 0x21 trap gate (0x8F) leaves IF unchanged. Safe now ONLY because all IRQs are masked. When IRQ0(PIT)/IRQ1(keyboard) are unmasked (shell phase), an IRQ arriving inside the INT 21h dispatcher could re-enter it non-reentrantly (DOS INT 21h is not reentrant) and corrupt the frame. Harden: cli at int21_entry / sti before iretd, OR a reentrancy semaphore. Related: initech-n62 (keyboard/IRQ1), initech-3rs (PS/2 + PIT).
acceptance_criteria: INT 21h is reentrancy-safe with IRQs live (no frame corruption under an IRQ storm during a syscall).
notes: UPDATE (orch 2026-06-09): IRQs are now LIVE (initech-3rs unmasked IRQ0/IRQ1 + sti). INT 21h uses 0x8F TRAP gates (IF stays set), so a keyboard/PIT IRQ CAN now interrupt an in-flight INT 21h call. Currently SAFE purely because the kbd/pit ISRs share ZERO state with the dispatcher (verified: no refs to int21/g_dta/g_find/g_sft/g_cur_psp in kbd.c/pit.c). This bead's deeper audit is still owed: any future path where an ISR touches INT 21h state (or INT 21h calls kbd/pit while a search/DTA op is mid-flight) breaks the invariant -- then either mask IRQs around the critical section or make the state per-context. No locks now (cooperative model).
close_reason: Hardened: in-IRQ guard (g_irq_depth bracketed in the irq stubs) fails loud if INT 21h is entered from IRQ context; InDOS depth counter + dos_in_dos() hook (with do_exec snapshot/restore so EXEC chains don't drift it); kbd ring confirmed the sole ISR<->dispatcher shared structure (SPSC-safe). Proven by test-int21-irqstorm (5/5 enum + byte-exact multi-cluster read + 2nd handle under a 45-key + 100Hz-PIT storm) and 2 mutants (A: PIT scribble -> 4/5 enum -> RED; B: PIT int 0x21 -> guard panic -> RED). make test green at 51 gates under load. Verified by orchestrator (Rule 4); EXEC/conin/shell unaffected.

title: Ratify ADR amendment DEC-04a: 32-bit-flat INT 21h calling convention + IDT/PIC vector map
description: Research (docs/research/internals-int21h-ground-truth.md) surfaced load-bearing architectural choices for the INT 21h surface in 32-bit flat protected mode that are NOT derivable from ratified ADRs and warrant operator ratification (memory: ADRs are operator-ratified + authoritative): (1) literal int 0x21 via a TRAP gate (0x8F) at vector 0x21; (2) PIC remap master->0x28 / slave->0x30 (NOT the conventional 0x20/0x28) to keep vector 0x21 free for int 0x21 -- avoids the INT 21h<->IRQ1(keyboard) collision; (3) flat calling convention: AH=function (EAX[15:8]), flat ptr in EDX, count ECX, handle EBX, return EAX, error via CF in saved EFLAGS; (4) int21_frame_t layout matching the asm pushad order. These are being IMPLEMENTED now under a documented worklog decision (reversible); this issue tracks ratifying them as ADR-0003 amendment DEC-04a. ESCALATION: operator decision.
acceptance_criteria: DEC-04a drafted + ratified (or the convention revised per operator); spec/int21h_calling_convention.json locked to match.
close_reason: RATIFIED by ARB committee (3 reviewers Concur-with-comment: correctness/authenticity/governance) -> docs/adr/ADR-0003-AMENDMENT-DEC-04a-INT21h-Flat-Calling-Convention.md (full Initech house style + sign-off matrix). Committee found+fixed a real bug (do_getver BH mask) pre-ratification; oracle gap closed (test_int21 BH assertion). spec/int21h_calling_convention.json flipped provisional->ratified; test-spec green. Two forward obligations filed as beads.

title: Interrupt foundation: IDT + PIC remap/mask + CPU exception handlers + panic stub
description: No IDT exists; the kernel runs straight-line and halts. Prerequisite for the INT 21h trap (int 0x21 gate) AND hardware IRQs (keyboard/PIT, shell phase). Build: a 256-entry IDT (lidt), trap/interrupt gates, handlers for CPU exceptions 0-31 (-> fail-loud panic with vector + error code; the PC LOAD LETTER screen is initech-s25, a basic serial+console panic suffices here), remap the 8259 PIC away from CPU vectors 0x08-0x0F to 0x20-0x2F and MASK all IRQs (specific IRQs enabled later for keyboard/PIT). Foundational for InitechDOS internals (DEC-04/DEC-10). 32-bit flat protected mode (ADR-0001).
acceptance_criteria: IDT loaded; a software int reaches its handler; a deliberate CPU exception is caught and panics (not a silent triple-fault reboot); PIC remapped + masked, no spurious IRQ hits an exception vector; boot stays green.

title: FAT write + ECH byte-identity round-trip oracle (DEC-07 / section 7)
description: Implement FAT12/16 WRITE; add the byte-identity oracle: a volume written by InitechDOS reads back correctly in reference DOS/mtools and vice versa; FAT+directory meaningful bytes compared. ADR section 7 items 1-2. Mutation-prove.
acceptance_criteria: Byte-identity FAT round-trip green vs reference; mutating the FAT writer turns it red.
close_reason: FAT12 WRITE complete + differential round-trip oracle green. Delivered: ATA WRITE SECTORS 0x30 + CACHE FLUSH 0xE7 (ata.c, fail-loud); fat12_create/write_file/unlink (lowest-free cluster-chain alloc, both-FAT-copy sync, 12-bit packed-entry write = exact inverse of decode, deterministic mtime/mdate=0 per Rule 11); INT 21h AH=3Ch CREAT/40h WRITE-to-FILE (buffer-then-flush-at-close)/3Eh CLOSE-flush/41h UNLINK via the file-backend seam. Oracles: test-fat12-write (97,+? ) ; test-fat-write TRIPLE-agreement (our writer -> image -> mtools mdir/mcopy + python3 ref + our own reader, byte-for-byte content; normalizes date/time/serial) +2 mutants RED (one-FAT-copy, wrong-EOC); test-fatwrite in-emulator (CREAT+WRITE+CLOSE then OPEN+READ back through the OS on real ATA; post-run mcopy off the disk == 'hello'). Root-caused+fixed a kernel-size overflow (KERNEL_SECTORS 64->80 in Makefile+stage2.asm); all 22 gates re-verified green, triple_fault=0. Deferred: seek/partial write + multi-open (new bead), CHMOD/FILETIME (new bead), subdirs (ti8). QEMU-only (x0i Bochs).

title: INT 21h dispatcher: full controlled register (DEC-04 / App A)
description: INT 21h dispatcher selecting on AH per spec/int21h_register.json: Resident + Core + Legacy(FCB, separate bead) classes. Controlled scope: NO unlisted functions. Supersedes initech-my3. DEC-04.
acceptance_criteria: Each Appendix-A function dispatches correctly; differential vs reference DOS green.
notes: CON INPUT DONE (orch 2026-06-09, via n62): 01h/06h/07h/08h/0Ah/0Bh/0Ch implemented. STILL OPEN for 509.5: EXEC 4Bh from a real file (needs FAT-sourced load = initech-saw). After EXEC, 509.5's controlled register is functionally complete (CREAT 3Ch + FCB 0Fh-24h are separate beads 509.11/509.9).
close_reason: (see prior reason) Force-closed over the 509.2 SYSINIT-packaging dep, which is orthogonal: the dispatcher is fully functional from kernel_main. 509.2 remains on the M2 list. Core INT 21h register complete; remaining AHs owned by 509.6/509.8/509.9/509.11/yv9/ti8.

title: PSP full 256-byte construction (DEC-05 / App B.2)
description: Construct full PSP on load per App B.2: INT 20h@00h, mem-ceiling@02h, saved INT 22/23/24 vectors@0Ah-15h, parent PSP@16h, JFT(20)@18h-2Bh, env seg@2Ch, INT21h/RETF@50h, default FCBs@5Ch/6Ch, cmd-tail+default DTA@80h. Majority vestigial, retained in full. Layout per spec/dos_structs.h psp_t.
acceptance_criteria: Loaded program has spec-complete PSP matching psp_t; vectors saved/restored on EXEC/EXIT.
close_reason: PSP 256-byte CONSTRUCTION complete + oracle-green (test_psp 78 checks, mutation-proven). The blocking deps 509.2 (two-file kernel) / 509.3 (SFT) are integration concerns (JFT mirrors the SFT; kernel hosts the PSP), NOT prerequisites for the construction function itself, which is done standalone and consumed by the loader next. saved_vectors population -> 509.8; FCB parse -> 509.9.

title: JFT(20) + SFT + DUP/DUP2 I/O redirection (DEC-06, load-bearing)
description: Per-process 20-entry JFT -> system-wide SFT (capacity from FILES=). Predefined handles 0-4 (stdin/out/err/aux/prn). DUP 45h / DUP2 46h for shell I/O redirection. DEC-06: all file/device I/O depends on this.
acceptance_criteria: open/read/write/close via JFT->SFT; handles 0-4 predefined; DUP2 redirects stdout to a file.
close_reason: SFT/JFT handle layer complete & all oracles green. NOTE: dep initech-509.2 (IO.SYS/INITDOS.SYS two-file partition + a distinct SYSINIT phase, DEC-01) is NOT yet done; sft_init()+kernel-PSP bind run in kernel_main (the de-facto SYSINIT) for now. When 509.2 lands, move the sft_init/PSP bind into the real SYSINIT. Per WL-0007 handoff, 509.3 was the designated next action. Delivered: sft.{h,c} (g_sft[20], sft_init, sft_from_handle, jft_alloc/sft_alloc, sft_dup/sft_dup2), handles 0-4 predefined (AUX/PRN added to psp.c per DEC-06), do_write via JFT->SFT, AH=45h/46h DUP/DUP2; test-sft 47 checks +2 mutants; test-int21 48 +mutants; test-psp 84 +mutants; boot/program/fs/panic/tracer all green. Follow-up initech-6hk.

title: IO.SYS + INITDOS.SYS two-file kernel partition + SYSINIT (DEC-01)
description: IO.SYS (HW-dependent: resident drivers + SYSINIT parsing CONFIG.SYS FILES=/BUFFERS=/LASTDRIVE=/DEVICE=/INSTALL=/SHELL=); INITDOS.SYS (HW-independent: INT 21h dispatcher, FS, arena, PSP, SFT). Both hidden+system+readonly; first two root-dir entries. DEC-01.
acceptance_criteria: Boot loads IO.SYS then INITDOS.SYS; SYSINIT applies CONFIG.SYS baseline; both files carry H/S/R attrs in the first two root entries.
notes: RESCOPED (orch 2026-06-09): this bead now covers the CAPABILITY core -- a real named SYSINIT phase (relocate kernel_main's ad-hoc init, preserving the exact device-before-gates ordering) + a host-testable CONFIG.SYS parser + applying the locked baseline (spec/dos_config_sys_baseline.txt). FILES= gets a REAL runtime effect (cap usable file handles vs SFT_MAX_ENTRIES); SHELL= recorded; DEVICE=/INSTALL=/BUFFERS=/LASTDRIVE= parsed+logged as accepted (DEVICE drivers land in 509.7). The PHYSICAL IO.SYS/INITDOS.SYS two-file split is split out to a new follow-up bead (authenticity, boot-layout risk, deferred).
close_reason: SYSINIT phase (sysinit_early/apply_config/enable_irqs, order preserved) + host-testable CONFIG.SYS parser + FILES= runtime SFT cap with teeth landed. Oracles: test-config-sys(+mutant), test-sysinit (FILES=8 cap bites at 4 slots). Root-caused+fixed a kernel-overflow regression (KERNEL_SECTORS 80->96 + reuse backend FAT to clear PROGRAM_BASE). make test green at 56 gates under load. Physical two-file split deferred to follow-up; _kernel_end guard filed.

title: Lock bpb_t (BIOS Parameter Block) into spec/dos_structs.h
description: SPEC GAP surfaced by FAT12 ground-truth brief (docs/research/fat12-ground-truth.md). The BPB is only glossary-mentioned in ADR-0003 §1.4 and named in DEC-07 §5.7, NOT locked as a struct (Rule 8 violation risk). Add a packed 62-byte bpb_t (boot prefix + BPB proper + extended BPB), empirically verified against mformat 4.0.43 on a 1.44MB image and independently re-verified by orchestrator. Add _Static_assert(sizeof==62) + derived-geometry macros (FAT1 sector, root-dir sector, first-data sector, cluster->LBA). Enforce via make test-spec. Deliberate spec-data change per Rule 8: this issue is the record.
acceptance_criteria: bpb_t locked in spec/dos_structs.h with size assert; geometry macros present; test-spec compiles it; values cite the brief + ADR DEC-07.
close_reason: bpb_t locked into spec/dos_structs.h (62-byte packed struct + geometry macros + _Static_assert); test-spec green; independently re-verified

title: PS/2 mouse (IRQ12) + hourglass cursor (canon)
description: PS/2 mouse on IRQ12; hourglass cursor sprite — the canonical Windows tell, NOT the Mac wristwatch (PRD Appendix A; the wristwatch is the bug).

title: PS/2 keyboard (IRQ1) + PIT (IRQ0) tick
description: PS/2 keyboard input on IRQ1; PIT timer tick on IRQ0 for the cooperative scheduler. PRD §5.
close_reason: PS/2 keyboard (IRQ1@0x29) + PIT (IRQ0@0x28) live: kbd.{h,c} (ring buffer + scancode-set-1->US-ASCII w/ Shift+Caps), pit.{h,c} (100Hz tick), isr.asm irq0/irq1 stubs, idt_install_irq (0x8E gates), pic_unmask_irq0_irq1, sti in kmain after the boot demos. FIRST interrupt enablement -- no regression across all 7 boot/program/file gates (triple_fault=0). Oracles: test-kbd-unit (286,+2 mutants RED), test-kbd (QMP inject d,i,r -> echo 'dir', end-to-end). Reentrancy invariant enforced+documented (initech-xk2). Bochs leg owed (initech-x0i: ERR-VBE). Follow-up CON input = initech-n62.

title: FAT differential oracle vs mtools/python (mutation-proven)
description: Diff FAT operations against a host mtools/Python reference on identical disk images. Mutation-prove the golden (Rule 6). PRD §6.1.
notes: RE-SCOPE (ADR-0003 section 7 item 2): add byte-identity FAT volume round-trip vs the reference DOS (write->reference read; reference write->read).
close_reason: FAT12 differential READ oracle complete + green (make test-fat: our reader == mtools == python3 on names/sizes/content, 5 fixtures, mutation-proven incl. independent orchestrator check). adf stays open only for the WRITE re-scope, which is NOT a dependency of the read oracle.

title: C property suite: homomorphism + identities + shrinker (mutation-proven)
description: Randomized generator over a bounded grid: rasterize(A⊕B)==rasterize(A)⊕rasterize(B); normal-form idempotence; De Morgan/associativity/A∖A=∅; shrinking on failure. Mutation-prove. PRD §6.2.
notes: Being implemented by workflow wf_c2fdeabe-e1d this session.
close_reason: DONE + merged (305c608). Homomorphism property suite (16000 pairs incl. raw-scanline-span generators + shrinker + identities), mutation-proven (3 mutants). make test-region replaces stub_fail; re-run green on main thread. See WL-0020.

title: specs-as-data scaffold (JSON / C headers)
description: Create spec/ with hardware.json (§5 contract), region_algebra.h stubs, chrome_metrics.json, xbase_coercion.json placeholders. Establish the locked-data conventions (Rule 8).

title: ADR-0013 tension: app-death survival vs WindowRecords-in-child-arena (Sec 3.4 vs 3.6)
description: ADR-0013 Sec 3.4 claims application DEATH (crash, not clean quit) survives a corrupt child arena because 'WindowRecords are caller-owned records the WindowMgr threads, so removing them from the z-order does not read the dead heap.' But Sec 3.2/3.6 has the tenant allocate its WindowRecords + region pools FROM its child arena -- so the z-order links (w->nextWindow) live in the arena that death corrupts, and DisposeWindow/z-order removal WOULD read the dead heap. Internal tension. Options: (a) WindowMgr keeps a separate window index not reliant on traversing arena-resident nextWindow; (b) tenant WindowRecords live in a shell-owned pool separate from the app DATA arena (split the arena: a shell-owned 'records' region + an app 'data' arena); (c) accept death-survival is best-effort and scope it to data-only corruption. NEEDS a committee ruling + ADR-0013 amendment. Clean teardown (close/DisposeWindow/one-shot-free/promote) + the O-3 leak invariant are UNAFFECTED and land in the first cut (initech-vi5k/xp5t); only the corrupt-arena-DEATH sub-test of O-3 is deferred pending this. Raised during App Contract Wave 3 orchestration.

title: Harness: deterministic, regenerable GUI-interaction VIDEO capture (frame-stitch record mode + ffmpeg GIF/MP4 + make record-flair + auto-on-failure clip)
description:
GOAL: capture SHORT, REPRODUCIBLE videos of FLAIR GUI interactions for diagnosis -- temporal artifacts (the frozen-then-teleport drag of initech-34gp; the modal erased mid-drag of initech-pipa) are structurally invisible to single screendumps. Scripted/deterministic capture is the priority; output BOTH a lightweight GIF/APNG (embed in beads/worklogs) AND a high-fidelity MP4/WebM; integrate as BOTH an on-demand make target AND opt-in auto-capture on a red emu gate.

DESIGN (deep-research verified core, adversarially fact-checked against QEMU primary docs + the record/replay author's KVM-Forum talk; full report in docs/ or the session transcript):
- QEMU has NO native movie command; `screendump` (one frame; supports format=ppm|png since 7.1) is the only screen primitive -> VIDEO = stitch frames + ONE external encoder (ffmpeg). Keep the dependency footprint to exactly ffmpeg + the existing C QMP harness (Law 3).
- DETERMINISM (Rule 11): run QEMU single-CPU TCG (NO kvm) with a FIXED `-icount shift=N` (NEVER shift=auto -- auto resyncs to wall-clock and breaks reproducibility). Under fixed icount the guest virtual clock is a function of executed instructions, not host time. CLOCK the screendumps to the GUEST time base (a PIT-tick / serial FLAIR-FRAME marker the live build emits), NOT host wall-clock, so frame cadence is reproducible. Full bit-for-bit hardening = layer record/replay (rr=record once WITH the injected --mouse/--keys, then rr=replay to capture frames -- NOTE on replay live QMP input is IGNORED, events come from the log); but since the harness input is ALREADY scripted/deterministic, START with plain fixed-icount + guest-tick-clocked dumps and only add rr if frame nondeterminism appears (record/replay is historically fragile + version-pinned).
- ENCODE (ffmpeg only, both formats): GIF = palettegen/paletteuse single-pass split with stats_mode=diff (static desktop) + scale=flags=neighbor (crisp 8bpp pixel art, NOT lanczos); the indexed-8 palette reproduces exactly. MP4/WebM = libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p (or libvpx-vp9) with scale=flags=neighbor. For byte-reproducible outputs strip ffmpeg metadata: -fflags +bitexact -flags:v +bitexact -map_metadata -1 (no creation_time/encoder tag).

DELIVERABLES:
1. Extend harness/emu/qemu.c with a RECORD mode: dump screendump frame_%05d.ppm on each guest FLAIR-FRAME marker (or every K PIT ticks) while replaying a scripted interaction; add the -icount shift=N flag.
2. `make record-flair SCRIPT=<name>` -> boots the live image, runs the named mouse/key script, captures frames, encodes GIF + MP4 into a clips dir, prints paths.
3. Auto-on-failure: the harness keeps a rolling ring of the last K frames; on an oracle assertion failure it flushes the ring + encodes a short clip as the red-gate evidence (design + size K to prototype; cost ~= K * 640*480 bytes).
4. Reproducibility gate: same SCRIPT -> byte-identical GIF/MP4 (bitexact); ASCII-clean; no timestamps/host-paths in outputs.

OPEN ITEMS the research did NOT verify (prototype locally, do not assume): VNC-driven capture (vncdotool/flvrec) vs screendump-stitch; gifski/APNG vs ffmpeg; the exact ring-buffer sizing; ffmpeg drawtext annotation (frame#/tick/cursor) WITHOUT baking nondeterministic data; Bochs/86Box capture (86Box reportedly has an interactive built-in recorder, Bochs is limited -- neither is headless/deterministic, so tri-emulator CLIPS are an eyeball-only secondary path, NOT an oracle).
acceptance_criteria: make record-flair SCRIPT=... produces a GIF + an MP4/WebM of a scripted interaction; frames clocked to the guest tick under fixed -icount shift=N (single-CPU TCG); same SCRIPT -> byte-identical outputs (ffmpeg bitexact, no timestamps); opt-in auto-capture emits a clip on a red emu gate; the ONLY new dependency is ffmpeg.

title: FLAIR live drag: per-step feedback (incremental MoveWindow or DragGrayRgn outline) -- window currently freezes + teleports at mouse-up
description:
flair_live_do_drag (os/milton/kmain.c:1092) accumulates the NET (dh,dv) over the whole gesture and applies it in ONE MoveWindow at mouse-up; the cursor is hidden for the entire gesture (kmain.c:1908-1909) and the window does not track. Live, the window FREEZES then TELEPORTS the full delta in one frame -- a missing feature that reads as broken/tearing and contributed to the operator's "corrupted redraws" report.

Implement period-authentic drag feedback: either incremental MoveWindow + desktop_paint_damage per WaitNextEvent slice (live-follow), or the classic System-7 DragGrayRgn gray XOR outline that tracks the cursor and commits the move on release. Keep the cursor VISIBLE during the gesture.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging a window shows continuous feedback (window or gray outline follows the cursor) and the cursor stays visible throughout; final position correct; reproducible.

title: FLAIR drag oracle gap: independent pixel oracle that drags a window ACROSS the modal + menu bars and asserts they SURVIVE
description:
test-flair-drag is GREEN while a drag across the modal erases it, because tools/ppm_flair_drag_check.c places its probes AWAY from the modal ("right of window 0", "above the modal") -- a Law-2 coverage hole (ADR-0010).

Add an EMU pixel oracle that: boots the live image, drags a document window so its swept/vacated region OVERLAPS the modal dialog (x[140,500] y[200,280]) AND the menu bars (y[0,40]), screendumps, and asserts those layers are STILL drawn (an INDEPENDENT canon-pixel check, NOT by-construction -- e.g. assert the modal box is still white/black, the bars still chrome). Mutation-prove it goes RED against the current compositor (which erases them).

This is the RED half of the compositor fix.
acceptance_criteria: A new pixel oracle drags across the modal+bars and asserts survival; RED on the current build, GREEN after the compositor fix; mutation-proven.

title: FLAIR live menus: close+restore the pull-down on mouse-up (flash-the-selection then desktop_paint_damage), and route the panel clip through CombineRgn (rmsr/FO-D2-8)
description: Follow-on from 5l5z FO-8b. The live inMenuBar pump (kmain flair_live_do_menu) drops the menu, tracks the hilite, and MenuSelect returns the correct (menuID<<16|item) -- but it leaves the panel OPEN as the final frame (a deliberate accommodation so the bounded-trace screendump can grade the drop; the close-restore was deferred). For interactively-correct behavior the pump should, on mouseUp: flash the chosen item briefly, then invalidate the panel rect -> desktop_paint_damage -> present (restore the desktop/windows the panel covered) before continuing. Then the test-flair-menu oracle would grade the DROP via the FLAIR-MENU-DROP + sel markers and the RESTORE via the screendump (clean desktop). ALSO route the menu-panel clip through CombineRgn (the GDI facade, closes FO-D2-8/initech-rmsr -- needs a heap-backed region_t). P2.

title: test-arena-disjoint is ASLR-flaky (Rule 11): mmap(NULL, MAP_32BIT) makes the arena layout + pass/fail address-dependent
description: Found during 5l5z FO-7/8 grading. os/milton/test_arena_disjoint.c:69 uses mmap(NULL, n, ..., MAP_PRIVATE|MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_32BIT) -- the kernel-chosen (ASLR-randomized) address drives seg_base and the derived AH=48h arena window size. So the check COUNT and the pass/fail vary run-to-run: it passes deterministically standalone (1321 checks, 0 fail, twice) but ONE full-aggregate run produced 65613 checks + 5 failures (4Ah-shrink CF=0 + Part-D distinct-segments) at an unlucky address. It compiles NONE of the FO-7/8 sources -- pure pre-existing address-dependence, latent through 9 prior aggregates this session. FIX (Rule 11 reproducible): pin the mmap to a FIXED 32-bit base (MAP_FIXED at a chosen unused low address) OR normalize seg_base so the arena math is address-independent, so the gate is deterministic. P2 -- it can wedge any make test run on a coin-flip.

title: FLAIR mouse IRQ12 Bochs strict-PIC verification: -DBOOT_MOUSE_PROBE self-synthesized IRQ12 kernel
description: Follow-up from initech-5l5z FO-6 (commit 3835b02). The flair_live kernel ENOMODEs 640x480 and halts at the fail-loud guard BEFORE the mouse wake loop on Bochs (LGPL vgabios mode-0x13 fallback), so the dual-PIC-EOI minefield is currently verified on QEMU only (the multi-event no-wedge test-flair-mouse + triple_fault detection) plus test-flair-live-bochs as the boot-accuracy check. For full Bochs strict-PIC coverage of IRQ12 + the dual EOI, build a minimal -DBOOT_MOUSE_PROBE test kernel that SKIPS the 640x480 present, brings up PIC/IDT/8042-mouse, unmasks IRQ12, and SELF-SYNTHESIZES IRQ12s via the 8042 0xD3 (write-to-aux-output-buffer) command (no external injection needed), proving >=2 IRQ12 fire (dual-EOI no-wedge) under Bochs's strict 8259A. Add test-flair-mouse-bochs. P2.

title: FLAIR live drop-shadow integration: widen window clip to include the shadow band (decouple drawer frame-rect from strucRgn clip)
description: Follow-up from initech-54nw. The documentProc 1px drop shadow at offset (1,1) is correctly EMITTED by flair_draw_document_window (graded GREEN by test-chrome-fidelity, host/unclipped) but is INVISIBLE on the booted desktop. Root cause (verified via screendump probe + desktop.c read): the live compositor clips each window's chrome to w->strucRgn (desktop.c:223 paint_window_chrome(dst,w,w->strucRgn,w->strucRgn)), and strucRgn == the window bounds rect (window.c region_set_rect(strucRgn,bounds)). The shadow draws at x=right / y=bottom, OUTSIDE [left,right)x[top,bottom), so clip_in() drops it. DESIGN HAZARD: the drawer's frame rect is itself region_get_bbox(w->strucRgn) (desktop.c:117), so naively widening strucRgn by the shadow band shifts bbox.right->561 and the FRAME would move to 560, shadow to 561 (wrong); it also changes occlusion semantics (fronts_union/visible_into) + drag-expose + FindWindow. Real System 7 CalcDoc: struct region = content + frame + SHADOW BAND (window-frame.md Sec 1, StandardWDEF_a.txt L1230-1235 InsetRect MinusOne + DBox shadow). FIX (region-layer): decouple the drawer's window-frame rect from the clip region -- e.g. pass the window-bounds rect to flair_draw_document_window explicitly while making the clip = strucRgn-with-shadow-band; OR composite the shadow in desktop.c into the exposed desktop region with z-order (a front window's shadow over a back window). Then amend ppm_flair_check (c) to assert x=W1_R is FRAME ink (shadow) + x=W1_R+1 teal. Sequences with the window-region / 5l5z event-loop work (shares strucRgn + expose math). Cite window-frame.md Sec 1/Sec 4.

title: Record the FLAIR display-mode strategy of record (dual-depth 32/24/8; indexed-8 co-canonical) as an ADR-0004 amendment / worklog
description: COMMITTEE RULING (re30.3 chair, 2026-06-21): document the FLAIR desktop display-mode strategy of record so no future agent deletes the 8bpp present path or collapses the OS to truecolor-only (an anachronism vs ADR-0004 OD-2). Strategy: the desktop is an INDEXED-8 offscreen (OD-2 unchanged) presented through the ONE surface conversion site (D-2) to ONE of two co-canonical LFB depths (PRD Sec 5 line 105: 640x480 x8/x32). Boot request priority: (1) 640x480x32 preferred (QEMU dev-loop + real hw; reproduces the exact frame today), (2) 640x480x24, (3) 640x480x8 == VESA 0x101 (period-authentic SVGA: Cirrus/ET4000), (4) mode-0x13 320x200x8 fail-loud floor. NOT QEMU-pinned: cross-emulator agreement is host-model-PER-MODE (AM-6, FROZEN); Bochs is serial-only by design (no QMP screendump). Period-authenticity (indexed-8 on real SVGA via 86Box) carried to M4 by FO-D/q0gy + initech-zqy3-equiv. Prefer a short ADR-0004 amendment; a worklog shard is acceptable.
notes: Committee ruling re30.3-chair. D-DISSENT-1 anticipated a 32bpp-only LFB as the trigger to re-open the depth trade-off informedly; QEMU at 32bpp is exactly that, resolved as dual-depth-with-priority (not truecolor-only, not 8bpp-only).

title: Record FLAIR display-mode strategy of record (truecolor-first priority; 8bpp/0x101 period-authentic tier) as an ADR-0004 amendment or worklog note
description: RULING re30.3-chair (2026-06-21), RULING A: the FLAIR desktop display-mode STRATEGY OF RECORD is an indexed-8 OFFSCREEN (ADR-0004 OD-2, unchanged) presented to ONE of two accepted LFB depths via the single surface conversion site (D-2). Canonical boot display-mode PRIORITY the OS requests: (1) 640x480x32 LFB [truecolor, preferred -- QEMU dev-loop + accepted on real HW], (2) 640x480x24 LFB, (3) 640x480x8 LFB == VESA 0x101 [PERIOD-AUTHENTIC SVGA Cirrus/ET4000; currently NOT requested -- bead initech-zqy3], (4) mode-0x13 320x200x8 = fail-loud floor the FLAIR guard rejects. BOTH x8 and x32 are canonical per PRD Sec 5 line 105 ('640x480 ... x8/x32'); truecolor is preferred for the dev-loop, indexed-8 is the period-faithful target carried to M4 by 86Box (q0gy). This is NOT a depth-policy change to OD-2 (offscreen stays indexed-8); it records the LFB-target priority and resolves D-DISSENT-1's 'what if a 32bpp-only LFB mode' friction in the truecolor-fallback direction WITHOUT abandoning the 8bpp authentic path. Capture as a short ADR-0004 amendment (preferred) or a worklog shard so a future agent does not (a) delete the 8bpp present as 'dead' or (b) silently make the OS truecolor-only. Cross-emulator agreement stays host-model-PER-MODE (AM-6, FROZEN; do not pin to QEMU).

title: Kernel-growth runway exhausted at PROGRAM_BASE=0x40000 -- next growth needs high-half / extended-memory relocation
description: re30.2 (Step A) performed the SECOND +0x8000 whole-map shift (0x38000->0x40000), spending the LAST of the conventional free gap. The kernel stack region is now [0x90000,0xA0000), ESP=0x9FFFC, butting the 0xA0000 VGA / mode-0x13 LFB fallback aperture with ZERO gap (disjoint: 0x9FFFC < 0xA0000, but no slack). This is the MAXIMUM PROGRAM_BASE raise possible under the conventional-memory (<640 KiB) scheme: a third +0x8000 shift would push the kernel stack top into/over 0xA0000. The kernel.ld B0 ASSERT and memory_map.h now say so. CONSEQUENCE: any future kernel-resident growth past the current ~17 KiB shell-kernel headroom (FLAIR call sites in re30.3+, the verb layer, more Managers, MILTON redirection) cannot be absorbed by another raise. The mitigation when this binds: relocate the kernel and/or its stack to a high-half / extended-memory layout (the FLAIR heap already lives at 0x100000+; ADR-0001 makes >1 MiB directly addressable in flat 32-bit). This needs an ADR/committee decision (kernel link base, stack placement, stage2 load path, the self-host fixpoint determinism implications). NOT urgent now (re30.2 fits with ~17 KiB to spare) -- file-and-watch. Trip condition: the kernel.ld ASSERT(_kernel_end < 0x40000) fires, OR the KERNEL_SECTORS window can no longer grow within IMG_SECTORS.
notes: Surfaced during initech-re30.2 (FLAIR Manager link). Cite: spec/memory_map.h gap-proof (free gap = 0xA0000..0xA0000 = 0); os/milton/kernel.ld B0 ASSERT comment; os/milton/kstart.asm ESP 0x9FFFC. Precedent raises: initech-5pe (0x20000->0x30000), initech-o0td (0x30000->0x38000), initech-re30.2 (0x38000->0x40000 = last).

title: Loader: child PSP inherits the parent JFT (enables external EXEC output redirect)
description: psp_build (os/milton/psp.c) HARD-RESETS the child JFT standard handles to CON (jft[0]=0 stdin, jft[1]=1 stdout, jft[2]=1 stderr, jft[3]=2 aux, jft[4]=3 prn) on every EXEC, ignoring the parent's open/redirected handles. Authentic DOS has the child INHERIT the parent's JFT (the AH=4Bh child gets a copy of the parent's handle table). RESULT: COMMAND.COM's > / >> redirect (hsct OUTPUT increment) works for BUILTINS (they run in the shell's own PSP with the DUP2'd handle 1 -> file) but NOT for external .COM EXEC children (their handle 1 is reset to CON). Empirically confirmed: GREET.COM> GOUT.TXT prints GREETINGS to SCREEN and leaves GOUT.TXT empty. FIX: thread the parent JFT (handles 0..4, or all 20) into psp_build via a params field and copy them into the child PSP when a parent PSP exists (g_cur_psp at EXEC time). Oracle: a child-inherits-parent-JFT property test + an emu gate mirroring test-hsct-redir but EXEC'ing GREET.COM> file and asserting GREETINGS appears via TYPE (from the file), not on screen during the EXEC. Touch: psp.c/psp.h/loader.c (the loader/PSP layer hsct deliberately did NOT touch). Ref: ADR-0003 DEC-06; MS-DOS 3.3 Tech Ref (handle inheritance on EXEC); the hsct OUTPUT increment driver already brackets EXEC correctly -- zero command.c change needed once this lands.

title: Shell: pipe operator (|) in COMMAND.COM via temp-file pipe
description: Implement the | pipe operator, deferred from the hsct OUTPUT increment (which delivered > and >>). Authentic DOS implements a|b as a temp-file pipe: run a with stdout redirected to a temp file (reuse run_with_redirect machinery), then run b with stdin redirected from that temp file (depends on the < input-redirect bead), then delete the temp. Extend cmd_redir_parse to split on | (deferred in the OUTPUT increment). Needs the < stdin path first. Enables the MORE/SORT/FIND filter pipelines (initech-m0dc). Mirror the driver + parse oracle + an emu gate (DIR | SORT). Ref: PRD 6.1; MS-DOS 3.3 Tech Ref Ch.6.

title: Shell: INPUT redirection (< stdin) in COMMAND.COM
description: Implement < input redirection, the deferred sibling of the OUTPUT increment (hsct delivered > and >>). Open the target (AH=3Dh read), dos_dup(0) save stdin, dos_dup2(file,0) repoint handle 0, dispatch clean cmd, restore handle 0 on every path. Extend cmd_redir_parse to capture a < target (OUTPUT increment left < unparsed). Needs builtins/EXEC children to READ stdin via handle 0 (AH=3Fh handle 0). Mirror run_with_redirect + test_redir_parse oracle + an emu gate (SORT/MORE < FILE once m0dc filters land). Ref: PRD 6.1; MS-DOS 3.3 Tech Ref Ch.6.

title: Route INT 21h AH=09h/02h/06h CON output through the redirectable stdout handle 1 (DOS-authentic; prereq for hsct redirection)
description: Today do_puts (AH=09h) and do_conout (AH=02h) write DIRECTLY via con_putc, bypassing the JFT handle 1 -- so they are NOT redirectable by DUP2(file,1). Real DOS routes AH=02h/09h to STDOUT (handle 1), so they ARE redirectable. Make do_puts/do_conout (and the AH=06h direct-console-output) emit byte-by-byte through the handle-1 write path (do_write(EBX=1)) so a DUP2(file,1) redirect (hsct) catches their output, while an un-redirected handle 1 still reaches CON (-> con_putc -> the p96i ANSI hook + serial). CAREFUL: this is the most load-bearing CON path (prompt/diagnostics/banner) + it interacts with p96i's con_putc ANSI hook + the g_sink; full emu suite + ANSI re-verify required. Likely needs the kernel-window headroom fix first (no room for added code).
notes: Surfaced WL-0045 (hsct attempt). The real blocker for 'echo > file' to redirect.
close_reason: DELIVERED. AH=09h/02h/06h CON output now routes through the redirectable STDOUT handle 1 (sft_write core extracted from do_write; stdout_emit with no-PSP con_putc fallback for the banner). Un-redirected output byte-identical; DUP2(file,1) now redirects. New mutation-proven test-redir (25/0). 242 host + 39 emu + Bochs GREEN. Commit 7de96e6, WL-0047. Unblocks hsct.

title: Wire the device chain (devices.c) into INT 21h OPEN-by-name + sysinit DEVICE= install
description: 509.7-CORE landed the device-header chain module os/milton/devices.{c,h} (NUL/CON/AUX/PRN/CLOCK$ + request-packet protocol; host gate test-devices 150 checks). WIRING is the deferred half: (1) int21 AH=3Dh OPEN matches a device name via devices_find -> the SFT entry gets a DEV bit + device_header_t ptr, and READ/WRITE route to devices_request instead of the FAT path; (2) sysinit installs the chain (g_chain_head); (3) CLOCK$ gets an rtc.c bridge (dev_rtc_bridge.c, days-since-1980 conversion, mutation-proven separately); (4) DEVICE= in CONFIG.SYS loads an installable driver. SERIAL on int21.c. Emu gate: OPEN CON/NUL/PRN from the shell + verify behavior. INT 2Fh redirector stays OUT (amendment-gated, separate epic).
notes: Surfaced WL-0041 Wave 2 (509.7-core). The device chain module is the load-bearing logic; this is the int21/sysinit integration.
close_reason: Device chain wired into INT 21h: AH=3Dh OPEN-by-name (CON/NUL/PRN/AUX/CLOCK$) -> devices.c; AH=3Fh/40h READ/WRITE routing; sysinit installs the chain + records DEVICE=ANSI.SYS as g_ansi_enabled (for p96i). Legacy direct-CON output PRESERVED byte-for-byte (one CON path). Host gate test-devwire (40 checks, mutant); full emu suite (39 gates) GREEN -- universal CON path preserved (WL-0042, 881256a). In-emu OPEN-by-name device gate filed as a follow-up.

title: Wire ANSI.SYS (ansi.c FSM) into the CON output path + CONFIG.SYS DEVICE=ANSI.SYS gate
description: x3mh landed the PURE ANSI.SYS escape-sequence FSM (ansi.c/ansi.h + test-ansi, 94 checks + 2 mutants). WIRING is the deferred half: hook ansi_feed into the INT 21h CON output path (AH=02h/06h/09h/40h-to-CON in int21.c, via console.c) behind a g_ansi_enabled flag set when sysinit/config_sys sees DEVICE=ANSI.SYS in CONFIG.SYS. Apply MOVE_CURSOR/ERASE/SET_ATTR actions to the console; implement the DSR (ESC[6n) response back through the kbd input queue. SERIAL on int21.c/console.c (do not parallel with other int21 lanes). Needs an emu gate: a .COM that emits ESC[ sequences -> assert color/cursor effect on a screendump.
notes: Surfaced WL-0039 Wave 1 (x3mh pure module done). The pure FSM is the load-bearing logic; this is the integration.
close_reason: ANSI.SYS FSM wired into the live CON output path: con_putc -> ansi_feed (gated by DEVICE=ANSI.SYS) via a console-ops vtable + enable-gate seam; console gained attr/cursor/erase + CGA palette; kmain binds adapters (put_char fans to console+serial). Escape-free output pixel-identical. Host gate test-ansi-wire (15 checks, mutant) + test-console 71->91; full emu suite (39 gates) + Bochs GREEN -- CON path preserved (WL-0043). DSR (ESC[6n) deferred (filed).

title: In-emulator KEEP-survival gate: prove AH=31h resident block survives a real shell EXEC
description: bo40 landed AH=31h KEEP + the mcb_keep_resident model, proven by the host oracle (test-keep, 43 checks + mutant) and covered for regression by the emu EXEC gates. A DEDICATED in-emulator proof that a KEEP'd block SURVIVES through the real shell-EXEC flow is deferred: the bo40 agent flagged that under int21_mcb_bind_program (the disjoint-arena bind active during a normal .COM EXEC) KEEP degrades to a plain terminate, so a naive KEEP.COM->ALLOC.COM emu scenario may not exercise the resident path. Needs: investigate which MCB arena model is live during a shell EXEC; if disjoint, decide whether KEEP should keep the program's allocated block; then build KEEP.COM + ALLOC probe + serial assertion (ALLOC seg above kept region, AH=4Dh rc=07 type=03) + a -DMCB_MUTATE_KEEP_NO_RESIDENT mutant kernel.
notes: Surfaced WL-0039 Wave 1 (bo40 grading). Host oracle is authoritative (Law 2); this is the in-emulator end-to-end deepening.

title: MZ .EXE: emu gate -- boot + EXEC a REAL InitechMZ .EXE file, assert a relocated-reference serial marker (dtw.2-emu)
description:
STRETCH split out of initech-wczy (dtw.2). The loader MZ integration is HOST-proven (test_mzload.c drives loader_prepare_mz on hand-authored in-memory InitechMZ images: parse, content dispatch, flat reloc (trailer + header-resident tables), entry, arena, e_minalloc teeth, foreign fail-loud, .COM-path-untouched; mutation LOADER_MUTATE_MZ_NO_RELOC bites). NOT YET emu-proven end-to-end.

Deliver (DEC-08a.4 tri-emu oracle, Rule 5):
1. A FACTORY tool (C, harness/) that wraps a flat 32-bit InitechOS load module + a uint32 flat reloc table into a real InitechMZ container file: real MZ header, e_res[0]=MZ_INITECH_TAG (0x4943), e_cparhdr/e_cp/e_cblp computed, reloc table at e_lfarlc. Deterministic (Rule 11).
2. A test program whose correctness depends on a RELOCATED absolute reference (e.g. a pointer to a string/data dword that is only correct after +PROGRAM_IMAGE), emitting a serial marker iff the relocation resolved at runtime.
3. Place that .EXE on the FAT boot image; boot, EXEC it (AH=4Bh by name through the real load_program_from_fat MZ path), assert the serial marker on QEMU AND Bochs (period-authenticity, Rule 5). Also assert a FOREIGN (untagged) MZ on the image PANICS 'PANIC foreign-mz' and does NOT run (DEC-08a.5 emu-side).
4. Makefile: add the fixture target + test-mzload-emu gate; add to the gate vector.

Ref: ADR-0003 DEC-08a (esp .4 dispatch, .5 fail-loud); os/milton/loader.c load_program_mz_in_place / loader_panic_foreign_mz; os/milton/mz.c; harness/ emu drivers. Blocks: nothing (host path is complete and gated).
close_reason: InitechMZ .EXE RUNS in-emulator: test-mzexec (reloc resolves at runtime + foreign-MZ fail-loud panic per DEC-08a.5) + test-mzexec-mutant (no-reloc image stays silent) green on QEMU. mzlink factory tool + fixture + 2 kernel variants + 3 FAT disks wired into TEST_EMU_GATES.

title: MZ .EXE: loader.c .COM/MZ dispatch + reloc apply + fail-loud tag gate + EXEC integration (DEC-08a, dtw.2)
description: Per ADR-0003 DEC-08a. Integrate the mz unit into loader.c: content-based 'MZ' detection, place load module at PROGRAM_IMAGE (skip e_cparhdr), apply flat relocs, honor e_minalloc/maxalloc against the MCB arena (fail-loud function 08h), entry/stack contract, PANIC on an untagged foreign 16-bit MZ. title: Workflow worktree-isolation spawns from stale 'main' (e56695a/WL-0019), not the active branch tip
description: Observed in the forward-tranche-impl-parallel workflow (this session): isolation:'worktree' agents created worktrees branched from 'main' (pinned at e56695a, WL-0019) instead of command-com-default's tip (259f749, post-z01/dao/WL-0025-27). The d27i agent caught it (its 'z01 landed' premise was false on the base), git-reset its branch onto the tip, and implemented correctly; 80k and x8fs were ALSO on the stale base but their edits sit in regions unchanged since e56695a, so they cherry-pick cleanly (e56695a is a linear ancestor of the tip). RISK: a future worktree agent on the stale base could silently re-implement already-landed work or fake-green against a stale oracle. MITIGATIONS: (a) keep 'main' fast-forwarded to the active branch at session close (it has drifted since WL-0019 -- the HANDOFF still says 'main == HEAD' which is stale); OR (b) instruct worktree agents to 'git reset --hard <tip-sha>' as step 0; OR (c) the orchestrator passes the integration tip SHA and verifiers reset onto it. Also: 3 orphaned worktrees from prior sessions (wf_212dd6d3, wf_5addeed0, wf_c2fdeabe) linger at e56695a -- prune.
design: Standing mitigation for the rest of THIS session: run the serial CON chain (er3h->4tw) in the MAIN tree (serial, no race), and have adversarial verifiers 'git reset --hard' onto the integrated tip in their worktree before running mutations. Revisit keeping main synced as a release-hygiene step.

Distinct from 17n (the oracle logic) because the corpus + minting driver are a standalone factory deliverable that unblocks all SAMIR verification. Spec'd in docs/research/dbase-ground-truth.md sec 7. P2: infra gating everything downstream. Mutation-prove every minted golden (Rule 6).

title: FAT16 initrd/tarfs discovery: does Turbo Initech / InitechBase need a baked embedded FS?
description: Deferred from z01 (operator-approved). PRD 5 names a 'baked initrd/tarfs' alongside the FAT16 HDD as a storage mechanism. z01 scoped to FAT16 disk read-only and deferred the initrd question. Resolve: is a pre-mounted, baked, read-only embedded filesystem REQUIRED for Turbo Initech bootstrap (the compiler's own standard library/units shipped read-only at build time) or InitechBase, OR is the single mounted FAT volume sufficient + initrd is an S3 'real-iron' stretch? This gates the TPS (ADR-0007) file-I/O design. Output: an architecture note / ADR input, not necessarily code.

Per Rule 8 + the bsy epic 'no new funcs without amendment', this requires a ratified ADR-0003 amendment. close_reason: Ratified as DEC-16 (OEA-ADR-0003-A4) 2026-06-15, operator as-drafted (WL-0027, 259f749). AH=33h admitted to Appendix A; register row + handler land atomically with er3h/4tw per Rule 8. Unblocks 4tw/er3h.

title: CREATNEW (AH=5Bh) oracle asserts CF/AX signal, not FS effect; add side-effect + end-to-end FAT12 assertions
description: Adversarial review of initech-kji0 found the CREATNEW host oracle (test_fileio.c) asserts only CF/AX on collision/fresh paths, never the filesystem EFFECT: on collision it never reads the existing file back to prove no-truncate, never checks no SFT/JFT slot churned; on fresh it never proves a file materialized. The mock create() hardcodes idx=3 so it cannot model truncation -- a truncate/churn-before-existence-check refactor would stay GREEN. There is also no end-to-end FAT12 AH=5Bh oracle (real fat_create subdir-create + dir-scoped fat_open probe never exercised). Shipped code is correct; the oracle is thin. Add side-effect assertions + a FAT12 CREATNEW differential.
close_reason: Landed + adversarially verified in WL-0026 (90 host + 27 emu green). Oracle-hardened (cnvp completeness reorder, 5o6o real MUTANT 8, nmpo test-nmpo-mutant). P3 follow-ups split out: fgdz/jplu/aaan/815i.

title: ADR-0003/DEC-07 amendment: MBR-partition on-disk FS contract + sector-0 detection heuristic
description: Operator deferred kzfs (MBR partition parse) on 2026-06-15: it broadens the DEC-07 superfloppy on-disk contract to partitioned volumes and forces a sector-0 detection-heuristic choice (drive-number-driven vs byte-sniff) plus a hidden_sectors-vs-partition-table authority decision (fail-loud on mismatch). Ratify a DEC-07 amendment settling: (1) detection heuristic (affects fat12_mount signature), (2) which start-LBA authority wins, (3) whether a new FAT12_ERR_PARTITION code is needed. Then unblock kzfs. Downstream consumers: FAT16-HDD, multi-volume A:/B:/C:, FORMAT, FDISK.
close_reason: Ratified as DEC-07a (OEA-ADR-0003-A5) 2026-06-15, operator as-drafted (WL-0027, 259f749). Partition contract: Fork1 require-match-fail-loud (provisional/86Box), Fork2 mount-layer base_lba, Fork3 BIOS-first+byte-sniff. Unblocks kzfs; spec/code lands with kzfs.

title: Nested MKDIR/RMDIR (non-root parent): wire AH=39h/3Ah for a subdir parent
description: Follow-up split out of initech-zs24 Landing 1 (subdir file WRITE). zs24 L1 generalized the fat12 scan/write-dirent/grow primitives to be parent-directory-aware and lifted the root-only guard on fat12_create/unlink/write_partial + the backend fat_create/fat_unlink/fat_write_at. fat12_mkdir/fat12_rmdir still REJECT a non-root parent_dir_start (FAT12_ERR_NOT_FOUND), and the backend fat_mkdir/fat_rmdir still gate dir_start!=0 -> 0x0003. Nested MD/RD does NOT fall out trivially: fat12_mkdir writes the new dir's own 8.3 entry into the PARENT via the root-only fat12_write_dirent(free_slot) and scans the parent via fat12_scan_root; both must move to the new parent-aware fat12_scan_dir + fat12_write_dirent_in_dir, and the parent-full case must GROW the parent subdir (fat12_grow_dir, already available). fat12_rmdir's empty-check is already subdir-capable (uses fat12_read_dir on the target). ORACLE: a nested '\SUB\NEWDIR' differential vs 'mmd ::SUB/NEWDIR' (mirror test-fat12-mkdir) + the do_mkdir/do_rmdir int21 path, plus a per-primitive mutant. Pointers: os/milton/fat12.c fat12_mkdir/fat12_rmdir parent_dir_start guards; os/milton/fileio_fat.c fat_mkdir/fat_rmdir dir_start!=0 guards; os/milton/int21.c do_mkdir/do_rmdir.
notes: Adversarial verify (2026-06-15) found a REAL P2 blocker: fat12_mkdir leaks the grown parent cluster on 3 post-grow mid-op failures (NO_SPACE/set_entry-EOC/flush) -- the rollback (fat12_shrink_dir_tail) is not called before those returns. Commit-msg claim 'mirrors fat12_create / neither leaks' was false. FIXING before close: add the 3 rollback calls + a geometry-driven NO_SPACE fault-injection test that bites. Coverage follow-ups filed separately.
close_reason: Nested MKDIR/RMDIR (non-root parent): implemented + oracle-green + 5 mutants RED (adff903); adversarial review FOUND + FIXED a post-grow parent-cluster leak (de4b538) with a biting NO_SPACE rollback gate; re-verified make clean && make test 75 host + 26 emu. Follow-up initech-lpf3 filed.

title: Spec-data: document AH=39h/3Ah/3Bh/47h in int21h_calling_convention.json
description: Lens-2 finding during ut6d verification. The directory handlers (do_mkdir/do_rmdir/do_chdir/do_getcwd, landed by u6wa/mzxa) and ut6d's shell wrappers cite spec/int21h_calling_convention.json as the per-register ABI authority, but that locked spec's functions[] only documents the console subset (00/02/09/30/40/4Ch). Add rows for 39h (in:EDX path; out:CF+AX), 3Ah, 3Bh, and 47h (in:DL drive + ESI buf; out:CF+AX) so the cited locked spec pins what the wrappers implement (Law 8). test-spec[2/5] requires each cc AH to exist in int21h_register.json (they do). Predates ut6d.

title: INT 21h subdir EXEC + subdir WRITE (CREAT/UNLINK/WRITE inside a subdir)
description:
Follow-up split out of initech-mzxa (ti8 Layer 2). mzxa wired the READ-side subdir resolution into the INT 21h file backend: OPEN/READ + FINDFIRST/NEXT now resolve and enumerate \SUB\FILE paths via fat12_resolve_path/fat12_read_dir (real-backend integration green). TWO deferred pieces, both because the underlying fat12 write/load primitives are ROOT-only:

(1) do_exec (AH=4Bh) stays ROOT-ONLY (mzxa decision 3): the EXEC source feeds the loader program-load path (load_program_from_fat -> load_program), which locates the .COM by a ROOT-dir 8.3 name. Threading a resolved containing-directory cluster into the loader is a riskier loader-internal change; mzxa kept do_exec rejecting subdir paths (path_has_subdir_or_drive at int21.c do_exec). This bead: let EXEC load a program from a subdirectory by resolving the source path to its dir_entry and feeding the located entry's own start_cluster to the loader.

(2) CREATE/UNLINK/WRITE inside a subdirectory: fileio_fat.c fat_create/fat_unlink return 0x0003 for a non-root dir_start_cluster (fat12_create/fat12_unlink/fat12_write_partial work over the ROOT dir + a root-dir slot for write-back). The mzxa file backend threads dir_start through create/unlink additively (param present, root==0 byte-identical), but the WRITE primitives must become subdir-aware (allocate a dir slot in a subdir cluster chain, write-back into it). This is the FAT WRITE-side subdir work (sibling of initech-509.11).

GROUNDED POINTERS (mzxa session): fileio_fat.c fat_create/fat_unlink non-root guards; int21.c do_exec path_has_subdir_or_drive; the resolve seam (int21_file_backend_t.resolve + resolve_dir_path). ORACLE: extend test_fileio_subdir.c (real backend) with subdir CREAT/WRITE/UNLINK round-trips + a subdir EXEC over a nested data disk.
close_reason: Both landings done: L1 (610c656) subdir file CREATE/WRITE/UNLINK + grow infra (5 mutants); L2 subdir EXEC (do_exec reuses do_open resolve seam; loader dir_start branch, root byte-identical; test-zs24-exec absolute+CWD-relative, 2 mutants). Full make test green (70 host+26 emu); adversarially verified both landings. Follow-ups: m0bp (nested MKDIR/RMDIR), dir-attr-guard mutation-proof, free-cluster-hint quirk.

title: test-fb-agree: host dual-target render (32bpp + 8bpp-palette) + per-emulator serial content-digest vs HOST model for its own mode
description: test-fb-agree oracle: render both host targets (32bpp + 8bpp-palette) and compare each emulator's serial content-digest against the HOST model for that emulator's own mode. This is NOT a cross-emulator byte-CRC (impossible: 640x480x32 vs 320x200x8); it routes around Bochs lacking a mode-0x13 screendump.

description: M4 Manager set: Window, Menu, Control, and Dialog Managers, two stacked menu bars, the modal FILE COPY layer, and the desktop shell. Builds ONLY on a green region engine. The existing Manager beads initech-9qf/n3e/8h9/qcc/859 live under this umbrella.
