Visually audited (build/desktop_scene.ppm). Modal blends due to gray-white (content-white follow-up).

title: ti8 Layer 2: thread resolved subdir through the INT 21h file backend (vtable cross-cut + g_cwd)
description:
Core-tier cross-cut (Rule 7) split out of initech-ti8 after grounding (workflow wf_fc0ea9c9-a7a, this session). ti8 Layer 1 (fat12.c: fat12_dir_t + fat12_read_dir + fat12_resolve_path, READ-side) is additive and self-contained, oracle-gated at the fat12 layer. THIS bead is Layer 2: actually wire subdir-path resolution into the INT 21h DOS API so OPEN/FINDFIRST/etc. resolve \SUB\FILE paths.

WHY SEPARATE: the int21_file_backend_t vtable (int21.h:149-220, VERIFIED) is purely root-relative today (open()/create()/dir_entry()/unlink() take only name83 / a flat index). Threading a resolved directory (a uint16 start_cluster, 0=root, is the lowest-coupling thread) is a multi-file change: int21.h + fileio_fat.c (real backend, fat_open@81/fat_dir_entry@147) + THREE host mocks in lockstep (test_fileio.c@319, test_int21.c@103, test_int21_edge.c) or nothing compiles.

GROUNDED SCOPE (all line numbers VERIFIED this session, the brief's were stale):
- 5 path_has_subdir_or_drive() rejection sites to replace with resolve_path: do_open@858, do_creat@931, do_unlink@995, do_findfirst@1405, do_exec@1476. Preserve AX=0x0003 PATH_NOT_FOUND on a bad/non-dir component (keeps do_geterr AH=59h class/action/locus green) and preserve the INT21_PATH_SCAN_MAX overlength fail-loud + the INT21_MUTATE_PATHSCAN_NOBOUND mutation oracle.
- g_cwd: a file-static in int21.c near the globals block (~L52), NOT a psp_t field (psp_t is LOCKED spec with sizeof==256 _Static_assert). Reset to root on program launch (loader.c:287 beside int21_set_psp/int21_mcb_reset) and on do_terminate (int21.c:1721); save/restore around the 5 kmain.c int21_set_psp(&g_kernel_psp) sites (423/462/837/1048/1086) so kernel-context CWD does not leak a child's dir. Wire do_getcwd (int21.c:1895, currently hard-codes root buf[0]=0) to report g_cwd.
- DEPENDS ON ti8 (Layer 1: fat12_resolve_path + fat12_dir_t must exist first).

OPEN DECISIONS (operator/committee): (a) do_exec subdir scope -- resolve a subdir-qualified EXEC target (bigger blast radius: loader load_program_from_fat must accept a non-root start-cluster) or keep EXEC root-only and leave its site rejecting? (b) CWD inheritance: real DOS gives a child its own inherited copy; simplest ti8 model is reset-to-root on launch -- confirm acceptable. (c) when AH=56h RENAME (gnrc) lands it must also use resolve_path.

ORACLE: extend test_int21/test_fileio with resolved-path open/findfirst cases (mocks model a tiny nested namespace); mutation points INT21_MUTATE_RESOLVE_NODRIVE / INT21_MUTATE_RESOLVE_FINALDIR. Mutation-prove.
notes: Source of grounding: workflow wf_fc0ea9c9-a7a maps (Layer-2 + oracle agents). Conflict resolved on main thread: int21.c:1476 is do_exec (AH=4Bh), NOT rename.

title: make test self-completion: spec/assets/preview.webp gates the whole suite on clean checkouts
description: spec/assets/preview.webp is gitignored (.gitignore:39) + untracked with no generation rule, so 'make test' aborts at test-assets on a clean checkout BEFORE the post-assets unit mutants and the entire test-emu suite run -- a reviewer without the local fixture won't see those gates execute (surfaced while verifying initech-509.6). Fix: commit a tiny deterministic fixture OR add a Makefile rule that generates a placeholder asset deterministically (Rule 11), so 'make test' self-completes. Do NOT commit the copyrighted reference frame still.
close_reason: DONE + merged (d4e7f74). test-assets now skips gracefully+loudly when the local-only gitignored frame fixture is absent (so make test self-completes on a clean checkout) and runs the FULL palette-honesty check when present. Verified both paths on the main thread; did NOT fake the check (Stop-condition respected).

title: Shell: environment store + SET built-in + EXEC env inheritance
description: No environment exists: the env block is a fixed 2-byte empty stub (loader.c:256, ENV_BLOCK 0x5F000) and EXEC passes env_block=0 (command.c:433). Build a real master environment (PATH/COMSPEC/PROMPT vars), a SET built-in (set/list/clear), and per-process env copy passed through exec_param_block_t.env_block to the child PSP. Underpins PROMPT, PATH, and batch %VAR%.
design:
TRANCHE-E design (grounded WL-0037+ audit). Three increments, each RED->GREEN->mutation-proven->make test, SERIAL on shared files (Rule 3):
INC-1 (no shared-file risk): NEW pure-logic module os/milton/env.c + env.h -- a freestanding master environment store (fixed-capacity arena, NO libc): env_set(name,val) (upsert, upcases name per DOS), env_get, env_unset (NAME= clears), env_count/iter, and env_serialize(buf,cap)-> DOS-format block = sequence of ASCIIZ 'NAME=VALUE\0' strings + a final extra '\0' (empty env = lone '\0\0'). Host oracle test_env.c (HOSTED, like cmd_* TU). Seed PATH/COMSPEC/PROMPT.
INC-2 (command.c): NEW CMD_SET in command.h enum + cmd_classify table row; cmd_set handler: 'SET' lists 'NAME=VALUE' lines, 'SET NAME=VALUE' upserts, 'SET NAME=' clears. Wire the global master env into kmain shell loop. test via test_command + an emu DIR-style leg later.
INC-3 (loader.c): on EXEC, env_serialize the master env into ENV_BLOCK (0x5F000) and pass env_linear=ENV_BLOCK (not 0) so child PSP env_seg points at the real block (psp_build already does flat_to_fake_paragraph). Per-process: child inherits a COPY (already physically separate region). Mutation: drop the serialize -> child sees empty env -> oracle RED.
Ground truth: DOS env block format = ASCIIZ NAME=VALUE strings, double-NUL terminated (Ralf Brown INT 21h/AH=4Bh EPB + PSP:2Ch). DOS 3.0+ trailing word-count=1 + progpath is OPTIONAL nicety, deferred. Unblocks dibc(PROMPT)/atf(PATH)/xw1(batch %VAR%).
close_reason: Tranche E complete: env store (inc-1) + SET built-in (inc-2) + EXEC env inheritance (inc-3), all RED->GREEN, mutation-proven, 220 host gates + test-program emu EXEC green.

title: [EPIC] Core DOS external utilities (FORMAT/FDISK/CHKDSK/SYS/filters/...)
description: The external utility layer: standalone .COM/.EXE programs that ship with the OS and call the kernel (distinct from COMMAND.COM internal commands). REQUIRED-BEFORE-GUI/dBASE subset: FORMAT, CHKDSK, SYS, DISKCOPY, MORE/SORT/FIND, ATTRIB, LABEL, EDLIN/DEBUG. Each is gated on the kernel services it needs (absolute disk, partitions, subdirs, CHMOD, redirection). Most are unrunnable until k-cert lands -- hence the epic depends on the kernel-complete certificate. Authored in C as flat .COM-equivalents (MZ .EXE deferred, dtw).

title: SYS.COM: transfer IO.SYS/INITDOS.SYS + boot sector
description: Make a formatted volume bootable: write the boot sector and copy the two system files as the first root entries (H/S/R). Depends on absolute disk (k-absdisk) and the two-file kernel split (kod).

title: INT 21h AH=31h KEEP (TSR) + resident-block memory model
description: Listed (int21.c:265) but undispatched; do_terminate notes 'no TSR' (int21.c:1514). Needs a resident-memory model: on KEEP, mark the program's MCB block(s) as owned-resident (not reclaimed) and return AL as exit code. Depends on the MCB arena being wired into INT 21h (509.6). Also enables a real AH=4Dh termination-type and the InDOS flag exposure (AH=34h) for resident code.
close_reason: AH=31h KEEP dispatched + mcb_keep_resident model (shrink + mark MCB_OWNER_SYSTEM; ALLOC avoids the kept region; terminate reclaim skips it) + AH=4Dh exit-type-3, routed through the existing do_terminate path. Host gate test-keep (43 checks + mutant) green; emu EXEC/terminate regression 8/8 PASS (WL-0040, 9a5fea3). In-emulator KEEP-survival deepening tracked as initech-2cn6; AH=34h InDOS companion filed.

title: Shell: PROMPT built-in + $P$G template engine
description: The prompt is a hardcoded literal 'A:\>' (command.c:676); there is no PROMPT command and no $-metacharacter engine. Implement a real PROMPT string renderer ($P cwd, $G >, $T time, $D date, $N drive, $$, $_) sourced from the PROMPT env var (needs s-env).

title: FORMAT.COM: write BPB/FAT/root, /S system transfer
description: Low-level volume format: lay down boot sector + BPB, empty FATs, root dir; /S copies the system files (with u-sys). Needs absolute disk write (k-absdisk) and partition awareness (k-mbr).

title: INT 21h AH=33h CTRL-BREAK state + CONFIG BREAK= + BREAK built-in
description: AH=33h (get/set BREAK flag) is absent (not even recognized -> 'unknown AH'). Add the BREAK on/off state, honor it from CONFIG.SYS BREAK= and a BREAK shell built-in, and gate the ^C/INT 23h check (4tw) on it. Small, but part of authentic DOS break semantics.
notes: POST-CLOSE FIX (commit 8d3f287, from adversarial review): do_break wrote AL=normalized on AL=01h SET, violating DEC-16 3.2 ('no register other than the saved flag state changes' on SET). Fixed: SET writes no output register; oracle [er3h.2] strengthened to assert AL-unchanged; new M7 mutant (SET_WRITES_AL) proves it; calling_convention.json AH=33h stanza corrected. Bead remains closed (feature complete; this was a contract-detail correction).
close_reason: Landed (commit 7c21b0b): AH=33h Get/Set CTRL-BREAK per ratified DEC-16 -- g_break_flag (default ON), int21_set/get_break_flag seam, AH=33h dispatch (GET/SET/DL-normalize/out-of-scope-AL fail-loud), CONFIG.SYS BREAK=ON|OFF -> SYSINIT, BREAK [ON|OFF] shell built-in. Spec edits (Rule 8, atomic per DEC-16): AH=33h row in int21h_register.json + calling-convention stanza + test-spec strengthened. test-er3h + 3 mutants (no-dispatch/DL-raw/default-OFF) all RED. Host gates 99->101. Full gate 103 host + 27 emu GREEN (incl 4tw); Bochs boot leg PASS.

title: Shell built-in: REN/RENAME
description: REN built-in absent; sits directly on the kernel AH=56h RENAME (k-rename56). Add wildcard rename (REN *.TXT *.BAK) once 80k is in.
close_reason: REN/RENAME shell built-in done (AH=56h same-dir); cross-dir MOVE is kernel bead ycb3. 120-check test-command + no-ren mutant.

title: Shell built-ins: COPY and DEL/ERASE (wildcard-aware)
description: COPY (file->file, file->dir, CON->file) and DEL/ERASE are absent. AH=41h UNLINK exists in the kernel but no shell verb. Build on the wildcard engine (80k) so COPY *.TXT and DEL *.BAK iterate. COPY uses OPEN/READ/WRITE/CLOSE; DEL uses UNLINK.
close_reason: COPY (single-file) + DEL/ERASE (plain + wildcard) done. Wildcard COPY + DEL *.* confirm prompt deferred -> follow-up bead.

title: Shell: I/O redirection (> >> <) and pipes (|)
description: The shell never scans for redirection/pipe operators (the only '>' in command.c is the prompt literal). Implement >, >>, < by re-pointing child stdin/stdout via DUP2 (AH=46h already exists) around EXEC, and | as a temp-file pipe between two children. Enables the MORE/SORT/FIND filter utilities.
notes: ATTEMPTED + REVERTED (WL-0045): the pure redirect PARSER (cmd_redir_parse: > >> < | scan, target extraction, clean-command) + the DUP2-around-dispatch DRIVER + temp-file pipe were built and host-proven (test_command 203 checks, 2 mutants). BUT the feature is FUNCTIONALLY INCOMPLETE and was NOT committed (Law 2/Rule 3): the shell builtins emit most output via AH=09h (dos_print -> do_puts -> con_putc DIRECT, bypassing the JFT), which is NOT redirectable by DUP2(file,1). So 'echo HELLO > file' (the canonical case) would NOT redirect -- only AH=40h-handle-1 output (external filters) would. TWO BLOCKERS surfaced: (1) AH=09h/02h must route through the redirectable stdout handle 1 (the authentic DOS model; new prerequisite bead) -- this interacts with p96i's con_putc/ANSI hook; (2) the kernel window is EXHAUSTED -- the driver's ~1.9 KiB blew the 0x30000 guard, only fit via BATCH_FILE_MAX 2048->1536 + a 192-byte margin (untenable). The parse/driver DESIGN is sound; redo it once both prerequisites land.

title: CHKDSK.COM: FAT/dir integrity scan (+/F free-space, lost chains)
description: Walk the FAT + directory tree, report total/used/free, find lost cluster chains and cross-links, /F to fix. Needs subdir traversal (ti8) and the streaming cluster walk (dao).

title: Filters: MORE, SORT, FIND
description: The classic stdin->stdout filters. Need shell redirection/pipes (s-redir) to be useful. MORE paginates, SORT orders lines, FIND greps a literal.

title: Multi-volume drive letters A:/B:/C: + real SELDISK(0Eh)/GETDISK(19h)
description: Only ONE volume is mounted (the primary ATA slave, kmain.c:696). SELDISK(0Eh)/GETDISK(19h) are dispatched but never actually switch volume (drives >1 treated as invalid by getcwd/getdisk). Build a small mounted-volume table (A: floppy, C: HDD partition) so SELDISK switches the current drive and path 'C:\...' resolves to the right volume. Depends on partitions (k-mbr) and FAT16 (z01) for the HDD.

title: Shell built-ins: MD/MKDIR, RD/RMDIR, subdir-aware CD
description: Add the directory built-ins to COMMAND.COM (command.c classify table command.c:52-61 currently has only DIR/TYPE/CD/CLS/VER/ECHO/EXIT). CD is a fail-loud stub for any real path today (command.c:553). Wire MD/RD/CD to AH=39/3A/3B (k-dir39) and real GETCWD.
close_reason: Shell MD/RD/CD wired to AH=39/3A/3B + GETCWD prompt; HOST classifier gate + EMU test-ut6d (RD-removal + dot-dir assertions) + 2-leg mutation-proof; full make test green (68 host+24 emu); adversarially verified. Committed.

title: Shell built-ins: DATE and TIME
description: DATE/TIME built-ins absent though the RTC is live (AH=2Ah-2Dh wired, rtc.c). Add the display-and-prompt-to-change behavior (or display-only first), formatting per the locale convention.
close_reason: DATE + TIME shell built-ins done (2Ah/2Bh/2Ch/2Dh, get+set+validation). test-command + no-date/no-time mutants.

title: ANSI.SYS escape-sequence interpreter on the CON output path
description: DEVICE=ANSI.SYS sits in the CONFIG.SYS baseline (sysinit.c:43) but there is NO ESC[ interpreter -- console.c renders raw bytes, so cursor positioning/color/clear are not honored. Implement an ANSI.SYS device that parses CSI sequences (cursor move, CUP, ED/EL clear, SGR color, save/restore) on CON writes. Loads as a 509.7 DEVICE= driver.
notes: PURE FSM HALF LANDED (WL-0040, 9a5fea3): os/milton/ansi.{c,h} -- GROUND/ESC/CSI state machine emitting an action stream (PUT_CHAR/MOVE_CURSOR/ERASE/SET_ATTR/DSR/KEY_REMAP), cursor/erase/SGR + grounded ANSI->CGA colour map; host gate test-ansi (94 checks, 2 mutants). REMAINING = the CON-output-path wiring + CONFIG.SYS DEVICE=ANSI.SYS gate, tracked as initech-p96i (x3mh depends on it). x3mh stays OPEN until the wiring lands.
close_reason: COMPLETE: the pure ANSI.SYS FSM (ansi.c, Wave 1 WL-0040) + the CON-path WIRING (p96i, this wave WL-0043) both landed green. ANSI escape sequences (cursor/colour/erase) now take effect in the booted OS when CONFIG.SYS loads DEVICE=ANSI.SYS.

title: INT 21h AH=3Fh on CON (handle 0): real device line-read
description: AH=3Fh READ on a CON device handle returns 0 bytes/EOF today (int21.c:975-982) -- only the direct CON-input calls (01/06/07/08/0A) read the keyboard. Make handle-based read of CON deliver cooked line input (like AH=0Ah) so redirected/handle-driven programs read the keyboard. Pairs with 4tw (^C/break in the input path).
close_reason: Landed: AH=3Fh on CON handle 0 delivers cooked line input (line+CR+LF, count inclusive per KB Q113058) via a shared conin_cooked_line() helper extracted from do_buffered_input (0Ah byte-layout preserved -- proven by the surviving 0Ah mutants). test-x8fs + mutant. Integration caught a host HANG (conin_cooked_line spun on conin_get's no-source 0 sentinel via test_fileio's kji0 CREATNEW mutants); fixed at root cause with a host-safe EOF guard (Rule 2). I also wired the orphaned test-x8fs gate. Commit ddef39a; full gate 99 host + 27 emu GREEN.

title: Shell message-catalogue conformance oracle (ADR-0003 7.4); CD/DIR diagnostics off-spec
description: ADR-0003 7.4 requires a message-catalogue conformance oracle (diagnostic output == Appendix C verbatim). test_command.c exercises only pure tokenizer/classifier/formatter; nothing checks emitted strings vs spec/dos_messages.json -- which is why a non-catalogue CD diagnostic (command.c:531) and 'ECHO is on' (command.c:574) slipped in. Also DIR ignores its argument and always lists *.* (wrong result under valid input). This is the deferred initech-509.1 conformance work. Audit: shell no-message-catalogue-oracle + cd-noncatalogue-diagnostic + (dir-arg-ignored from summary).
acceptance_criteria: String-extraction oracle scans command.c (+ other diagnostic TUs) and asserts each user-facing literal is a MSG_DOS_* catalogue macro or a non-diagnostic UI element (mutation-proven by perturbing one diagnostic); CD diagnostic moved to the catalogue; DIR honors its path/wildcard argument; make test green.

title: ata.c: no oracle for fail-loud/error/write branches; missing BSY/DRDY-before-command; error codes collapsed
description: ata.c happy READ path is the only thing exercised (test-fs, QEMU only). No oracle drives TIMEOUT/DEVICE/NO_DRIVE-mid-poll/ARG or any write-path error branch (Rule 6: proven nothing; Rule 5: disk milestone wants tri-emulator). Protocol gaps: never waits BSY=0/DRDY=1 before writing the command register (ATA req on real hw/86Box); floating-bus check fires after the command rather than after drive-select; no IDENTIFY capacity bound; poll budget is an iteration count not a time bound. ATA error codes are collapsed to FAT12_ERR_READ by all callers, so distinct failure modes never surface. Audit: ata-blockdev ata-failloud-branches-no-oracle + ata-no-bsy-ready-before-command + ata-error-codes-collapsed.
acceptance_criteria: Boot WITHOUT --disk2 asserts FAT-MOUNT-FAIL via NO_DRIVE; a corrupt/short data image asserts the DEVICE/READ path; both mutation-proven; test-fs runs on Bochs as well as QEMU; BSY=0/DRDY=1 wait added before command writes; floating-bus check after drive-select; make test green.

title: console_init accepts degenerate geometry; no padded-pitch or 8bpp DAC oracle
description: console_init (console.c:273-290) copies lfb_pitch/width/height verbatim with no validation: pitch==0 aliases all scanlines to row 0 (silent FB corruption); width==0 draws nothing while returning CONSOLE_OK; pitch<width*bpp overruns into the next scanline. Violates console.h:84 'refuse bad input, do not scribble' + Rule 2. Test gaps: test_console.c always sets pitch==width*bpp (the 'honor pitch not width' contract is never exercised -- exactly the padded-LFB bug Bochs/86Box 24bpp hit), and the 8bpp DAC index->seafoam binding (kmain.c:548-551) has no end-to-end oracle. Audit: console console-init-no-pitch-validation + console-pitch-padding-test-gap + console-8bpp-dac-no-oracle.
acceptance_criteria: console_init returns CONSOLE_ERR_* on width==0/height==0/pitch<width*(bpp/8) and leaves console unusable; test_console adds a padded-pitch case (pitch=width*bpp+64) asserting correct scanline + untouched padding (mutation-proven by stride=width*bpp -> RED); a DAC/screendump oracle asserts idx1->seafoam, idx15->gray; make test green.
close_reason: console_init rejects degenerate geometry (width/height 0, pitch<width*bpp) via CONSOLE_ERR_GEOMETRY; added padded-pitch coverage test (pitch>width*bpp). Both mutation-proven (remove guard->RED; width*bpp stride->RED). 8bpp DAC end-to-end oracle split to follow-up (needs mode-0x13 screendump infra). make test 55h+19e GREEN.

title: Loader control-transfer asm reads jump target via memory operand after switching ESP
description: Entry asm (loader.c:323-336) does mov stack_top,%esp THEN jmp *entry_eip where entry_eip is a memory operand. Correct only because KERNEL_CFLAGS is -O0 with a frame pointer (EBP-relative locals resolve into the kernel frame). If the build moves to -O2 or -fomit-frame-pointer, entry_eip/psp_ptr/stack_top become ESP-relative and jmp *%4 dereferences the just-switched PROGRAM stack -> garbage/triple-fault. No compile-time guard pins this. Audit: loader-psp loader-asm-jmp-reads-operand-after-esp-switch.
acceptance_criteria: Load entry_eip into a dedicated register BEFORE the ESP switch (or pin the fn with an optimize/no-omit-frame-pointer attribute + a guard); behavior unchanged at -O0 and -O2; make test green.
close_reason: Entry asm loads the jump target (entry_eip) into EAX BEFORE the ESP switch and jmps via register, eliminating the only post-switch memory read. By construction optimization-safe. Demonstrated RED->GREEN at -O2 -fomit-frame-pointer (pre-fix: GREET.COM never runs; fixed: passes); no -O0 regression. The permanent -O2 coverage arrives free when the toolchain moves to -O2/i686-elf (initech-6pm/CDR-0001) -- test-exec then becomes the -O2 gate and now stays green. make test 55h+21e GREEN.

title: CONFIG.SYS larger than 1024-byte scratch is silently discarded (comment claims truncation)
description: sysinit_apply_config reads CONFIG.SYS into static char cfg_buf[1024] via fat12_read_file, which returns FAT12_ERR_BUFFER (does NOT truncate) when file>buf. So a >1024B CONFIG.SYS makes from_disk stay 0 and the user's whole file is silently ignored for SYSINIT_CONFIG_BASELINE; summary prints source=baseline masking the discard. Comment at sysinit.c:134-135 wrongly claims truncation. A 1024B CONFIG.SYS is period-realistic. Audit: sysinit-boot configsys-oversize-silent-baseline-fallback (+ sysinit-phases-no-host-test).
acceptance_criteria: Either honor truncation via incremental read, or fail loud (distinct 'SYSINIT: CONFIG.SYS too large' marker before fallback) and fix the comment; factor the file-read so sysinit_apply_config is host-drivable with an injected fake; host tests for from_disk success, oversize, FILES-absent baseline=20, FILES clamp; make test green.
close_reason: sysinit honors first 1KB of an oversize CONFIG.SYS via fat12_read_partial (trim to last line) + flags it 'source=CONFIG.SYS(truncated@1024)' instead of silently discarding to baseline (operator choice). Fixed misleading comment. New test-sysinit-oversize gate (oversize fixture, FILES=8 in first lines): RED (baseline FILES=20) -> GREEN (truncated@1024 FILES=8), mutation-proven. make test 55h+21e GREEN.

title: dos_reentry_panic serial output spins unbounded on a dead UART
description: panic.c bounds its serial spin (PSERIAL_SPIN_MAX) precisely so the fail-loud path cannot wedge. The sibling path irq.c rserial_putc (irq.c:60-66) spins while !(LSR&THRE) with NO bound; on an absent/stuck UART (common 86Box config) dos_reentry_panic hangs before it can int3 into the real panic -- defeating the loud-and-bounded guarantee. Audit: interrupts dos-reentry-panic-unbounded-serial-spin.
acceptance_criteria: irq.c rserial_putc uses the same bounded spin (PSERIAL_SPIN_MAX, drop byte on timeout) as pserial_putc; make test green.
close_reason: bcg.5: do_puts NULL-guard + INT21_PUTS_SCAN_MAX bound; RED (NULL deref segfault)->GREEN, mutation-proven. bcg.8: rserial_putc bounded by RSERIAL_SPIN_MAX mirroring panic.c (target-only port I/O; emu irq-storm serial path confirms no regression). make test 55h+19e GREEN.

title: 8259A spurious IRQ7 (0x2F) / IRQ15 (0x37) wedge the machine; no spurious-IRQ EOI discipline
description: Vectors 0x2F/0x37 are never installed with real handlers, so an 8259A spurious interrupt (INTR deasserted before ack -- electrical noise/timing) lands on isr_spurious -> permanent cli;hlt. Real-hw contract: master spurious (IRQ7) -> send NO EOI; slave spurious (IRQ15) -> EOI to master only. On period PCs (Rule 5) one line-noise glitch on IRQ7 halts the system. Audit: interrupts pic-spurious-irq7-irq15-unhandled.
acceptance_criteria: Dedicated stubs at 0x2F/0x37 checking the in-service register: master spurious IRET no-EOI; slave spurious EOI-to-master then IRET; not routed to the halt path; mutation-proven oracle; make test green.
close_reason: Dedicated isr_irq7_spurious(0x2F)/isr_irq15_spurious(0x37) stubs push real vectors; idt installs them; isr_dispatch_c applies EOI discipline (slave spurious 0x37 -> master-only EOI; master spurious 0x2F -> no EOI) then resumes. test-spurious extended to fire 0x2F/0x37, asserts vec=2F/37 (dedicated stubs ran) + resume; mutation-proven (remove installs -> RED). EOI correctness by-construction per 8259A datasheet (real spurious not deterministically triggerable). make test 55h+20e GREEN; test-boot-bochs PASS. Also fixed test_idt host stubs for the new externs.

title: Spurious/unhandled-vector ISR halts forever instead of returning cleanly
description: idt_init points all non-exception vectors at isr_spurious -> isr_common -> isr_dispatch_c, which ends in for(;;) cli;hlt (panic.c:140-142) for ANY vector. Spec says the spurious handler should print diagnostics and RETURN cleanly so a stray vector does not wedge the desktop. As written any unexpected software int / non-PIT-non-kbd hw vector permanently halts. Audit: interrupts spurious-halts-not-returns.
acceptance_criteria: Split spurious path from exception path: spurious stub emits a serial diagnostic and IRETs (isr_selftest pattern); permanent halt reserved for CPU exceptions 0-31; oracle fires an unexpected vector and asserts execution resumes; make test green.
close_reason: isr_dispatch_c now resumes (clean iret via isr_common) for spurious/unhandled vectors (vector>31, sentinel 0xFF), reserving the permanent halt for CPU exceptions 0-31. New test-spurious emu gate: BOOT_SPURIOUS fires int 0x70, asserts SPURIOUS-RESUMED + no PANIC + no triple-fault; RED->GREEN, mutation-proven. make test 55h+20e GREEN; test-boot-bochs PASS (interrupt-minefield Bochs check).

title: INT 21h AH=09h do_puts dereferences EDX with no NULL check and unbounded scan
description: do_puts (int21.c:313-331) walks bytes at EDX until 0x24 ('$') with NO NULL guard and NO scan bound. EDX==0 or a string lacking '$' walks arbitrary memory (fault on flat target / megabytes of garbage to CON). Contrast path-deref fns (NULL-check + INT21_PATH_SCAN_MAX) and do_read/write (DEC-14 guarded). AH=09h is the most common DOS output call; DEC-14's fail-loud rationale applies verbatim. Audit: int21 puts-unbounded-unguarded-edx.
acceptance_criteria: NULL check on EDX + runaway bound mirroring INT21_PATH_SCAN_MAX; no-'$' and EDX==0 fixtures; mutation-proven; make test green.
close_reason: bcg.5: do_puts NULL-guard + INT21_PUTS_SCAN_MAX bound; RED (NULL deref segfault)->GREEN, mutation-proven. bcg.8: rserial_putc bounded by RSERIAL_SPIN_MAX mirroring panic.c (target-only port I/O; emu irq-storm serial path confirms no regression). make test 55h+19e GREEN.

title: CONFIG.SYS decimal-overflow guard off-by-one (config_sys.c:76)
description: cfg_parse_uint guard 'if (v > 429496729u)' is checked before v=v*10+digit; at v==429496729 with next digit 6-9 the multiply-add overflows uint32 and WRAPS instead of saturating at 0xFFFFFFFF (the documented contract). 2^32-family values (e.g. FILES=4294967296) wrap to ~0 and clamp UP to FILES_MIN instead of DOWN to FILES_MAX. Benign (still in-range) but violates the saturation contract. Found by the wave-B CONFIG.SYS fuzzer (initech-hjv). Fix: guard 'v > (0xFFFFFFFFu - digit)/10u'. Must update the fuzzer's ref_parse_uint to the corrected saturating logic + add an explicit 2^32-family clamp-to-MAX assertion + a mutant restoring the off-by-one.
close_reason: Fixed in this commit: precise overflow guard (v > (UINT32_MAX-digit)/10), fuzzer ref updated in lockstep, off-by-one mutant added + caught. 55 host + 19 emu green.

title: harness/emu/86box.c headless driver (flatpak + Qt offscreen)
description: 86Box installed via flatpak --user. Build a best-effort headless driver (QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen, serial-to-file via generated 86box.cfg) for period-authenticity gating. Slower/looser than QEMU/Bochs; not on the fast inner loop. Front D. Relates to initech-x0i.

title: Tests for implemented-but-untested INT 21h fns (0Eh/19h/47h/59h/62h)
description: SELDISK/GETDISK/GETCWD/GETERR/GETPSP are implemented but have no test. Add unit coverage + mutants. Front B/C.
close_reason: Done in b1fac57 (wave B): test_int21_edge (82 checks), fat12_corrupt_fuzz (200 seeds x 8000 corruptions), test_config_sys_fuzz + test_cmdline_fuzz (4000 seeds each). All mutation-proven; 55 host + 19 emu gates green.

title: CONFIG.SYS + command-line/PSP cmd-tail fuzzers
description: Generative fuzzers for the CONFIG.SYS parser (malformed lines, non-numeric FILES=, >255-char lines) and command-line/PSP cmd_tail (whitespace, >127-char overflow). Deterministic seeds (Rule 11). Front B.
close_reason: Done in b1fac57 (wave B): test_int21_edge (82 checks), fat12_corrupt_fuzz (200 seeds x 8000 corruptions), test_config_sys_fuzz + test_cmdline_fuzz (4000 seeds each). All mutation-proven; 55 host + 19 emu gates green.

title: Fail-loud user-pointer guards on INT 21h (NULL / outside flat arena) + ADR
description: EDX user pointers in do_write/do_read/do_setdta/do_exec/dispatch are used unchecked. Add fail-loud guards (NULL / out-of-arena) consistent with Rule 2. Behavioral change vs real DOS -> write a short ADR first (Law 1/Rule 8). Front A.
close_reason: Done: ADR-0003 DEC-14 (fail-loud user-pointer validation) + user_buf_ok guards in do_read/do_write + defensive emit_find_data DTA guard. NULL/32-bit-wrap -> CF=1, AX=0x0009. Mutation-proven (test-int21-edge 94 checks; no-ptr-guard mutant SIGSEGVs=RED). 55 host + 19 emu green.

title: Compile-time asserts: loader exit-hook struct packing + kbd ring power-of-2
description: loader_exit_hook asm assumes a struct field offset; kbd ring assumes power-of-2 capacity. Add _Static_assert so a layout change fails the build, not silently at runtime. Front A.
notes: kbd.h power-of-2 _Static_assert DONE in fac5577. REMAINING: loader.c exit-hook struct-packing _Static_assert (assert the saved-context field offset the asm assumes). Keep open for the loader half.
close_reason: Both static asserts present: kbd ring power-of-2 added in fac5577 (kbd.h); loader exit-hook struct-offset asserts ALREADY existed (loader.c:163-170, all 4 fields). Audit over-reported the loader gap (Rule 4). No further work.

title: FAT12 mount geometry: unchecked data_sectors underflow / cluster-count bounds (fat12.c:105)
description: Mount-time geometry derivation subtracts first_data_sector from total_sectors with no underflow guard and classifies FAT type on an unvalidated cluster count. Add guards + malformed-BPB test. Front A.
close_reason: NOT A BUG (Rule 4): mount already rejects sectors_per_cluster==0 (fat12.c:80, no div-by-zero), total_sectors==0 (:92), and first_data_sector>=total_sectors/<=root_dir (:105, no underflow). Malformed-BPB edge TESTS to be added under the corruption fuzzer (initech-dnn); no code change.

title: Wire INT 24h critical-error handler to real disk-error sources (fat12.c/ata.c)
description: 509.8 implemented the INT 24h critical-error handler (presents MSG-DOS-0001, processes A/R/F, returns AL=0/1/2) and made it invokable (int $0x24 + the trap gate), but NO real kernel path RAISES it yet. Real DOS invokes INT 24h from the disk-I/O error path. Wire fat12.c / ata.c read/write failures (floating-bus, BSY/DRQ timeout, bad sector, write-protect) to invoke the INT 24h handler and honor its A/R/F return (Retry=re-issue the operation, Abort=terminate, Fail=return error to the syscall). Surfaced by 509.8 (handler exists, trigger deferred). Ref: ADR-0003 DEC-10; the Ignore=3 action is also deferred.
close_reason: INT 24h now RAISED by the disk layer + A/R/F honored: crit_blockdev wrapper (ata.c) -> int21_run_critical_error -> real int24_dispatch (MSG-DOS-0001); Retry re-issues (bounded), Abort=do_terminate(0x23), Fail propagates. Wired into kmain (mount-through-wrapper; hook armed POST-mount so no-disk boots stay graceful). Host gate test-int24-wired (19 checks, 2 mutants via lpf3); emu regression 9/9 PASS (WL-0041, db2e92d). In-emulator INT-24h-trigger gate deferred (needs a QEMU mid-file disk-fault mechanism) -- filed.

title: Build-time guard: kernel _kernel_end must stay below PROGRAM_BASE (0x20000)
description: The kernel size guards in the Makefile check only the LOADED .bin size vs KERNEL_SECTORS*512 -- they do NOT check that _kernel_end (the end of .bss, which is NOT in the .bin) stays below PROGRAM_BASE (0x20000). During 509.2 the shell kernel's .bss (cfg_fat_buf[4608]) pushed _kernel_end to 0x1FF60 -- only 160 bytes below PROGRAM_BASE -- a SILENT near-collision the existing guards could not see (the .bin still fit). A future kernel .bss growth would silently corrupt the program-load region (0x20000). Add a build-time check (extract _kernel_end via nm from each kernel ELF, assert < PROGRAM_BASE with a margin) so this fails LOUD (Rule 2) like the .bin-size guard does. Apply to all kernel variants (or the largest as a canary). Cite spec/memory_map.h PROGRAM_BASE.
acceptance_criteria: Building any kernel whose _kernel_end >= PROGRAM_BASE (or within a small margin) fails the build with a clear message naming _kernel_end and PROGRAM_BASE; deliberately bloating a kernel's .bss past 0x20000 is caught at build, not at runtime.
close_reason: Build-time _kernel_end < PROGRAM_BASE guard added to all 15 kernel .bin recipes: nm extracts _kernel_end, asserts < PROGRAM_BASE-256 (parsed from spec/memory_map.h), fails loud with actionable message. Mutation-proven (orchestrator independently: 0x1000 .bss bloat -> _kernel_end=0x20d60 -> build RED; .bin guard stayed green proving the gap closed). Current headroom: 480 bytes.

title: IO.SYS + INITDOS.SYS physical two-file kernel split (H/S/R, first two root entries)
description: Split off from 509.2: the PHYSICAL on-disk two-file partition. Build the kernel as IO.SYS (HW-dependent: resident drivers + SYSINIT) + INITDOS.SYS (HW-independent: INT 21h dispatcher/FS/arena/PSP/SFT), both Hidden+System+ReadOnly as the FIRST TWO root-dir entries; boot loads IO.SYS then INITDOS.SYS in order. This is period AUTHENTICITY (real DOS layout), carries boot-layout risk (cf. KERNEL_SECTORS in WL-0009), and is NOT capability-bearing -- deferred so the SYSINIT/CONFIG.SYS capability work (509.2) and the resident drivers (509.7) proceed first against the current single flat-binary kernel. DEC-01.
acceptance_criteria: Boot loads IO.SYS then INITDOS.SYS as two separate files; both carry H/S/R attrs in the first two root entries; the 53-gate vector stays green across the boot-layout change.

title: test-shell keystroke-injection gate flakes under extreme CPU oversubscription
description: Surfaced while verifying initech-3pe. test-shell drives a 37-key REPL transcript via --keys-after and gates on a SHELL-DONE serial marker within a fixed 12000ms wall clock; wait_for_serial_marker for keys_after uses a hardcoded 4000ms budget. Under pathological saturation (12/12 cores hogged) QEMU is starved and the transcript can't finish in 12s, so SHELL-DONE is missing -> RED. Passes solo and under realistic load (6/12 cores). Different mechanism from initech-3pe (the screendump grab); this is the key-injection/marker-completion timing path. FIX direction: tie the keystroke gate's completion budget to a marker-driven deadline (poll SHELL-DONE up to the wall clock) rather than fixed 12s/4s, mirroring the screendump-after approach, so it degrades gracefully under load instead of false-RED.
acceptance_criteria: test-shell passes deterministically under full-core saturation (12/12 hogs) 5x; no fixed sub-budget that false-REDs a guest that is merely slow; still fails honestly if SHELL-DONE genuinely never appears.

title: Pass command-tail args to EXEC'd child (PSP:0x80)
description: AH=4Bh EXEC and the shell run external programs with no argument tail; DOS passes the command tail into the child PSP at 0x80 (and the parsed FCBs). Wire the shell's parsed arg tail through load_program/psp_build into the child PSP cmd_tail so programs receive argv. Surfaced building initech-7pc (and noted in saw).
close_reason: AH=4Bh EXEC now carries the command tail to the child PSP:80h via a flat exec_param_block_t in EBX; cmd_parse captures the verbatim DOS tail. Chain covered+mutation-proven link-by-link (cmd_parse/do_exec/loader/psp_build). make test 55+21 green. Commit pending.

title: COMMAND.COM external-command resolution: PATH + COMSPEC
description: External command lookup currently checks only the FAT root for <cmd>.COM. Implement PATH-list search + COMSPEC self-location (DEC-11 re-scope). Needs subdir traversal (initech-ti8) for multi-dir PATH. Surfaced building initech-7pc.
close_reason: PATH-directory search + COMSPEC done: cmd_path_candidates (CWD->PATH dirs, .COM/.EXE/.BAT order, explicit-path verbatim) wired into run_external; test-command 166 checks + no-path mutant; test-shell emu EXEC green.

title: COMMAND.COM batch (.BAT) interpreter + AUTOEXEC.BAT
description: COMMAND.COM should interpret .BAT batch files line-by-line (and run AUTOEXEC.BAT at startup). spec/dos_autoexec_bat_baseline.txt + spec/dos_config_sys_baseline.txt already exist. Ties to initech-509.10 (CONFIG.SYS/AUTOEXEC baseline). DEC-11 re-scope. Surfaced building initech-7pc.
notes: PARSER HALF LANDED (Wave 5): os/milton/batch.{c,h} pure batch_expand + batch_classify + batch_label_matches, test-batch 101 checks + 3 mutants. REMAINING (deferred): the command.c REPL integration -- run a .BAT line-by-line, AUTOEXEC.BAT at boot, IF/FOR/SHIFT/CALL/PAUSE execution semantics, ECHO ON/OFF state, ERRORLEVEL. batch_expand takes argv/argc so SHIFT = pass argv+n.
close_reason: REPL integration of the .BAT interpreter complete: dispatch_line refactor + run_batch (IF/FOR/SHIFT/GOTO/CALL/PAUSE/ECHO-state/ERRORLEVEL via AH=4Dh) + AUTOEXEC.BAT at REPL entry + .BAT dispatch in run_external. Host gate test-batch-exec (63 checks, 2 mutants) + EMU gate test-autoexec(+mutant) green (WL-0040, 9a5fea3). Deepenings (wildcard FOR, PATH built-in) noted in bead history.

title: Reconcile Enter encoding: kbd LF (0x0A) vs DOS CR (0x0D)
description: os/milton/kbd.c decodes the Enter scancode (0x1C) to LF 0x0A; DOS/BIOS convention (INT 16h) returns CR 0x0D for Enter. AH=0Ah currently normalizes LF->CR as a workaround (documented). Root fix (Rule 3, no bandaids): make kbd.c emit CR for Enter (matching real PC keyboard via BIOS), or introduce an INT 16h-style canonicalization layer, so the whole CON path uses one convention. Surfaced building initech-n62.
close_reason: Root fix: kbd emits CR (0x0D) for Enter in both scancode tables; AH=0Ah terminates on CR only, LF bandaid retired (new regression test locks it). Both mutation-proven; test-conin emu end-to-end green. make test 55+21 green.

title: INT 21h AH=01h/08h/0Ah: Ctrl-C / INT 23h break handling
description: CON input 01h/08h/0Ah deliver Ctrl-C (0x03) as an ordinary char -- there is no INT 23h Ctrl-Break handler or process model yet (INT 22/23/24 = initech-509.8). Once a break handler exists, the input functions must check for ^C and invoke the INT 23h handler. Surfaced building initech-n62.
notes: Dep direction corrected 2026-06-15 (WL grounding): 4tw now DEPENDS-ON er3h (was: 4tw blocks er3h). Code evidence: 4tw's ^C check is gated on g_break_flag + int21_set_break_flag(), which do not exist today (grep int21.c = 0 hits; comment int21.c:501-504 confirms ^C/break model deferred). er3h CREATES those symbols. x8fs->4tw edge dropped: x8fs edits do_read SFT_KIND_DEVICE leg (int21.c:1906-1962), disjoint from conin_get_pb seam.
close_reason: Landed (commit e4b2222): CON-input ^C (0x03) -> INT 23h check-point via shared conin_check_ctrlc, hooked into 01h/08h/0Ah (NOT 07h DIRECT / 06h -- the documented DOS exceptions deliver 0x03 raw; overrode the bead's file-touch map per Law 1 ground truth). Gating per DEC-16 Fork A: CON family is ALWAYS a check-point (ON-widening to other INT 21h calls = followup C-6). test-4tw + mutant (no-ctrlc-check) RED. Host gates 101->103. Bumped KERNEL_SECTORS 144->160 (kernel grew); full gate 103+27 GREEN + Bochs boot leg PASS (geometry change, Rule 5).

title: FINDFIRST DTA layout deviates from real-DOS byte offsets
description: spec/find_data.h locks attr@0x0C, time@0x0D, date@0x0F, size@0x11, fname@0x15 (per the fs-mount-sft-ground-truth.md brief, padded to 43 bytes). Real DOS 3.3 places the found-file fields at attr@0x15, time@0x16, date@0x18, size@0x1A, name@0x1E (the first 0x15 bytes are reserved/search-template). Our DIR.COM reads our offsets so it is self-consistent, but the DTA is NOT byte-compatible with real DOS -- a real-DOS program (or a differential test vs real DOS FINDFIRST) would read garbage. Decide: keep the simplified layout (document as intentional InitechDOS divergence) or correct to the real-DOS offsets. Surfaced verifying initech-509.5.
close_reason: FINDFIRST/NEXT DTA corrected to real-DOS byte offsets (attr@0x15..name@0x1E); spec+writer+both asm readers+brief reconciled; compile-time offsetof oracle + runtime byte-golden, both mutation-proven; make test 55+21 green. Commit 650b23d.

title: Full DOS 8.3 wildcard engine for FINDFIRST/NEXT
description: FINDFIRST/NEXT (4Eh/4Fh) match only '*.*' (all) and exact 8.3 now. Implement the full DOS wildcard semantics (? matches any one char in position; * fills the remainder of name or ext with ?; the quirky 8.3 expansion rules). Needed for 'DIR *.TXT' etc. in the shell. Surfaced building initech-509.5.
close_reason: Landed: mutation-proven DOS 8.3 wildcard oracle (test-80k + 4 mutants) over the REAL FCB matcher. Finding: the matcher (build_pattern/pattern_match) ALREADY implemented full FCB wildcards on this branch -- the bead premise was stale; corrected the grounder's wrong FOO?.* guess against Old New Thing ground truth (? matches the pad space). +7 FINDFIRST integration checks. Integrated commit ce76f69; full gate 99 host + 27 emu GREEN.

title: Subdirectory / path traversal in OPEN + FINDFIRST
description: AH=3Dh OPEN and AH=4Eh FINDFIRST handle ROOT-directory 8.3 names only; a path containing '\' or ':' is rejected (OPEN -> 0x0003 path-not-found). Implement subdirectory cluster-chain traversal so paths like \SUB\FILE.EXT resolve. Needed before CD into subdirs is meaningful (shell 7pc). Surfaced building initech-509.5.
notes: LAYER SPLIT (grounded this session, wf_fc0ea9c9-a7a). ti8 is now scoped to LAYER 1 ONLY: fat12.c additive READ-side subdir traversal -- new fat12_dir_t{is_root,start_cluster} + fat12_read_dir (is_root branch delegates to UNCHANGED fat12_read_root_dir; else cluster-chain walk reusing fat12_next_cluster+fat12_cluster_in_range, anti-hang bounded by total_clusters+2) + fat12_resolve_path (split on '\', strip 'A:', per-non-final-component DIR_ATTR_DIRECTORY gate, '.'/'..' with start_cluster==0=>is_root). Do NOT edit the 4 root primitives (read_root_dir@397, read_dirent_local@1064, write_dirent@1089, scan_root@1123 -- all VERIFIED accurate). Oracle: nested mtools image (mmd ::SUB, ::SUB/DEEP, ::BIGDIR with 40 files=3 clusters), fat12_ref.py --list-path, test_fat12_subdir.c 3-way differential, mutants FAT12_MUTATE_SUBDIR_SINGLESECTOR/_NOATTR. test_fat12_subdir.c drives REAL fat12.c via blockdev_file (NO int21 mock needed) so L1 is independently mergeable+green. The DOS-API wiring (OPEN/FINDFIRST resolve paths) is the separate Core-tier bead initech-mzxa (Layer 2), which depends on this.
close_reason: Layer 1 DONE + merged (99b84bd). fat12.c additive subdir traversal (fat12_dir_t + fat12_read_dir + fat12_resolve_path); 4 root primitives byte-unchanged; oracle test-fat-subdir (3-way differential, multi-cluster BIGDIR) + 2 mutants mutation-proven + adversarially verified; full make test 61 host + 22 emu GREEN. DOS-API wiring (OPEN/FINDFIRST resolve paths) is the separate Layer-2 bead initech-mzxa, now unblocked.

title: FAT-sourced program load: load a .COM from the FAT12 volume via ATA (connect loader<->fat12<->ata)
description: The loader currently runs a program baked into the kernel image. Connect the three proven pieces -- fat12.c (read, oracle-green), ata.c (ATA PIO backend, written), loader.c (load/run/return) -- so InitechDOS reads a .COM from a real FAT12 volume (attached to QEMU as an ATA disk) into PROGRAM_IMAGE and runs it. This also validates ata.c on the emulator for the first time (deferred from the FAT session). Part of the 'rest' phase toward the f8v.4 keystone (DIR/TYPE over a FAT volume). Related: initech-adf (FAT read), initech-z01 (FAT16/ATA), initech-7pc (shell).
acceptance_criteria: A .COM on a mformat'd FAT12 disk attached to QEMU is read via fat12+ata, loaded, run, and returns; differential/serial oracle green; ata.c exercised on the emulator.
notes: PARTIAL (mount + proto-DIR landed this session): harness --disk2 attaches a FAT12 data volume (IDE primary slave); kernel mounts it via fat12.c+ata.c and renders a DOS-style directory listing to the LFB console (Directory of A:\ + NAME.EXT + size). make test-fs gate (serial filenames + DIR-OK + screendump DIR-band check). A dangling-console lifetime bug (con declared in a nested block, used after scope by the DIR path) was caught by a visual screendump check + root-caused + fixed (hoisted con to function scope); the screendump oracle was STRENGTHENED (ppm_text_check optional band) to bite a DIR-not-rendered regression. REMAINING for saw: load a .COM FROM the FAT volume (vs the current baked program) -- needs the file-handle OPEN/READ path (M3 steps 3-4).
close_reason: Done & green: load_program_from_fat (os/milton/loader.c:411-471) connects loader<->fat12<->ata, wired into real EXEC (kmain.c:707/809, INT21 AH=4Bh); make test-exec GREEN (EXEC-SAW-EXIT rc=7, no triple-fault). (audit recon 2026-06-13)

title: INT 21h gate DPL=0 -> DPL=3 upgrade for ring-3 callers (DEC-04a C-4)
description: Per ADR-0003 amendment DEC-04a Consequence C-4: the int 0x21 trap gate is DPL=0 (correct for the current ring-0-only kernel). Before any ring-3 program (Turbo Initech user program, ADR-0007) issues int 0x21, the gate DPL MUST be raised to 3 -- otherwise every user-mode syscall #GPs. Blocked-by/related to the ring-3 milestone (ADR-0007).
acceptance_criteria: int 0x21 gate DPL=3 when ring-3 programs exist; a ring-3 int 0x21 dispatches (no #GP); ring-0 callers still work.

title: INT 21h CON input (01h/06h/0Ah) + the keyboard path
description: The INT 21h register lists CON input (01h direct input, 06h direct console I/O, 0Ah buffered input) but the dispatcher returns the 'not-yet-impl' diagnostic for them -- they need a PS/2 keyboard driver (IRQ1) + a CON input ring buffer. Unblocks an interactive COMMAND.COM. Depends on the interrupt foundation (initech-a5a, done) for IRQ1, and on PIC IRQ unmask + EOI. Related: initech-3rs (PS/2 keyboard + PIT).
acceptance_criteria: 01h/06h/0Ah read characters from the keyboard via CON; echo per DOS semantics; differential vs reference DOS behavior.
close_reason: INT 21h CON input complete: AH=01h (echo)/06h (DL=FF poll w/ ZF; else output)/07h/08h (no-echo)/0Ah (buffered line: BACKSPACE edit, max-len clamp+BEL, LF->CR normalize)/0Bh (status)/0Ch (flush+chain). New int21_set_conin(get,poll) seam mirrors the sink (kernel binds kbd hlt-spin + non-blocking poll; host binds queued mock; NULL=EOF, never hangs). Oracles: test-conin-unit (45,+2 mutants RED: 01h-no-echo, 0Ah-count-CR), test-conin (a real .COM reads a keyboard line via AH=0Ah, --keys d,i,r,ret -> CONIN-LINE=dir). No regression. Deferred: Ctrl-C/INT 23h. Follow-ups raised.

title: Appendix D baseline: CONFIG.SYS + AUTOEXEC.BAT (DEC-11/12)
description: SYSINIT applies spec/dos_config_sys_baseline.txt; COMMAND.COM runs spec/dos_autoexec_bat_baseline.txt on first boot. Verbatim per App D.2/D.3. Oracle: mount the generated FAT image, verify bytes match the spec-data.
acceptance_criteria: Generated boot volume CONFIG.SYS/AUTOEXEC.BAT match the baselines byte-for-byte; AUTOEXEC runs on boot.

title: INT 22/23/24 handlers + PSP vector save (DEC-10)
description: Termination 22h, control-break 23h, critical-error 24h handlers. Critical-error emits MSG-DOS-0001 and processes A/R/F. Save prior vectors in PSP 0Ah-15h on EXEC; restore on EXIT. DEC-10.
acceptance_criteria: A critical error presents MSG-DOS-0001 and processes Abort/Retry/Fail; vectors saved/restored across EXEC/EXIT.
close_reason: DEC-10 landed: INT 22h (terminate)/23h (control-break)/24h (critical-error) IDT trap gates + handlers; INT 24h presents MSG-DOS-0001 + processes A/R/F (AL=0 Retry/1 Abort/2 Fail) via pure host-testable crit_error_action; INT 21h AH=25h SETVECT / 35h GETVECT (completed the already-listed register surface); PSP saved_vectors[0Ah-15h] saved on EXEC + restored on EXIT (DOS-authentic: child SETVECT does not leak). Oracles: test-int24 (44 checks) + test-int24-mutant (4 mutants bite) + emu test-vect (V24POST==V24PRE + CRIT-AL=1 + MSG-DOS-0001). Orchestrator independently mutation-proved host + emu teeth. Full vector 45 host + 19 emu = 64 gates green. Prereqs cleared first: u0a (build guard), 5pe (PROGRAM_BASE 0x20000->0x30000). Deferred: real disk-error INT 24h trigger (new bead); ^C->INT 23h keyboard wiring (4tw).

title: Resident device drivers CON/PRN/AUX/CLOCK$/NUL + INT 2Fh redirector (DEC-09)
description: Heritage device-header model (Strategy+Interrupt entry points, request packets). Resident char devices CON/PRN/AUX/CLOCK$/NUL; installable via DEVICE=. INT 2Fh multiplex network redirector (INITNET.SYS) for mapped drives, per App D + enterprise context. DEC-09.
acceptance_criteria: CON/PRN/AUX/CLOCK$/NUL respond to the standard command set; a DEVICE= driver loads; INT 2Fh presents a mapped drive.
notes: CORE LANDED (WL-0041, db2e92d): os/milton/devices.{c,h} device-header chain + 5 char devices NUL/CON/AUX/PRN/CLOCK$ + request-packet protocol; host gate test-devices (150 checks, 2 mutants). REMAINING = int21 OPEN-by-name + sysinit DEVICE= install (initech-6zd9); INT 2Fh redirector stays OUT (amendment-gated). 509.7 stays OPEN until the wiring lands.
close_reason: CORE + WIRING complete for the parity scope: the heritage device-header chain + 5 resident char devices CON/PRN/AUX/CLOCK$/NUL (devices.c, test-devices 150 checks) + INT 21h OPEN-by-name + READ/WRITE routing + sysinit install (6zd9, test-devwire 40 checks), all green host + full emu suite (WL-0041/WL-0042). The 5 devices RESPOND to the standard command set -- the Core scope the 40oq capstone needs. OUT/deferred (separate beads): installable DEVICE=*.SYS driver loading (deepening, filed); INT 2Fh network redirector (amendment-gated, separate epic per the operator parity ruling -- om2a/ws3x/t1hl).

title: MCB memory arena 48h/49h/4Ah (DEC-03, vestigial)
description: 16-byte MCB chain: 'M'/'Z' signature, owner id (0=free), paragraph size; wire ALLOC/FREE/SETBLOCK. Vestigial on 32-bit flat but retained IN FULL per DEC-03 and the design stance. Layout per spec/dos_structs.h mcb_t.
acceptance_criteria: ALLOC/FREE/SETBLOCK operate on a walkable MCB chain with correct M/Z signatures + owner/size.
notes: PART 1 DONE (commit 47df811): pure MCB allocator os/milton/mcb.{c,h} + property suite test_mcb.c + test-mcb/test-mcb-mutant gates (first-fit, split, coalesce, setblock, failure-largest, bad-block/owner guards, 20k-op data-integrity fuzz; mutation-proven). REMAINS (part 2): wire AH=48h/49h/4Ah in int21.c to an mcb_arena_t over a real region + DECIDE the arena region -- the program window 0x30000..0x70000 is fully tiled, so either (A) carve a heap window (reduce PROGRAM_IMAGE_MAX in spec/memory_map.h, deliberate) or (B) the authentic shrink-then-alloc model. Segment = (arena_base_linear>>4)+data_para; owner = current PSP. Add a host dispatch test + an emu gate.
close_reason: Part 2 done: AH=48/49/4Ah wired to the MCB arena (authentic single-big-block over locked [0x30000,0x70000); merged 0c9e97c). make test 59 host + 22 emu GREEN, Bochs boot green after the IMG_SECTORS geometry fix. Mutation-proven.

title: fat12_read_file uses 5760-byte on-stack chain[2880] scratch
description: os/milton/fat12.c:529 fat12_read_file declares uint16_t chain[2880] (~5.6KB) on the stack, sized to the 1.44MB floppy cluster count. Safe for the floppy read path, but a kernel-stack hazard: (a) the kernel stack budget is small, (b) FAT16 HDD geometry (initech-z01) has far more clusters and would overflow it. Rule 3 (all bugs deep): move to a caller-provided chain buffer OR a streaming cluster-by-cluster walk (no full-chain materialization). Surfaced during initech-adf dir/read implementation; not yet a live bug on the floppy path.
acceptance_criteria: fat12_read_file no longer holds a geometry-sized array on the stack; FAT16 path cannot overflow; floppy read tests stay green.
close_reason: Streaming cluster walk landed + verified (WL-0027, 654e48a); on-stack chain[2880] eliminated, anti-hang + RISK-5 + corrupt-fuzzer contract preserved; test-fat-readfile-mutant (3) + BIGCHAIN large-file leg. 93 host + 27 emu green.

title: M1 boot robustness: LBA fallback, multi-bpp/mode, LinBytesPerScanLine, tri-emulator VBE
description: Tracer boot (f8v.2) made pragmatic choices that M1 must harden: (a) INT13h CHS read -> add LBA (AH=42h) fallback for geometries where C0/H0/S2 isn't valid; (b) QEMU stdvga gave 24bpp@0xFD000000 -- real iron/86Box/Bochs differ, test 32bpp + 800x600/832x624 across the tri-emulator gate; (c) prefer VBE2.0 LinBytesPerScanLine (mode-info 0x32) over BytesPerScanLine (0x10) when present. Surfaced building initech-f8v.2.
acceptance_criteria: Boot succeeds across QEMU/Bochs/86Box at 640x480 and one larger mode, CHS and LBA disks, 24 and 32 bpp.

title: Seed compiler: grow Pascal subset (if/while/for, procedures, types, relational/logical ops)

To eventually cross-build the OS, grow to if/then/else, while, for, repeat, case, procedures/functions, const, boolean/char/real, relational+logical ops, arrays/records/pointers, units, inline asm (PRD §6.7). Grammar TODOs marked in seed/ast.h, parser.h.
acceptance_criteria: Subset sufficient to compile the kernel/Toolbox sources; differential vs Free Pascal on the grown corpus.
notes: RE-SCOPE (PNC-1 Option A): grow the Pascal subset to compile TURBO INITECH (self-host compiler) + Pascal apps -- NOT the OS kernel (which is C). Seed initech-znb is the genesis of Turbo Initech, not the OS bootstrap.

title: FLAIR mouse_init: drain the 0xFA enable-ACK so it doesn't leak onto IRQ1 as a phantom keyDown
description: Found during FO-7/8 (5l5z pump). mouse_init sends 0xF4 (enable data reporting) and the mouse replies 0xFA (ACK); under the live pump that ACK byte arrives via IRQ1 and WaitNextEvent cooks it into a phantom keyDown EventRecord at boot (FLAIR-EVT what=3 msg=0000FA00). BENIGN (all gate assertions key on the SPECIFIC msg/what, never bare ^FLAIR-EVT), but mouse_init should fully drain/ACK-consume the 0xFA before unmasking so the cooked stream is clean. Small fix in os/milton/mouse.c (consume the ACK on the data port with a bounded spin before the IRQ path goes live). P3.

title: FO-D2-8: exercise the GDI CombineRgn facade ON METAL (Photoshop menu-bar clip)
description: ADR-0005-AMENDMENT-AM-1 FO-D2-8 (NOT a blocker; filed at n79q landing). The GDI peer facade (CombineRgn/GetRgnBox/PtInRegion/RectInRegion) is verified-but-UNUSED: nothing in the artifact calls CombineRgn today (os/flair/menu.c:344 takes a raw const region_t *clip). For a full dual-heritage GUI the Win/Photoshop menu-bar clip should route through CombineRgn(dst, bar_rgn, vis_rgn, RGN_AND) so the peer GDI facade is load-bearing ON METAL, not only in the test-region-gdi oracle (ADR-0004-AMENDMENT-DEC-09 D-9b 'THE GAP-CLOSURE'). Depends on the menu.c clip path + the era-skin registry (initech-m6qx). gdi_ref_ namespacing mandatory; the single-engine grep gate (C-7) still guards it.

title: Consider raising the minimum hardware target to 486+ (historical accuracy for the dBASE/Lotus/WordPerfect -> Office Space era)
description: OPERATOR NOTE (2026-06-21): On reflection + research, the hardware era for the canonical app suite (dBASE III+/IV, Lotus 1-2-3, WordPerfect) running into the Office Space-frame period skews LATER than 386 -- closer to 486/Pentium. It is well plausible and DEFENSIBLE for the FINAL InitechOS to require 486-or-later hardware. ADR-0001 currently ratifies '386+, 32-bit flat'. This is a note to REVISIT the minimum-hardware contract for PRD / north-star purposes: a 486 floor is period-honest (the FPU question -- 486DX has an on-die x87, which matters for InitechBase/SAMIR soft-float vs hardware float, Initech 123 recalc, and any FP in the apps), and it would let us assume CPUID, larger caches, and a faster baseline. NOT a decision to make now and NOT blocking current FLAIR work; record it so the next PRD/ADR pass weighs it. If adopted: amend ADR-0001 (386+ -> 486+), the PRD Sec 5 hardware contract, spec/hardware.json, and reconsider whether SAMIR's soft-float profile (ADR-0009) should gain a hardware-x87 path. Decision owner: operator + committee.
notes: Raised by operator while reviewing era hardware. Cite: ADR-0001 (386+ ratification); PRD Sec 5 (hardware contract); spec/hardware.json; ADR-0009 (SAMIR soft-float, FLOW_MAX_REGISTRY=1). Tension to weigh: period authenticity (Law 4) vs the 386+ floor already built. 486DX on-die FPU is the most consequential delta.

title: Installable DEVICE= driver loading: load a .SYS device driver named in CONFIG.SYS into the chain
description: 509.7 Core (the 5 resident char devices + OPEN-by-name + sysinit recognition) is done. The remaining piece of 509.7's literal acceptance -- loading an INSTALLABLE DEVICE=FOO.SYS driver from CONFIG.SYS into the device chain (read the .SYS, relocate, link its device_header into g_chain_head, call its INIT) -- is a deepening beyond the resident chain. Ground vs the MS-DOS device-driver load protocol. ANSI.SYS itself is recorded as a flag (p96i implements its behavior); a GENERIC installable loader is this bead.
notes: Surfaced WL-0042 Wave 3 (509.7 wiring). The resident chain is what the 40oq capstone needs; this is the installable-driver deepening.

title: In-emulator OPEN-by-name device gate: a .COM that OPENs NUL/CON/PRN by name on QEMU
description: 6zd9 wired AH=3Dh OPEN-by-name -> devices.c + READ/WRITE routing, proven by the host oracle test-devwire (40 checks, mutant, driving the REAL int21_dispatch over the REAL devices.c chain) and by the full emu regression (the universal CON path is preserved -- every boot/shell/EXEC gate still renders). DEFERRED: a dedicated IN-EMULATOR gate -- a .COM (asm) that AH=3Dh OPENs 'NUL'/'CON'/'PRN', AH=40h WRITEs (NUL discards, CON->serial, PRN->sink), AH=3Fh READs NUL->EOF, emits a DEVWIRE-OK serial marker. Mint + boot + assert markers. Mirrors test-zs24-exec.
notes: Surfaced WL-0042 Wave 3 (6zd9). Host oracle is authoritative (Law 2); this is the in-emulator end-to-end deepening.

title: In-emulator INT 24h trigger gate: force a mid-file disk fault on QEMU + assert MSG-DOS-0001 + A/R/F
description: mvg wired INT 24h to the disk layer (crit_blockdev wrapper, armed after mount) + honored A/R/F, proven by the host oracle test-int24-wired (19 checks, 2 mutants via the lpf3 fault-injection backend) and by the emu EXEC/fs regression (the wrapper is transparent on success -> normal boots unaffected; no-disk2 boot still degrades gracefully). DEFERRED: a dedicated IN-EMULATOR gate that actually TRIGGERS INT 24h needs a QEMU disk-fault mechanism (a disk that mounts OK but where a file read hits a hard sector error -- e.g. a crafted cluster chain pointing past the medium, or a QEMU blkdebug/werror=stop drive). Build it: mint such a disk, boot, TYPE/EXEC the bad file, assert MSG-DOS-0001 on serial + an A/R/F outcome (inject a key). Mirror the test-int24-wired logic at the emu level.
notes: Surfaced WL-0041 Wave 2 (mvg). Host oracle is authoritative (Law 2); this is the in-emulator end-to-end deepening, like initech-2cn6 for KEEP.

title: INT 21h AH=34h GET InDOS FLAG ADDRESS (TSR companion to AH=31h KEEP)
description: bo40 added AH=31h KEEP + AH=4Dh exit-type-3 but deferred AH=34h. Real TSRs poll the InDOS flag to know when DOS is reentrant. int21.c already has a dos_in_dos() predicate; AH=34h must return ES:BX -> the InDOS flag byte (a flat ptr on this substrate). More than a few lines (exposes a kernel flag address to user space). Ground vs RBIL INT 21/AH=34h; check spec/int21h_register.json lists 34h (recognized, undispatched).
notes: Surfaced WL-0039 Wave 1 (bo40). Companion to bo40/KEEP.

title: EXEC: honor a caller-provided env block at an arbitrary address (currently degraded to inherit-empty)
description: do_exec sanitizes env_block to {0, ENV_BLOCK}: a real-DOS program that passes its OWN environment segment via AH=4Bh has that env DEGRADED to inherit-empty (we only honor the shell's ENV_BLOCK region). To fully support arbitrary callers, copy/validate the caller's env block from its pointer into the child's env. Low priority -- no current InitechOS caller except the shell passes an env, and the shell uses ENV_BLOCK.

title: Tranche-F shell polish: wildcard COPY + DEL *.* confirm prompt

title: In-emulator env-inheritance leg: child .COM echoes an inherited var over serial (1i0x follow-up)
description: Tranche E inc-3 proved env inheritance on the HOST oracles (test_exec/test_loader) + the empty-env emu path (test-program). The end-to-end populated-env leg -- a baked child .COM that reads its PSP env_seg:0 block and echoes an inherited variable (e.g. PATH) over serial, asserted by an emu gate -- is the remaining proof. Also: parent_psp_linear is still 0 (child cannot walk to COMMAND.COM's PSP) -- a separate future item.

Refactor to draw flow_state+memvar arena from pal->alloc, removing the static registry. Schedule AFTER S8.2 ships so it cannot destabilize integration. Touches a load-bearing file 6+ interp gates link.
acceptance_criteria: all interp gates green; engine static BSS ~0; no behavior change.

title: Kernel x87 FPU init (CR0.EM=0/MP=1/NE=1 + FNINIT + deterministic CW) for SAMIR numeric-on-target
description: The SAMIR engine emits pervasive x87 (fldl/fistpl/fdiv/fcomi/fldcw); the kernel has no FPU init and #NM(7)/#MF(16) route to panic, so any SAMIR numeric op would PC LOAD LETTER. Add FPU bring-up in the boot/kernel path: clear CR0.EM, set CR0.MP+NE, FNINIT, load a deterministic control word (Rule 11). Cooperative single-task => no lazy FPU context-switch needed. Enables S8.1/S8.2 (ax9.1/ax9.2). New InitechDOS work authorized by operator 2026-06-19. ADR-by-committee will fix the exact sequence + hardware.json line.
acceptance_criteria: kernel inits x87 at boot; a freestanding x87 op runs without #NM/#MF panic under QEMU+Bochs; deterministic CW; mutation-proven (skip-init -> the op panics RED).
notes: DEFERRED by ADR-0009 DEC-01 to post-M6 end-state. M6 SAMIR uses SOFT-FLOAT (no kernel FPU). Kernel x87-ready lands only when FLAIR/Turbo-Initech want hardware FP. Not on the SAMIR-in-InitechOS push.
