title: Defensive: conin_cooked_line should bound against a non-terminating BOUND conin source (not just unbound)
description: Adversarial review of x8fs flagged that the host-hang guard covers the NO-source case (g_conin_get==NULL) but not a bound-but-broken source returning 0x00 forever -> conin_cooked_line would spin. ASSESSED NOT CURRENTLY REACHABLE: the kernel binds the real keyboard (blocks for real keys, never a 0x00 stream) and the host mock (test_conin.c:129-151) FATALs on underflow ('can never hang'). So this is latent fragility, not a live defect. Defensive hardening: treat a sustained no-progress condition (e.g. repeated 0x00 with a full buffer) as EOF, or have host mocks return -1/EOF on exhaustion and handle it. Low priority -- the real (no-source) hang that cost a session is already fixed (commit ddef39a).

title: DEC-16 Fork A ON-widening: check ^C on every INT 21h call when BREAK=ON (not just CON family)
description: 4tw landed the CON-family ^C check-point (always active). DEC-16 Fork A 'C-6': when BREAK=ON, DOS widens the ^C check to every INT 21h call (disk I/O etc.), not just CON char input. Add the BREAK-gated wide check-points. From initech-4tw / DEC-16 Sec 3.3.

title: FAT16 AH=36h GET-FREE-SPACE walks all clusters through the 2-sector FAT window (O(clusters) device reads)
description: d27i's fat_freespace on FAT16 is correct (cache amortizes consecutive clusters) but touches every cluster one FAT sector at a time. If AH=36h latency matters on a period HDD, use a larger free-count window or a sequential full-FAT-sector scan. From initech-d27i followup.

title: AH=3Fh on AUX/PRN read returns EOF (no serial/parallel input driver)
description: x8fs cooked-reads only dev_id==SFT_DEV_CON; AUX(COM1)/PRN(LPT1) reads fall through to 0-byte EOF (no driver). Pre-existing limitation, documented. From initech-x8fs followup.

title: AH=3Fh on CON: buffer the over-long line remainder for the next read (KB Q113058)
description: x8fs cooked CON read handles the common case count>=line+CR+LF; real DOS leaves the unread tail of an over-long line in an internal CON buffer for the next 3Fh read. From initech-x8fs followup.

title: spec/dbf_normalization.json: locked meaningful-vs-normalize .dbf/.dbt/.ndx byte map (round-trip contract)
description: Realize PRD §6.6 'meaningful bytes' clause as LOCKED spec-data (CLAUDE.md Rule 8) so the initech-17n round-trip oracle consumes the normalization mask as DATA, not prose. Captures which .dbf/.dbt/.ndx bytes must round-trip byte-exact (version byte, counts, field descriptors name/type/len/dec, 0x0D terminator, all record bytes, memo content+pointer) vs which spans get masked to a sentinel before diffing (last-update date 0x01-0x03, language-driver 0x1D, reserved runs, per-descriptor data-address, trailing 0x1A, deleted-record payload policy, .dbt block tails, .mdx dates+garbage). Spec'd in docs/research/dbase-ground-truth.md sec 2 + 4.4. Lock + mutation-prove (flip one mask cell -> a passing round-trip must go RED).
design: Mechanical recipe: parse both files to a canonical struct; value-level diff for data; PLUS a masked raw memcmp (zero the NORMALIZE ranges both sides) to catch structural drift; bidirectional (SAMIR-writes/ref-reads AND ref-writes/SAMIR-reads). House JSON conventions: _comment provenance + status/ratification line.

title: test-fat-corrupt-fuzz scratch-image mint flake under concurrent/stale-build runs (Rule 11)
description: Both the dao and z01 adversarial verifiers observed test-fat-corrupt-fuzz flaking (scratch-image mint race / stale build/ artifacts) when run concurrently with other gates or over a stale build; it is GREEN in a quiet serial 'make clean && make test'. Same class as the 8403 emu serial-capture race (initech-fgdz). Harden the scratch-image mint to be reproducible under concurrent/incremental runs (unique temp names, or a make-clean guard). Not blocking (green the way 'make test' runs serially).

title: nmpo host oracle leg (e) pins 0x0003 from a mock limitation, not real subdir-create behavior
description: Adversarial review of initech-nmpo: the strengthened host CREATNEW oracle leg (e) asserts 0x0003 for an empty/path-component case, but that value comes from a mock-backend limitation rather than real subdir-create behavior. The end-to-end FAT12 gate (test-nmpo) pins the real path, but leg (e)'s 0x0003 is a mock artifact. Either drive it through the real backend or annotate that leg as mock-shaped so a future reader doesn't treat it as ground truth. P3, non-blocking.

title: lpf3 fault-rollback oracle coverage gaps: mkdir set_entry-EOC-fail leg uncovered; scenario [A] on-disk asserts always-true on fault path
description: Adversarial review of initech-lpf3: the new fault-injection gate (test-fat-fault-rollback, 35 checks) leaves two gaps. (1) The fat12_mkdir set_entry-EOC-fail rollback leg (fat12.c ~2916-2926) has no DIRECT oracle (only flush-fail post-grow + create/write are driven). (2) In scenario [A] the on-disk + dir-entry assertions are always-true on the fault path -- only the in-memory free-count is the load-bearing pin; strengthen so the on-disk state is actually exercised. Also scenario [C] write-count arithmetic silently assumes num_fats==2 (add a guard if geometry changes). Strengthen the oracle to drive the EOC-fail leg + make [A]'s on-disk assertions load-bearing.

title: IOCTL AH=44h fidelity (4nbn): AL=08 takes BL=drive not a handle; AL=01 asserts an invented DX output
description: Adversarial review of initech-4nbn (both P3, benign): (1) real DOS AX=4408h (Is-Block-Device-Changeable) takes BL=drive-number on a BLOCK device, not an EBX/JFT handle; our model resolves EBX as a JFT handle and always returns AX=0x0001/CF=1 (not-applicable, since the SFT exposes no block-device handle). Documented in spec, but record as an intentional model deviation or add the BL=drive shape if a consumer needs it. (2) AL=01h Set-Device-Info is modeled as a no-op success that echoes DX=0x80D3, but real DOS 4401h does not define DX as an output -- the oracle over-asserts an invented return (WL-0025 'oracle asserts a value the contract doesn't produce', benign). Relax the AL=01 oracle to not pin DX, or document the choice.

title: test-absdisk-emu (8403) serial-capture race: flaky under concurrent QEMU relaunch (Rule 11 reproducibility)
description: Adversarial review of initech-8403: the new in-emulator gate test-absdisk-emu passed deterministically in a quiet serial 'make test' (5/5 checks: ABSDISK-BIND-OK/ABS-W26/R25/RT=OK/exit rc=0) but flaked 0/8 under rapid back-to-back QEMU relaunches racing other QEMU instances (serial_len=0, cpu_reset, EIP=0 -- guest never executes past power-on reset). test-vect (which it mirrors) is rock-solid 5/5 under the same load. Harden the serial-capture: mirror test-vect's proven-stable serial-flush/wait, and add a guard that fails with a distinct 'serial not captured (harness race) -- retry' message vs a genuine marker-missing failure. Not blocking (the gate is green the way 'make test' actually runs it).

title: test-absdisk: add a direct in-emulator int 25h/26h self-test (close the asm-stub coverage gap)
description: Adversarial review of 4mq7: the host oracle drives int25/26_dispatch DIRECTLY; the asm int25_entry/int26_entry -> dispatch -> IRETD path is NOT invoked by any host/emu gate (no in-kernel program issues int 0x25/0x26). It is provably-correct-by-identity to int24_entry (emu-proven via test-vect), so risk is low. Add a BOOT-flavored in-emulator int $0x25/$0x26 self-test (mirroring test-vect for int24) to close the gap directly. P3, not blocking.
close_reason: Landed + adversarially verified in WL-0026 (90 host + 27 emu green). Oracle-hardened (cnvp completeness reorder, 5o6o real MUTANT 8, nmpo test-nmpo-mutant). P3 follow-ups split out: fgdz/jplu/aaan/815i.

title: spec/absdisk_int2526.json: add the bad-buffer (0x0F/0x0C) error row ratified by DEC-15
description: Adversarial review of 4mq7 (2026-06-15): the DEC-15 ADR (line 160) ratifies bad-buffer (NULL / 32-bit wrap, user_buf_ok fail) -> AL=0x0F/AH=0x0C, and the code emits it (oracle-covered by test-absdisk), but the locked spec/absdisk_int2526.json error_codes block omits that row. JSON is silent, not in conflict. Add the row + keep the test-spec[6/6] gate green in lockstep (Rule 8 locked-spec completeness).
close_reason: Landed + adversarially verified in WL-0026 (90 host + 27 emu green). Oracle-hardened (cnvp completeness reorder, 5o6o real MUTANT 8, nmpo test-nmpo-mutant). P3 follow-ups split out: fgdz/jplu/aaan/815i.

title: RENAME AH=56h: add a non-root same-dir rename success leg to test-gnrc
description: Adversarial review of gnrc (2026-06-15): the gnrc oracles exercise rename only with dir_start==0 (root). The non-root same-dir success path (\SUB\A -> \SUB\B) is unverified directly, though the underlying non-root scan/write primitives are proven by zs24/m0bp/chmod/set_time. Add a same-subdir success leg to test-gnrc (mmd ::SUB; mcopy OLD into ::SUB; mren ::SUB/OLD ::SUB/NEW.BAK), mirroring the m0bp nested pattern. P3 coverage; no code fix required (gnrc shipped clean).
close_reason: Landed + adversarially verified in WL-0026 (90 host + 27 emu green). Oracle-hardened (cnvp completeness reorder, 5o6o real MUTANT 8, nmpo test-nmpo-mutant). P3 follow-ups split out: fgdz/jplu/aaan/815i.

title: RENAME AH=56h: cross-directory MOVE (old_dir != new_dir)
description: Deferred from initech-gnrc (which implements SAME-directory rename only and returns 0x0011 NOT_SAME_DEVICE for a cross-dir EDX/EDI pair). Cross-dir move within a drive requires: dest-dir-full growth (fat12_grow_dir), an atomic remove-from-old-dir + add-to-new-dir with rollback on failure, and a .. fixup when the moved entry is itself a directory. Materially larger and higher-risk than same-dir rename. Implement once a consumer (e.g. shell MOVE, or a tool) needs it.

title: CHMOD AH=43h SET: PRM masks/ignores type bits, b53d rejects 0x0005 (intentional fail-loud deviation, operator-approved)
description: Adversarial review of b53d (2026-06-15) + RBIL 4301h: real DOS SET-attributes MASKS/IGNORES a CX with the Directory/VolLabel bit set (does NOT error), and a SET targeting a directory (dir bit cleared in CX) SUCCEEDS changing the other bits. InitechOS b53d instead rejects all these with 0x0005 -- a deliberate fail-loud hardening (Rule 2) the operator EXPLICITLY approved (2026-06-15 Q4 DOS-semantics). Recorded as an intentional Law-4 fidelity deviation; revisit only if a real DOS consumer needs mask-and-succeed. NOTE: GET-on-dir was a SEPARATE unsanctioned over-reject, fixed in the b53d F1 follow-up commit.
close_reason: Landed + adversarially verified in WL-0026 (90 host + 27 emu green). Oracle-hardened (cnvp completeness reorder, 5o6o real MUTANT 8, nmpo test-nmpo-mutant). P3 follow-ups split out: fgdz/jplu/aaan/815i.

title: INT 21h AH=44h IOCTL minors AL=01/06/07/08 (set-info, input/output status, changeable-media)
description: Deferred from initech-ro6c per operator 2026-06-15 (ratify AL=00 get-device-info first). ro6c returns CF=1/AX=0x0001 for AL!=00. Implement AL=01 (set device-info word back), AL=06 (get input status: AL=0xFF ready / 0x00 not-ready), AL=07 (get output status), AL=08 (changeable media, block-device only) as thin SFT-derived answers; ground the bits from the DOS 3.3 PRM.
notes: Adversarial review of ro6c (2026-06-15) found int21.h:63-68 mislabels the device-info bit NAMES for bits 1-4 vs the real MS-DOS char-device contract. Correct layout to ground from the PRM/RBIL: bit0=stdin, bit1=stdout, bit2=NUL, bit3=CLOCK, bit4=special, bit5=raw/binary, bit6=not-EOF-on-input, bit14=IOCTL-strings, bit15=ISDEV. The header currently has ISCIN=bit1/ISCOT=bit2/ISNUL=bit3/ISCLK=bit4 (shifted one position). These macros are DEAD today (CON=0x80D3 literal + ISDEV/STDIN are the only ones referenced) and the shipped VALUE 0x80D3 is correct, so NO behavioral impact -- but this bead MUST correct the bit-name labels (PRM-grounded) before implementing the AL=01/06/07/08 minors against them.
close_reason: Landed + adversarially verified in WL-0026 (90 host + 27 emu green). Oracle-hardened (cnvp completeness reorder, 5o6o real MUTANT 8, nmpo test-nmpo-mutant). P3 follow-ups split out: fgdz/jplu/aaan/815i.

title: Fault-injection blockdev backend (write-fail-at-Nth) to drive fat12 post-grow/own-entry rollbacks RED
description: Surfaced by the m0bp adversarial review + the m0bp agent's own follow-up flag. The blockdev_file harness has no write-fail seam, so the rollback paths in fat12_mkdir (set_entry-EOC-fail, flush-fail post-grow), fat12_create, and fat12_write_file are structurally present but cannot be driven RED by any oracle except via real disk-geometry exhaustion (which only reaches the NO_SPACE path). Add a fault-injecting blockdev backend that fails the Nth write, and oracles that drive each rollback path RED. (The immediate m0bp NO_SPACE leak is fixed separately + geometry-tested.)
close_reason: Landed + adversarially verified in WL-0026 (90 host + 27 emu green). Oracle-hardened (cnvp completeness reorder, 5o6o real MUTANT 8, nmpo test-nmpo-mutant). P3 follow-ups split out: fgdz/jplu/aaan/815i.

title: CREATNEW (AH=5Bh) robustness: assert null/no-backend/exhaustion arms + fail-loud when create() bound but open()==NULL
description: Adversarial review of initech-kji0: (1) the null-path(0x0002)/no-write-backend(0x0005)/SFT-JFT-exhaustion(0x0004) branches of do_creatnew are byte-copies of do_creat but are NOT directly asserted for AH=5Bh, so a future divergence stays green -- add direct AH=5Bh cases. (2) Latent Rule-2 gap: the existence guard at int21.c:1189 is wrapped in an "if g_file->open != 0" test, so a hypothetical backend binding create() but NOT open() would skip the existence check and let create() truncate an existing file with CF clear (opposite of CREATNEW contract). Asymmetric with do_open which treats open==NULL as a hard error. Unreachable today (g_fat_backend binds both fat_open + fat_create) -- harden defensively (panic or hard error, never silent-skip). Also confirm 0x0002 vs 0x0003 for an empty CREATNEW path against a DOS-3.3 golden.
close_reason: Landed + adversarially verified in WL-0026 (90 host + 27 emu green). Oracle-hardened (cnvp completeness reorder, 5o6o real MUTANT 8, nmpo test-nmpo-mutant). P3 follow-ups split out: fgdz/jplu/aaan/815i.

title: do_geterr: dedicated AH=59h classifier arm for 0x0050 ERROR_FILE_EXISTS
description: Surfaced during initech-kji0 (CREATNEW). do_geterr has no explicit arm for 0x0050; it falls to the UNKNOWN/IGNORE bucket. Functionally safe (the dispatch wrapper auto-notes AX=0x0050 into g_last_error and the oracle passes), but a dedicated class/action arm would be tidier. Low priority.

title: Mutation-proof the loader EXEC directory-attr guard + tighten test-zs24-exec-mutant leg A to n==1
description: Polish from zs24 L2 adversarial verification (both lenses clean; these are the only minor/nit items). (1) loader.c load_program_from_fat's directory-attr guard (~491: refuse a DIR-attr leaf as a program -> 0x0002) is correct + closes a latent 'load a directory's bytes as a .COM' hole, but no oracle/mutant exercises it (Rule 6 gap on the only new branch lacking coverage). Add an EXEC-a-directory leg (mint a fixture whose resolved leaf 8.3 name is a directory, EXEC it, assert 0x0002/marker absent) + a -DLOADER_MUTATE_EXEC_NOATTR seam proven RED. (2) test-zs24-exec-mutant leg A asserts only 'n<2' for the GREET marker; tighten to 'n==1' so a future regression that also breaks the CWD-relative bare 'greet' (count 0) is caught. Both low-risk polish; the core subdir-EXEC functionality is fully oracle-proven with 2 biting mutants.

title: FAT12 allocator: free-cluster search hints from prev+1, can falsely report NO_SPACE under fragmentation
description: Lens-2 finding during zs24 L1 verification (pre-existing, NOT introduced by zs24). fat12_grow_dir (fat12.c ~1120) and the file-extend/write paths (fat12.c ~1796, ~2056) call fat12_find_free(vol,fat,fat_len, prev+1), i.e. search for the lowest free cluster AT OR ABOVE the current tail. If all free clusters sit below the tail, allocation returns FAT12_ERR_NO_SPACE even though space exists. Mirrors the established extend convention so it is self-consistent, but a fragmented volume can spuriously fail. Fix uniformly across all three call sites (wrap-around search from cluster 2) with a fuzz case that places free clusters only below the tail. Low priority (floppy/low-fragmentation today).

title: CWD-aware DIR header: builtin_dir should compose 'Directory of A:\SUB' from GETCWD
description: Lens-3 fidelity gap (Law 4) surfaced during ut6d verification. os/milton/command.c builtin_dir (~575) hardcodes 'Directory of A:\' regardless of CWD; inside a subdir real DOS 3.3 prints 'Directory of A:\SUB'. Out of scope for ut6d (MD/RD/CD + prompt). Fix: compose the DIR header from the same cwd_display/GETCWD seam ut6d added. Add an oracle assertion (test-ut6d or test-fs) that DIR inside SUB prints the subdir header.

title: Fix kmain.c canon-drift comment (hourglass is canon; wristwatch is THE BUG)
description: Fix a canon-drift comment in kmain.c so it correctly states the hourglass cursor is canon and the wristwatch is THE BUG (PRD Appendix A). ALREADY DONE this session in commit 4aad132; created here for the record and closed immediately.
close_reason: Done in 4aad132; comment now states hourglass is canon, wristwatch is the bug (PRD Appendix A).

title: Michael Bolton's salami-slicing virus (with the canonical rounding-error bug)
description: The Office Space plot device, built for real and played straight: a program that skims the sub-cent rounding remainder from each transaction in the Initech accounting system (the InitechBase app) and accumulates it. It carries the CANONICAL ROUNDING-ERROR BUG that makes it skim far too much, far too fast (the movie's bug). Must actually work against the in-universe accounting software inside the emulated OS. Self-contained homage targeting ONLY the project's own fictional accounting app. Relates to initech-bf2 (michael_bolton.conf).
close_reason: Wave 21: Bolton salami/rounding virus canon app; too-much-too-fast bug enforced + mutation-proven (a fix breaks the gate); ALL GREEN 174 host

title: Initech accounting system (InitechBase app) with the canonical Year-2000 bug
description: A real, working accounting/payroll application built on InitechBase (the dBASE-alike, SAMIR). Ships with a DELIBERATE, period-authentic Year-2000 bug: 2-digit year storage + comparison, so dates roll over incorrectly at 2000 (00 < 99). The bug must be genuine and demonstrable (the real DOS/dBASE-era Y2K failure mode), played completely straight -- never flagged as a joke. This is the system Michael Bolton's virus targets.
notes: Host canon app is DONE+green (test-canon-y2k). The IN-INITECHOS run (DO <file> + emu gate) is tracked separately as the capstone deepening of S8.2 -- see the new 'CANON in InitechOS' bead.
close_reason: Wave 20: Y2K accounting canon app done + mutation-proven green (172 host). Dep-block is the coarse 586.1->7az/aul epic edge (those epics retain GATED children: transcendentals/committee, Tier-2 mint, etc.); 586.1's own work is complete.

title: A:SETUP install program (period-style installer)
description: The SETUP the README install section refers to: copies system files to the fixed disk, writes CONFIG.SYS/AUTOEXEC.BAT, period install UI. Part of the final straight build.

title: [EPIC] Period-correct physical distribution (the final straight build)
description: The END-STATE artifact that must be 100% straight and never admit anything: 5.25in distribution diskette images + period diskette labels, the printed Installation & User's Guide, box copy, registration card, the Software License Agreement in period form, and a SETUP install program. The 'discovered on floppies, uploaded for posterity' deliverable -- deadpan in-character throughout. Built steadily toward final release; lower priority now.

title: DOS 4.0/5.0 large disk: >32MB FAT16-big + INT 25/26 packet form
description: 32-bit sector addressing for >32MB partitions (DOS 4.0 FAT16-big) and the CX=FFFFh packet form of INT 25h/26h. Builds on FAT16 (z01), partitions (k-mbr), and absolute disk (k-absdisk).

title: DOS 5.0 INT 21h surface: 3306h true-ver, 4B05h, 65xx country, 6Ch ext-open, 69h serial
description: The 4.0/5.0 kernel additions beyond 3.3: AH=3306h true version (SETVER-immune), AH=4B05h SET EXECUTION STATE (task swapper hook), AH=65xx extended-country uppercase/filename/collate tables, AH=6Ch extended open/create, AH=69h disk serial number, AH=38h get/set country. Needs the ADR scope amendment first.

title: LABEL.COM (+ VOL): set/show the volume label
description: Set the 11-byte volume label (boot-sector + root volume-label entry) and show it. VOL display lands with the shell s-misc bead; LABEL writes.

title: Shell built-ins: VOL, VERIFY, BREAK, CTTY, TRUENAME
description: Remaining small internal commands: VOL (show volume label), VERIFY (on/off write-verify), BREAK (Ctrl-Break flag, on k-break33), CTTY (console redirect), TRUENAME (4.0). Low priority cleanup to round out the internal-command set.

title: ATTRIB.COM: view/set R/H/S/A attributes
description: Thin frontend over AH=43h CHMOD (k-chmod43) with wildcard support.

title: DISKCOPY / DISKCOMP: sector-level copy & compare
description: Track/sector copy and compare of whole volumes via INT 25h/26h (k-absdisk).

title: [EPIC] DOS 5.0 coverage beyond ADR-0003 Appendix A (phased; ADR amendment required)
description: Captures the operator's ultimate goal of full DOS 5.0 coverage, parked behind the DOS-3.3 kernel-complete milestone (k-cert). Expanding the controlled INT 21h scope beyond ADR-0003 Appendix A is a deliberate act requiring an ADR-0003 AMENDMENT (a CLAUDE.md stop condition) -- this epic is the home for that decision and the phased work it unlocks. Children: 5.0 INT 21h subfunctions, SHARE/network, large-disk, XMS/UMB, and the 5.0 utility suite.

title: MEM.COM: report the MCB memory map (DOS 4.0)
description: Walk and print the MCB chain (free/used, owners) once the arena is wired (509.6). A 4.0 utility but small and useful for debugging the heap.

title: EDLIN + DEBUG: line editor and the assembler/debugger
description: EDLIN (line-oriented text editor) and DEBUG (examine/assemble/trace). Mostly need handle I/O (already present). DEBUG is a useful in-OS tool ahead of Turbo Initech.

title: FDISK: create/display/activate MBR partitions
description: Partition editor: create primary/extended partitions, set active, display the table. Needs absolute disk (k-absdisk), MBR parse/write (k-mbr), and multi-volume (k-multivol).

title: XCOPY + TREE: recursive copy & directory tree
description: XCOPY (recursive, wildcard, /S) and TREE (visualize the dir hierarchy). Need subdirs (ti8) and wildcards (80k).

title: 8bpp DAC index->seafoam end-to-end oracle (mode-0x13 screendump)
description: Split from bcg.11. The 8bpp path writes only palette indices CONSOLE_BG_IDX(1)/CONSOLE_FG_IDX(15) (console.c) and kmain programs the VGA DAC (kmain.c:548-551) so those render seafoam/light-gray. test_console asserts the indices but nothing verifies the kmain DAC mapping end-to-end; the seafoam screendump gate only runs on the LFB path, not the mode-0x13 fallback. Add a QEMU mode-0x13 DAC/screendump oracle asserting idx1->seafoam RGB, idx15->light-gray after vga_set_dac, mutation-proven by perturbing one DAC channel. Needs mode-0x13 screendump harness infra. Audit: console console-8bpp-dac-no-oracle.
acceptance_criteria: A mode-0x13 emu screendump oracle asserts index 1 renders seafoam and index 15 light-gray; mutation-proven (perturb one DAC channel -> RED); make test green.

title: P3 robustness + test-gap sweep (audit 2026-06-13)
description: Cluster of confirmed P3 findings from the kernel audit, each small, each needs a mutation-proven oracle: (a) do_lseek ignores high half of offset in ECX (int21.c:1069); (b) GETVER leaves BL/CX as caller garbage; (c) console rows==0 cursor underflow (console.c:178-187); (d) console_pack_rgb dead bpp branch (console.c:32-38); (e) ata niEN never set in device-control; (f) fat12 total_clusters vs FAT-coverage check missing at mount (fat12.c:123-138); (g) fat12 write prev+1 find-free quirk (fat12.c:1359); (h) SFT creat-truncate-while-open / close-predefined-handle-leak / open-mode-not-validated / fileio dir-entry-cb maps any rc to NOT_FOUND (fileio_fat.c:91-96); (i) loader nested-EXEC real-guard has no oracle (mock-only); (j) sysinit phase layer no host test (folded into bcg.9). Each item gets a RED->GREEN fix + oracle or an explicit documented WONTFIX with rationale.
acceptance_criteria: Each (a)-(i) either fixed with a mutation-proven oracle or closed with a documented rationale; make test green; no item left as a silent gap.

title: Retire redundant SHELL_IMG + add A:\> prompt screendump gate (k6x cleanup)
description: After initech-k6x made COMMAND.COM the default boot (TRACER_IMG now carries KERNEL_SHELL_BIN), the separate SHELL_IMG/shell_boot.img is redundant (identical content). Cleanup: retire SHELL_IMG + point test-shell at TRACER_IMG; optionally add a screendump gate asserting the A:\> prompt RENDERS on the framebuffer (the visual half of the shell, complementing the serial assertions). Low priority polish.
close_reason: DONE + merged (2330be8). Retired SHELL_IMG/shell_boot.img; test-shell now boots TRACER_IMG (byte-identical KERNEL_SHELL_BIN) + added the A:\> prompt screendump gate (PPM_TEXT_CHECK_BIN). SHELL_NAME preserved. make test GREEN incl. test-shell.

title: Subdirectory traversal not-yet-supported notice is an uncontrolled user-facing diagnostic
description: command.c:530 emits a fail-loud user-facing notice 'Subdirectory traversal not yet supported' that is NOT in the spec/dos_messages.json controlled vocabulary (DEC-13). Surfaced scoping initech-509.1. Either route it through the catalogue (needs a new approved MSG-DOS entry -- a deliberate locked-spec change, Rule 8) or confirm it is debug-only. Low priority; the catalogue is the controlled DIAGNOSTIC vocabulary and this is arguably a deferred-feature notice, but it reaches the user.

title: AH=43h CHMOD + AH=57h GET/SET FILETIME (dir-entry attr/time write-back)
description: With FAT write now present (509.11), implement AH=43h CHMOD (get/set attribute) and AH=57h GET/SET FILE DATE+TIME by patching the dir entry. SET-time needs a clock source (CLOCK$/RTC, initech-509.7). Currently dir-entry mtime/mdate are written as a fixed deterministic 0 (Rule 11). Surfaced building initech-509.11.

title: PREREQ: provision i686-elf-gcc cross-toolchain (ADR-0002)
description: ADR-0002 mandates i686-elf cross-compilation for the OS. i686-elf-gcc/ld/objcopy are NOT installed (host gcc -m32 used so far for factory tests). Build/install the cross-toolchain; set CC_KERNEL=i686-elf-gcc for OS image targets. Likely needs operator/sudo or a build script. Surfaced by the ADR-0003 conformance assessment.
acceptance_criteria: i686-elf-gcc/ld/objcopy on PATH; OS image targets compile with the cross-toolchain.
notes: DEFERRED per operator 2026-06-08 (CDR-0001): interim toolchain = host gcc -m32 -ffreestanding -nostdlib + nasm. i686-elf to be provisioned (from-source build script) when dev moves to a more capable device. NO LONGER blocks the DOS path. Closing this bead closes CDR-0001.

title: Author + ratify ADR-0007 (Turbo Initech: Pascal subset, single-pass codegen, self-host fixpoint, DDC, IDE)
description: PRD §1.2/§6.7/§7/§11 and CLAUDE.md now lean on ADR-0007 for the Pascal/self-host scope, but ADR-0007 is marked pending in ADR-0003 §9. Author it in house style to close the citation loop: Pascal subset, single-pass stack-machine codegen, the Phi fixed point (K2==K3), DDC, the Turbo-Vision IDE, and the seed (initech-znb) as its genesis. Surfaced during the ADR-0003 doc reconciliation.
acceptance_criteria: ADR-0007 ratified and filed in docs/adr; PRD/CLAUDE citations resolve to a real document.

title: Harness: requested --screendump silently skipped if guest exits before QMP connects
description: qemu_run() reports screendump_taken=0 but still ok=1 when --screendump is requested and the guest clean-exits (e.g. via isa-debug-exit) before the QMP socket handshake completes (race seen on the fast serial_hello fixture). Works fine vs a live/looping guest (verified vs hang fixture -> 720x400 PPM). For the tracer keystone (f8v.4) the guest loops so it's fine, but the harness should either fail loud when a requested screendump wasn't produced, or order screendump before the guest can exit. Surfaced verifying initech-f2s.
acceptance_criteria: When --screendump is requested, harness either produces the PPM or reports ok=0 (no silent skip).

title: Spec michael_bolton.conf: location in file map + format for PROGRESS_BAR_RATE
description: PRD Appendix B calls PROGRESS_BAR_RATE 'the single most important user-facing parameter' but neither PRD nor CLAUDE.md says WHERE michael_bolton.conf lives (FAT image? baked initrd? spec/?) or its format. Decide and lock it in spec/ before the FILE COPY dialog (m5-5). Surfaced by the repo-skeleton agent (initech-tse).
acceptance_criteria: michael_bolton.conf location + format documented in spec/ and referenced by the FILE COPY dialog issue.

title: @row,col SAY..GET../READ full-screen forms (CP437/Turbo-Vision)
description: Full-screen form engine with authentic CP437 / Turbo-Vision rendering. PRD §6.6.

title: Differential oracle vs real dBASE + interoperable round-trips
description: Generate xBase programs, run both, diff (PRD §6.6). Round-trips compared on meaningful bytes (header date/reserved/lang-driver normalized). Mutation-prove goldens.
design: Per brief sec 7. THREE oracles mirroring harness/diff/fat_diff: (1) round-trip (test-dbase-roundtrip) bidirectional, diffed on MEANINGFUL bytes via value-decode + masked memcmp over the NORMALIZE ranges; (2) differential program-output (test-dbase-diff) over a shared .prg corpus gated 100% (PRD 8); (3) coercion fuzzer (test-xbase-coercion). Reference tooling: apt python3-dbf (Furman, R/W+memos, NO indexes) + python3-dbfread (RO) + an AUTHORED dbf_ref.py for .ndx/.mdx (no host index lib exists) + real dBASE under DOSBox-X/86Box for authenticity/golden-minting. Consumes the locked spec/dbf_normalization.json byte-map.

title: Emit freestanding kernel + MZ .EXE app outputs
description: Targets: freestanding flat binary (kernel) and MZ .EXE (apps). PRD §5, §6.7.
notes: RE-SCOPE (ADR-0003 DEC-08): emit flat binary output ONLY for the current release; MZ .EXE deferred.

title: Reproducible-build discipline (no timestamps, deterministic order)
description: No timestamps, deterministic symbol ordering, stable layout — prerequisite for the §7 fixpoint. PRD §7, Rule 11.

title: M6 — InitechBase (SAMIR): dBASE-alike that really works
description: .dbf/.mdx I/O, xBase interpreter, @SAY/GET forms, in a text-console window inside the Mac desktop. Differential vs real dBASE; interoperable round-trips. PRD §6.6, §11 M6.

title: Stack-machine x86 codegen (no optimizer/allocator)
description: Expression evaluation to x86 with fixed register conventions; no optimizer, no register allocator (small and sufficient). PRD §6.7.

title: Differential vs Free Pascal on the shared subset (mutation-proven)
description: Compile a corpus with Turbo Initech and fpc, run both, diff outputs. Mutation-prove the corpus catches miscompiles. PRD §6.7.

title: Dot-prompt interpreter (USE/APPEND/LIST/LOCATE/SEEK/SET/DO WHILE/IF/REPLACE)
description: Tree-walking interpreter for the dot-prompt subset. PRD §6.6.
design: Per brief sec 5. The expression EVALUATOR is the load-bearing, separately-fuzzable core shared by REPLACE..WITH, LOCATE FOR, IF/DO-WHILE guards, and index key-expressions -- treat as a clean sub-module (gmo's coercion table targets it). Lock the command surface BEYOND the named subset as data before 586.1 starts (DELETE/RECALL/PACK lifecycle, SUM/TOTAL aggregates, STORE/= assignment, CREATE/MODIFY STRUCTURE) to prevent verb creep.

title: .ndx/.mdx B+-tree indexes
description: B+-tree .ndx/.mdx index read/write supporting SEEK/SET ORDER. PRD §6.6.
design: Per brief sec 4. SEQUENCE: .ndx FIRST (node layout fully documented + corroborated by 3 sources -> byte-identical achievable, deterministic, lowest-risk), then .mdx. .mdx node-body internals are UNDOCUMENTED (CDX != MDX) -> defer write/auto-maintenance, reverse the node layout from a real dBASE IV golden, prefer BEHAVIORAL (not byte-identical) round-trip where allocation policy varies. Stop-condition: never infer .mdx nodes from the .cdx analogy.

title: Rebuild the whole OS with seed-built Turbo Initech (cross, on host)
description: Use Turbo Initech (built by the seed) to recompile the entire OS on the host. PRD §11 M7.
notes: RE-SCOPE (PNC-1 Option A): the C kernel is rebuilt by the factory cross-toolchain (i686-elf), NOT by Turbo Initech. The self-host fixpoint (K2==K3) and the 'compile from inside the OS' finale apply to Turbo Initech + Pascal user-space apps, not the C kernel.

title: .dbf reader/writer (C/N/D/L/M header) + .dbt memos
description: Read/write the documented .dbf header and field types C/N/D/L/M; .dbt memo files. PRD §6.6.
design: Per docs/research/dbase-ground-truth.md (brief). SPLIT GUIDANCE: .dbf and .dbt are distinct formats -- give .dbt its own round-trip oracle. .dbt has TWO incompatible block dialects selected by the .dbf version byte (III+ 0x83 = 0x1A1A-terminated, IV 0x8B = FFFF0800 8-byte length header); block-0 next-block-pointer ENDIANNESS is a documented wild contradiction -> ORACLE-RESOLVE (mint from real dBASE, assert), do not guess. Brief sec 1-3.

title: Run as a character-mode text-console window in the desktop
description: Host InitechBase as an 80×25 text-console window inside the Mac desktop — the DOS-box-in-the-GUI made concrete. M6 gate. PRD §6.6.

title: Pascal front end (single-pass): lexer, parser, units, types
description: Single-pass front end for the Turbo-Pascal-flavoured subset: pointer, record, units. PRD §6.7.

title: xBase coercion table (locked data) + evaluator fuzzer
description: Capture string/number/date/logical + SET EXACT coercion as spec/xbase_coercion.json; fuzz the evaluator against the real interpreter. PRD §6.6.
design: Per brief sec 5.6 (proposed spec/xbase_coercion.json schema -- TWO axes: operator_coercion (lhs,op,rhs)->result|error|rule AND assignment_coercion (field,expr)->ok|truncate|overflow|error; SET_EXACT/SET_NEAR/CP437 modes). HAZARD (High): C+N is an ERROR in III+/IV -- the modern dbase.com PLUS '+' doc documents auto-stringification that did NOT exist; lock against the dBASE IV Language Reference, fuzz-confirm C+N=error before ratifying. Other weird cells the fuzzer MUST hit: =-begins-with-directional (EXACT OFF), ==-ignores-switch, $-empty->.F., D-D->N, blank-date-high.

title: inline asm support
description: Pascal-with-inline-asm for systems code (period-honest). PRD §6.7.

title: M7 — Turbo Initech (TPS), seed-built compiler
description: Pascal subset, single-pass codegen, freestanding output; rebuild the whole OS with it (cross, on host). Differential vs Free Pascal. PRD §6.7, §11 M7.

title: M4 — Toolbox (FLAIR): Window/Menu/Control/Event/Dialog + desktop chrome
description: Window/Menu/Control/Event/Dialog managers; desktop; live pull-down menus (incl. Photoshop-exact). Reproduce the frame's chrome at target fidelity. PRD §6.3, §11 M4.

title: M3 — ATKINSON region engine + GUI primitives
description: Region engine (C property suite green), blitting, Chicago/Geneva fonts, hourglass cursor. Draw a System-7 window, drag it with correct clipping. PRD §6.2, §11 M3.

title: Confirm DOS 3.3 boot default BREAK=ON against an 86Box golden (DEC-16 C-7)
description: er3h/4tw set g_break_flag default ON per DEC-16 Sec 3.3 (DOS 3.3 PRM/RBIL). DEC-16 flags C-7: confirm the boot default against a real 86Box dBASE/DOS golden; editorial-erratum flippable to OFF if a golden disagrees. Low priority -- captured in code comment. From initech-er3h.

title: TPS Report Generator: command-line report utility (must EXIT FLAIR to the prompt; genuinely uses the vestigial FCB API)
description: A real, working CP/M-heritage program that uses ONLY the legacy FCB calls (INT 21h 0Fh-24h) -- no handle API. Exists to exercise and justify the vestigial FCB API (initech-509.9): the canonical 'feature that looks like a joke but actually works'. Backburnered with FCB. Acceptance: runs under InitechDOS, performs a real FCB open/read/write/close+find round-trip, output verifies against reference DOS.
notes: Operator 2026-06-14: the TPS Report Generator is the FLAGSHIP use case for vestigial features. It MUST be run from the DOS command prompt -- the user must EXIT the FLAIR GUI to run it (authentic corporate tedium). It MUST actually work, and MUST genuinely require the legacy FCB API (INT 21h 0Fh-24h, initech-509.9) or a similar vestigial mechanism. The canonical 'looks like a joke, actually works' artifact and the in-universe reason FCB exists. (Office Space TPS reports.)

title: DOS 5.0 utility suite: EDIT, QBASIC, DOSKEY, UNDELETE, UNFORMAT, SETVER, HELP, EXPAND, MIRROR, DOSSHELL
description: The 5.0 usability/recovery suite. EDIT (full-screen editor) + QBASIC, DOSKEY (history/macros), UNDELETE/UNFORMAT/MIRROR (recovery), SETVER (version table), HELP, EXPAND, and DOSSHELL. Several (EDIT/QBASIC/DOSSHELL) want the FLAIR Toolbox; recovery tools want the FS internals. Phase last.

title: DOS 5.0 memory: HMA/UMB/XMS (HIMEM/EMM386) + LOADHIGH + DOS=HIGH,UMB
description: Upper-memory and high-memory management: an XMS provider (HIMEM.SYS, INT 2Fh AX=4300), UMB linking, DOS=HIGH,UMB, and LOADHIGH/LH to load TSRs/drivers high. Large; depends on the resident-block model (k-tsr31) and a real device chain (509.7). Note: the OS is 32-bit flat (ADR-0001) so this is in-universe personality, not literal real-mode segmentation.

title: DOS 5.0 SHARE/record-locking (5Ch) + network redirector (5D-5F)
description: AH=5Ch lock/unlock file region + SHARE semantics, and the 5D-5F server/redirector/assign-list family over INT 2Fh. Currently deliberately out of scope (north-star). Revisit only under the ADR amendment; large surface, low north-star value.

title: FCB legacy file API 0Fh-24h (DEC-04, Legacy/vestigial)
description: CP/M-derived FCB API: open/close/find-first/find-next/read/write/delete (0Fh-24h per App A). Vestigial but implemented for conformance (DEC-04) and per the design stance (legacy lovingly preserved).
acceptance_criteria: FCB open/read/write/close + find round-trip a file; differential vs reference DOS FCB behavior.
notes:
Operator decision 2026-06-14: FCB stays REQUIRED (design stance -- blandest corporate OS keeps every vestigial structure, implemented IN FULL; corporate software never deletes) but is BACKBURNERED behind kernel-complete + utils + GUI/dBASE. It does NOT block initech-40oq (kernel feature-complete cert). North-star (Turbo Initech, InitechBase) uses the handle API, not FCB. Finishing 509.9 later upgrades the kernel to literal-total Appendix-A 100%.
THEMATIC CORE of the recursive joke (operator 2026-06-14): FCB is the canonical 'vestigial dead code that actually works' -- legit-looking legacy for the one weird DOS 1.0-era program. Must eventually be built IN FULL (still backburnered), AND a companion DOS 1.0-era utility that genuinely requires the FCB API must be built to exercise it (see the FCB-utility bead).

title: Seed lexer: string-literal scratch pool wraps at LEXER_STRBUF_CAP (1024)
description: seed/lexer.c decodes string literals into a fixed slice pool; a single literal longer than LEXER_STRBUF_CAP, or many live at once, can wrap/reject mid-decode. Fine for the current subset; a real lexer should grow or arena-back string storage. Surfaced during initech-znb Step A.
acceptance_criteria: Long or many string literals decode without truncation or aliasing.

title: S2 — Sound: PC-speaker / Sound Blaster boot chime
description: Mac-bong pastiche boot chime. PRD §13 S2.

title: Stretch / Easter eggs (S1 VibeMode, S2 Sound, S3 real-iron)
description: Optional, off by default. PRD §13.

title: S3 — Real-iron boot on period hardware
description: Custom bootloader on an old BIOS ThinkPad or Pi-class target. PRD §13 S3.

title: Port Turbo Initech onto InitechOS (resident; InitechDOS file I/O)
description: Run the resident compiler on InitechOS, doing file I/O through INT-21h syscalls. PRD §6.7, §7.

title: S1 — VibeMode: unimplemented app hallucinated over UART by a host LLM proxy
description: Launching an unimplemented app routes draw/event state over a 16550 UART to a host-side C proxy that calls an LLM returning draw commands. No TCP in kernel. Off by default. PRD §13 S1.

title: Finale demo: boot → IDE → load OS source → Compile → new bootable image
description: The headline demo. PRD §11 M8.

title: Compile the compiler from inside the OS (K2 = K1(src))
description: Hosted on InitechOS, the resident compiler rebuilds itself. PRD §7.

title: DDC: diverse double-compilation against an independent seed
description: Compile src with an independent seed compiler and check the resulting K* matches — the trusting-trust antidote. PRD §7.

title: Turbo-Vision blue IDE: editor + compile + run
description: The Turbo-Vision blue-screen IDE as a resident app: editor, Compile, Run. PRD §6.7.

title: M8 — Resident IDE + self-host (the finale)
description: Port Turbo Initech onto InitechOS (blue IDE, InitechDOS file I/O); compile the compiler from inside the OS; verify bit-identical two-stage fixpoint. PRD §7, §11 M8.

title: Two-stage fixpoint: assert K2 == K3 bit-identical
description: K3 = K2(src); require K2 == K3 byte-for-byte (the self-reproduction certificate). PRD §7.

value: beads gotcha: a feature/task CANNOT depend-on (be blocked-by) an EPIC -- 'bd dep add <task> <epic>' errors 'tasks can only block other tasks, not epics'. Epic membership is the parent-child dep TYPE, not a blocks edge: 'bd dep add <child> <epic> -t parent-child'. Epic-blocks-epic IS allowed. Also: 'bd label add <id...> <label>' is IDs-first-label-last; 'bd epic' only has status/close-eligible (no add-member).

Hardware-era note (operator 2026-06-21): the dBASE/Lotus/WordPerfect -> Office Space era is closer to 486/Pentium than 386; a 486+ minimum is period-defensible for the FINAL InitechOS. ADR-0001 says 386+. Revisit for PRD/north-star (bead filed). Key delta: 486DX on-die x87 FPU vs SAMIR soft-float (ADR-0009). Not blocking current work.

INITECH COLOR CANON (operator, 2026-06-21, DEFINITIVE): The signature 'Initech teal' = VIC-20 cyan (hardware colour 3). SCREEN/phosphor value = #8DDCDC (operator motion read H180 S53 L71; alt VIC decodes #85D4DC Lospec / #87D6DD azulianblue -- prefer the NTSC 6560 / PAL 6561 decode if matching a real VIC). PIGMENT value = #57B19F for PRINTED PROPS ONLY (box/manuals/floppy labels, pre-CRT); the composite/CRT shader carries pigment toward the screen cyan. PALETTE RULE for FLAIR: System 7 (later System 8/Platinum) colours are GOLDEN, graded vs the system7-decomp goldens, EXCEPT (1) replace ALL System-7 LAVENDER tinge (#DADAFF/#CCCCFF/#B3B3DA/#8787B3/#333366 -- the 3D bevel/groove highlights) with Initech teal #8DDCDC (or closest), AND (2) the DESKTOP BACKGROUND = #8DDCDC. Use Win 3.1 colours (win31-decomp: BTNFACE #C0C0C0, navy #000080, etc.) for the additional Windows/Photoshop-side chrome bits. This teal is what gives InitechOS its distinctive character. SUPERSEDES ADR-0004 OD-4 (seafoam #6FA08E -> Initech teal #8DDCDC for the desktop). The preview.webp-sampled palette.h chrome values are NON-canonical (muddy CRT-photo samples).

InitechOS ADR PROCESS (operator directive 2026-06-14): every ADR must be designed by a COMMITTEE of subagents role-playing corporate functions -- compliance, engineer, corporate governance, manager, QA, legal, etc. The designed-by-committee tedium IS the point; it yields authentically bland, over-deliberated decisions in Initech house style. Mechanism: a Workflow with one agent per role that deliberates/argues, then a synthesis agent writes the ADR. Applies to upcoming ADRs (directory-chain design; the DOS 5.0 scope expansion, which also needs an ADR-0003 Appendix-A amendment).

InitechOS FOUND-FOOTAGE / CLEAN-ROOM AESTHETIC (operator 2026-06-14): the project should increasingly evoke 'found footage' -- going back through the git history should read as if the operator is CLEAN-ROOMING a real Initech OS they FOUND somewhere (reconstruction/archaeology), not greenfield invention. Apply going forward to the VOICE of dev artifacts: commit messages, worklogs, and ADRs increasingly framed as recovery/reconstruction of a discovered artifact. The transparent AI/agent history is consistent -- the agents ARE the clean-room tooling. Builds steadily; do NOT retroactively rewrite history. This connects the transparent dev journey to the final-straight 'discovered on floppies' build.

KERNEL_SECTORS (the kernel's reserved disk window) is defined in TWO places that MUST be kept in sync: the Makefile (KERNEL_SECTORS) and os/boot/stage2.asm (the kernel load count). Bumped 64->80 (40KiB) in 509.11 when the BOOT_SHELL kernel image outgrew 32KiB. If the kernel grows, bump BOTH; the size guard fails loud but a stale truncated kernel_*.bin produces garbage. Kernel loads at 0x10000 and must end below PROGRAM_BASE=0x20000.

test-fat (initech-5cu) is the FAT12 differential oracle: our reader (os/milton/fat12.c via harness/diff/fat_diff/fat_dump.c) vs TWO refs -- mtools (mdir/mcopy) + independent python3 (harness/diff/fat_diff/fat12_ref.py). Assertions: (A) names+sizes triple-agree (our==python==normalized mdir), (B) per-file content our==mcopy==python, (C) the 3 fat12 unit oracles, (D) tools present else fail-loud. Fixtures: hello/second/chain/empty(0B,start_cluster 0)/block.bin(1024B=exact 2-cluster, full last cluster). FINDING: the 0x00-sentinel-stop vs skip distinction is NOT exercised by a natural minted image (trailing dir entries are all 0x00 either way) -- only the SYNTHETIC garbage-after-sentinel case in test_fat12_dir.c catches a sentinel mutation. So the differential image alone underconstrains dir-scan termination; keep the synthetic unit case.

InitechOS BUILD-TRANSPARENCY PRINCIPLE (operator 2026-06-14): only the FINAL build must be completely straight -- the physical 5.25in floppies + period stickers/manuals/box, the 'for posterity' release (see the packaging epic). The DEV JOURNEY is intentionally transparent: public repo, AI/agent git history, CLAUDE.md/PRD/worklogs revealing intent, evolution easily discoverable -- ALL FINE AND INTENDED (part of the VibeOS-angle reveal). The joke gets STEADILY BUILT; do NOT hide the dev process or obsess over premature in-character purity. Only the product PRESENTATION LAYER (README, manuals, box, floppy labels, the OS UI) stays deadpan now.

InitechOS DESIGN SOUL (the recursive joke): a from-scratch OS that at FIRST GLANCE is indistinguishable from a real early-90s corporate product (in-universe: pretend it is the OS shown in Office Space; NEVER stated in the artifact). Layered reveal: (1) looks like genuine 90s corporate software; (2) VibeOS angle -- viewer assumes a vibe-coded AI toy that can't be real; (3) surprise: it REALLY boots on 386-era hardware; (4) the software REALLY works -- with deadpan corporate absurdities played straight (InitechCalc pie chart sums to 116%, hourglass cursor, 570- trailing-minus). Aesthetic: distilled 90s office-cubicle tedium, designed-by-committee blandness. Recursive layer: legit-looking joke-ish features that ACTUALLY WORK (canonical: the vestigial FCB API for one weird DOS 1.0 program, which must be built WITH a companion utility). The presentation NEVER admits the joke.
list

FLAIR live compositor clips each window's chrome to w->strucRgn (desktop.c:223 paint_window_chrome(dst,w,w->strucRgn,w->strucRgn)), and the drawer's window-frame rect is region_get_bbox(w->strucRgn) (desktop.c:117). strucRgn == the window bounds rect (window.c region_set_rect). CONSEQUENCE: anything flair_draw_document_window draws OUTSIDE [left,right)x[top,bottom) -- e.g. the documentProc drop shadow at x=right/y=bottom (offset (1,1)) -- is CLIPPED OUT on the booted desktop, even though test-chrome-fidelity (host, unclipped port) grades it GREEN. Naively widening strucRgn to include the shadow band ALSO shifts bbox(strucRgn) -> the frame rect moves (frame to 560, shadow to 561) AND changes occlusion (fronts_union/visible_into) + drag-expose + FindWindow. Real Sys7 CalcDoc: struct = content+frame+SHADOW band (window-frame.md Sec 1). The fix (bead initech-9d0e) must DECOUPLE the drawer's frame rect from the clip region. Shares the strucRgn/expose math with the 5l5z event loop.

Host unit tests that stuff a host pointer into an artifact boot_info/uint32_t field (e.g. fake framebuffer/font addr) must allocate in the low 4GiB -- the host has no 32-bit multilib so tests run 64-bit and a truncated pointer segfaults. Use mmap(MAP_32BIT) (see os/milton/test_console.c alloc_low). Artifact code is correct for the 32-bit target; this is a host-test-only concern.

Interrupt foundation (initech-a5a, DONE): IDT at os/milton/idt.{h,c}, exception stubs os/milton/isr.asm, fail-loud panic os/milton/panic.c, PIC os/milton/pic.{h,c}. PIC remapped master->0x28 slave->0x30 (NOT 0x20/0x28) per initech-1f9 to keep vector 0x21 free for int 0x21. int_frame_t order (low addr first) = edi,esi,ebp,esp_dummy,ebx,edx,ecx,eax (pushad), ds,es,fs,gs (stub pushes gs,fs,es,ds), vector, err_code, eip,cs,eflags (CPU). Error-code vectors 8,10,11,12,13,14,17,21 omit the dummy-0. idt_install_trap(vec,handler) is the SEAM for the NEXT task (int 0x21 trap gate 0x8F at 0x21). Kernel keeps IF=0 (no sti); all IRQs masked. Live boot self-test: int 0x80 trap gate -> IDT-SELFTEST-OK/IDT-RESUMED on serial. Gates: make test-idt (host encode+frame offsets), test-idt-mutant (Rule 6), test-panic (#DE caught, triple_fault=0). Markers PIC/IDT added to test-boot+test-tracer-boot.

ORCHESTRATION (operator cadence): delegated coding subagents must NOT git commit/push -- the ORCHESTRATOR owns grading (emu+Bochs+full clean aggregate) and commit-per-step. In the 54nw chrome element a sonnet subagent committed AND pushed a host-green change (271 host gates) that was EMU-RED (test-flair-desktop) -- its ppm_flair_check amendment asserted a live drop-shadow pixel the OS does not actually paint (strucRgn clip). Caught only by the orchestrator re-running the FULL clean aggregate (the HANDOFF hard lesson). Fix: instruct subagents to stop at 'host gates green + diff summary' and let the orchestrator grade+commit.

Rule-6 mutation mutants that ELIDE the sole call site of a static helper trip -Werror=unused-function -> the mutant fails to BUILD instead of failing the oracle ASSERTION, which makes 'mutant correctly RED' ambiguous (build error vs. real bite). Fix: annotate the helper with __attribute__((unused)) so the mutant still COMPILES and the oracle bites on the assertion. Seen in os/milton/sft.c (SFT_MUTATE_DUP2_NO_RELEASE elides the only sft_release call).

FLAIR north star EXPANDED 2026-06-27 (ADR-0012): a 4th, lowest-priority commitment (PRD Sec 1.4) -- FLAIR must be a credible early-90s app PLATFORM (Sys7/Win3.1 competitor), not just a faithful desktop. App Contract = ADR-0013 (NOT 'ADR-0010' -- that number shipped as Grading-and-Goldens; 0011 reserved-pending for Phase-6 hosting; 0012=charter, 0013=App Contract). ADR-0013 design: a tenant = FlairApp record of C entry-points + child arena, bound to windows via WindowRecord.refCon; shell inverts WaitNextEvent + demuxes by refCon through a HOST-BUILDABLE Layer-5 dispatcher os/flair/process.{c,h} (flair_app_dispatch) called by BOTH kmain and the test-process oracle (E-D2 single-spine); multi-app co-residency over the existing pump/z-order/heap within flat-32+cooperative+no-isolation. Fork ruled: Initech123/InitechWord = native Toolbox tenants; SAMIR/Turbo Initech = character-mode via the unchanged single-program loader. Accepted shortfalls (NOT silently closed): v8086/real-binary stays out (substitute='Initech versions', re-impl parity not binary compat) + real-iron stays a stretch. New work: epic initech-4e35 (first cut) + initech-49ez (Phase 4.5 services: Resource Mgr/Scrap/TextEdit+List/Standard File/Print Mgr, BEFORE the app suite).

InitechDOS (Base OS, milestone M2, codename MILTON) is the FIRST programme deliverable and milestone, per ADR-0003 + operator direction 2026-06-08. Ratified ADRs are AUTHORITATIVE over the PRD and CLAUDE.md; the beads plan is reconciled to the ADRs, not vice versa. GUI/Toolbox (ADR-0004 pending) is deferred behind the DOS.

Interactive in-emulator oracles inject keystrokes via the harness: qemu_main --keys "d,i,r,ret" (+ --keys-after <serial-marker>) sends QMP send-key qcode events (letters/digits/ret/spc/dot) after the marker. Built in initech-43b (harness/emu/qemu.c qmp_session). DOS commands are injected lowercase; the shell/COMMAND.COM upcases. This is how test-kbd/test-conin/test-shell drive the guest.

OPEN reconciliation (PNC-1): ADR-0002 mandates C via i686-elf cross-compile as the OS implementation language; PRD/CLAUDE.md Law 3 say the artifact is Pascal (a seed Pascal compiler, initech-znb, was already built). Pending operator ruling: OS base = C, Pascal reserved for the Turbo Initech self-host compiler (ADR-0007 pending). Also PNC-2: DEC-08 defers MZ .EXE (flat .COM for now) vs PRD 'MZ decided'.

OPERATOR RULING 2026-06-20 (DOS-3.3 parity push): parity target = 'Appendix-A now, amendments later'. Drive epic initech-bsy to the 40oq capstone using only the CURRENT ADR (KEEP 31h + shell tranches E/F/G/I + FCB-stub); NO ADR amendment for the milestone. COUNTRY/codepage (38h/65h/66h), SHARE/record-locking (5Ch), and INT 2Fh redirector are OUT of the parity milestone and move to a SEPARATE follow-on amendment-gated epic (om2a/ws3x/t1hl). FLAIR is PAUSED (golden masters being minted in sister repos ../system7-decomp + ../win31-decomp).

Bochs boot SOLVED (initech-6pj): the WORKING bochsrc is romimage=/usr/share/bochs/BIOS-bochs-legacy (the 64K legacy BIOS, NOT BIOS-bochs-latest) + vgaromimage=/usr/share/bochs/VGABIOS-lgpl-latest + cpu: model=pentium + vga: extension=vbe. With this combo the tracer boots clean (S1 JMP2 S2, no faults) and INT 10h standard VGA mode 0x13 FULLY works (BDA 0x449=13, framebuffer at 0xA0000 live, readback verified). Why the others fail: BIOS-bochs-LATEST needs CMOV (P6) so it triple-faults #UD on the pentium model, and even with model=p3_katmai it triple-faults on an IDT.limit=0 interrupt in PM during POST (the WL-0014 'IDT.limit=0' fault) -- the latest BIOS is unusable on this Bochs 2.7 build. SeaBIOS bios.bin boots our OS but its vgabios does NOT drive the VGA hardware under Bochs (mode 0x13 AH=0Fh reports 13h but framebuffer writes to 0xA0000 are dropped, read 0xFF; VBE 0x4F00 returns E00). VBE 640x480 LFB is unavailable on Bochs (legacy BIOS: VBE-ENOMODE; latest: VBE-E00) -> the portable answer is a stage2 standard-VGA mode-0x13 fallback + an 8bpp console renderer. Bochs 2.7 here is a DEBUGGER build (drive headless via -rc 'c'); only rfb/term/x display plugins; harness/emu/rfb_unblock.py unblocks the rfb wait.

FLAIR HERESY PURGE -- management decree 2026-06-21 (epic initech-qipc; from heresy-audit workflow wf_7dd9a33b-e7c). 20 candidate heresies adjudicated: 19 upheld, 1 struck. HARD-REVOKE (live anti-oracle, nothing supersedes it): HER-02 ppm_flair_check grades COLOR BY CONSTRUCTION -- expected RGBs come from flair_palette_rgb(), the SAME fn kmain.c:733 paints with, so a wrong palette is invisible (this is what let the 'very wrong' preview.webp palette pass every gate); its 3 mutants are STRUCTURAL only (menubar/modal/z-order), the color limb was NEVER proven to bite (Rule-6 unmet). Fix: re-key color asserts to LIVE decomp diffs + add a palette-VALUE mutant; keep the structural probes. FORMAL-REVOKE-OF-SUPERSEDED (operator overrode in WL-0053 but ADR/code never struck): HER-01 OD-4 seafoam->teal #8DDCDC; HER-03 preview.webp as render source; HER-04 the re30.4-palette-source committee ruling (worklog-level, was never an ADR decision-of-record); HER-05 FO-3/AM-9/FO-F seafoam reconcile; HER-06 the D-8 'canon' HARD-GATE seafoam row (an ENFORCED oracle, more dangerous than OD-4); HER-08 test-palette-seafoam self-check; HER-16 PRD 'seafoam palette' headline; HER-20 HANDOFF seafoam-green claim. AMEND (architecture asserts oracles/obligations the code does not honor): HER-07 chrome.c paints preview.webp pinstripe not the dead decomp #F3F3F3/#969696; HER-09 win31-decomp ZERO Makefile wiring, accents self-checked; HER-11/15/19 SSIM documented-but-unbuilt (make ssim=stub_fail, no harness/ssim.c), cross-emu host-model depends on it; HER-12/17 mech/policy split + region-spine never formalized into ADR-0004/0005, 5 color switches still in the mechanism; HER-13/18 ADR-0005 ratifies ATKINSON mono-heritage QuickDraw, no GDI/HRGN; HER-14 booted OS runs NO FLAIR event loop (WaitNextEvent spine is a static frame; flair_tick_advance zero ISR callers). KEPT (NOT heresy -- adjudicator rejected, category error): HER-10 clut.h is the device-CLUT MECHANISM + the in-repo EXEMPLAR of independent-golden grading (test_clut.c live-diffs ../system7-decomp/goldens/resources/clut_8_rom.bin); it is NOT chrome decoration -- do NOT conflate. These revocations get BAKED INTO THE ADRs in the final authoring step (after the architecture committees, workflow wf_80ad0434-5e1).

FLAIR mouse IRQ12 (FO-6, commit 3835b02): the dual-PIC EOI ORDER is load-bearing -- mouse_irq_handler MUST outb(0xA0,0x20) [SLAVE] THEN outb(0x20,0x20) [MASTER]; a master-only EOI leaves the slave ISR bit set and SILENTLY WEDGES all further IRQ12 (proven by the MASTER_ONLY_EOI mutant: 0 distinct FLAIR-MOUSE). A slave IRQ also needs BOTH the master cascade (IRQ2) AND the slave IRQ12 (bit4) unmasked (pic_unmask_irq12). isr.asm uses  so irq12_entry is an inert stub in the 26 non-flair kernels (mouse.o links only into flair_live) -- keeps them byte-stable. The no-wedge property is asserted by injecting >=2 mouse events and requiring >=2 DISTINCT FLAIR-MOUSE on QEMU; flair_live can't exercise IRQ12 on Bochs (640x480 ENOMODE halts before the loop) -> Bochs strict-PIC coverage is follow-up initech-<probe-bead>.

FLAIR Phase 3 committee ruling (2026-06-21, seats A=Toolbox/imaging + B=kernel/boot+risk; chair-synthesized, no gridlock): GROW the impl to honor the locked grafport.h/imaging.h/drawing_ops.h contract (NOT trim) -- build the GrafPort verb layer (Std rect/rgn/bits dispatched via grafProcs) on the existing blitter_*_clipped, ONE palette module sourced from spec/assets/clut.h (kills the 5 duplicated index->RGB switches + centralizes the scattered bpp depth-conversion), CopyBits+8 src modes+maskRgn+colorize, one offscreen GWorld from the FLAIR heap. SEQUENCING (Seat B, bisectable): B0 kernel-window headroom FIRST -> M3.0 link Managers into KERNEL_OBJS (gated on a link-time _kernel_end<PROGRAM_BASE assert) -> M3.1 SMALLEST live desktop = static shell_render scene+modal FILE COPY to the LFB on the EXISTING cfill path (NOT coupled to the verb refactor), gated by a new test-flair-desktop (QEMU screendump structural-band + Bochs serial differential) in TEST_EMU_GATES. The verb-layer refactor is a PARALLEL workstream OFF the first-pixels critical path, re-greening test-chrome/drag/fb-agree before merge. Input arc in 3 on-metal steps each with a serial marker: L1 tick (flair_tick_advance into pit_irq + HLT yield) -> L2 keyboard (kbd_irq ALSO flair_raw_post, additive) -> L3 mouse IRQ12 (initech-26d, dual-PIC EOI, Bochs-gated). Fidelity = host-model-PER-MODE (AM-6 frozen; NOT cross-emu byte-CRC); land initech-u9gf (Bochs RFB capture) + k8o5.11 (dual-target digest); SSIM guide-not-gate. M4: LAND 86Box (initech-q0gy), do NOT waive DEC-04. VERIFIED RISK R1: kernel window [0x10000,0x38000) headroom is ~61 KiB (_kernel_end=0x28890 measured; memory_map.h comment '~0x1fd20/99KiB' is STALE), FLAIR code ~51 KiB -> linking may bust the window; mitigation pre-authorized = +0x8000 PROGRAM_BASE raise (Rule-8, o0td precedent). See docs/plans/FLAIR-implementation-plan.md Phase 3.

Host oracle hang pattern: conin_get() returns 0 (not -1/EOF) when no CON input source is bound ('host-safe default'). A cooked-line reader that loops until CR (conin_cooked_line, do_buffered_input) will SPIN FOREVER on that 0. Kernel never hits it (keyboard bound at SYSINIT) but ANY host test linking int21.c that reaches a CON read without binding a conin source hangs (found via test_fileio's kji0 CREATNEW mutants after x8fs made AH=3Fh-on-CON a blocking cooked read). Fix: guard conin_cooked_line -- no source bound + no pushback => return EOF (Rule 2: a host oracle must fail loud, never hang). Lesson: per-lane targeted gates can miss a hang that only the FULL aggregate gate exercises -- always run the full make test at integration, with a timeout.

MCB arena (509.6 part 2) placement: the cleanest, LOWEST-RISK option is a per-load DYNAMIC arena over the free tail of the image region -- the loader, after copying the .COM, inits an mcb_arena over [paragraph_align(PROGRAM_IMAGE + image_size) .. ENV_BLOCK(0x5F000)), owner = the program's PSP segment (0x3000). This is the authentic DOS model (program owns memory above its image), needs NO spec/memory_map.h change (avoids the load-bearing PROGRAM_IMAGE_MAX edit), and the arena ceiling 0x5F000 stays below the env block + stack so there is no collision. Wire AH=48/49/4A behind an int21 mem-backend SEAM (like int21_set_exec_backend) so int21.c stays mcb-free and host-testable without adding mcb.c to every test link line; the kernel binds an mcb-backed backend in kmain.c (which already links mcb.c) and does the data_para->DOS-segment conversion ((arena_base>>4)+data_para).

RATIFIED OPERATOR DIRECTIVE (2026-06-21, no relitigation): FLAIR's architecture locks in TWO design choices stolen from the X Window System factoring. (1) MECHANISM/POLICY SPLIT -- the imaging/window MECHANISM (GrafPort draw verbs, blitter, region clip, event/window core) is kept SEPARATE from POLICY/DECORATION (chrome look, control look, dialog look, colors, patterns, metrics). The mechanism is policy-free and takes color/pattern/metric as PARAMETERS; decoration is a layer on top. Neither System 7 nor Win3.1 did this internally; FLAIR does -- it is invisible on screen so it is FREE under Law 4 (fidelity = look-and-feel of the artifact, not source structure) and makes every layer independently oracle-able. (2) REGION ENGINE = THE SHARED SPINE -- os/flair/atkinson/ (scanline inversion-list set algebra) is the SAME machinery behind both QuickDraw Rgn and GDI HRGN; BOTH heritages clip through the ONE engine; NEITHER heritage owns it. This is where the chimera's two bloodlines share bone. WHY: the Office Space frame has a Mac System-7 title bar AND a Photoshop/Windows menu bar on one screen; building two vertical chrome stacks duplicates the imaging engine and drifts (the preview.webp palette bug was a symptom of color-policy smeared into decoration code). Build ONE policy-free mechanism + the two heritages become two DECORATION POLICIES over it. HOW TO APPLY: re30.4 (one palette module, kill the 5 duplicated index->RGB switches) is the FIRST mechanism/policy cut -- extract color into a POLICY layer the mechanism reads from, not a dedup'd lookup baked into chrome/control/dialog. re30.5 GrafPort verbs land the imaging mechanism policy-free. To be formalized as an ADR (amends the ADR-0004/0005 FLAIR Toolbox architecture).

Clock-skew gotcha (env): build/ artifacts can acquire mtimes ~tens of minutes in the FUTURE relative to the system clock, which makes GNU make skip ALL rebuilds (every target looks newer than freshly-edited sources) -> false-green/false-red oracles. Symptom: 'Clock skew detected. Your build may be incomplete.' + a mutation-proof gate (e.g. test-dosmsg-mutant [B]) fails to bite because the intermediate 'make kernel_shell.bin' is a silent no-op. FIX: 'make clean' wipes the future-dated artifacts; rebuild from clean gives clock-consistent mtimes and the gates behave. Always 'make clean' before trusting an incremental oracle run if the skew warning appears.

COMMITTEE RULING 2026-06-20 (MZ .EXE loader, ADR-0003 DEC-08a, RATIFIED no gridlock): InitechMZ = genuine MZ container interpreted FLAT. Load module is 32-bit flat code; relocations are a list of u32 flat offsets, loader ADDs flat load base (PROGRAM_IMAGE 0x30100) to the 32-bit dword at each site. Real header fields honored (e_cp/e_cblp size, e_cparhdr skip, e_minalloc/e_maxalloc -> MCB arena with real teeth + fail-loud-if-insufficient, e_cs:e_ip/e_ss:e_sp as flat offsets). Content-based .COM/.EXE dispatch ('MZ' magic). Tag byte at e_res[0] (0x1C) sentinel distinguishes InitechMZ from a genuine 16-bit DOS MZ; loader PANICS fail-loud on an untagged foreign MZ (never misexecute 16-bit code in 32-bit flat mode). A parse-only genuine-16-bit reader lives in harness/ ONLY (differential oracle, never executed). PSP unchanged; MZ path is a prologue on loader_run_plan. Overlays (AL=03h) deferred. HONESTY: may claim 'runs MZ .EXE compiled for the flat 32-bit InitechOS target'; may NOT claim 'runs genuine DOS .EXEs'.

DESIGN STANCE (governs all naming/structure decisions): The blandness is a deliberate, rigorous design stance. Maximal genericness -> keep every canonical name and every vestigial structure, including ones that stopped meaning anything versions ago. Corporate software never deletes; it accretes and ships the cruft forever with a straight face. InitechDOS is not a parody of DOS -- it is DOS with the soul extracted and the legacy lovingly preserved. Implication: prefer generic heritage-canonical names (ADR-0003 DEC-12); implement vestigial structures (PSP, MCB arena, FCB API) IN FULL; never 'modernize away' legacy.

FLAIR canonical-app priority (operator, 2026-06-20): the SERIOUS era productivity suite comes FIRST among all apps -- it is the proof that FLAIR 'runs all serious apps of the era'. Order: (1) InitechBase = SAMIR, the dBASE-III+-alike, DONE-ISH (runs full-screen in-OS, M6 substantially complete; promote to first windowed tenant); (2) Initech 123 = the Lotus 1-2-3 spreadsheet clone; (3) InitechWord = the WordPerfect clone (NOT WordStar). Games (Doom flat-32 source port, then native 'Initech Mines' for the Minecraft slot) come AFTER this suite. OPEN committee fork at Phase 4 (ADR-0010 app contract): are Initech 123 / InitechWord native FLAIR Toolbox apps or character-mode tenants in the windowed text-console host (initech-ax9.3, 80x25 CP437)? Real Lotus/WordPerfect were character-mode DOS apps and SAMIR already runs character-mode -> argues the text-console host should be pulled EARLY, not last. See docs/plans/FLAIR-implementation-plan.md decision 4.

GOTCHA: 'bd create --graph <file> --dry-run' IGNORES --dry-run and ACTUALLY CREATES the issues (and so does a real run after) -- running it twice double-creates. Also the graph path SILENTLY DROPS the per-node 'deps' and 'parent' fields (they must be wired afterward with 'bd update <id> --parent' and 'bd dep add <blocked> <blocker>'). Dep rule: epics can only block epics, tasks only block tasks (cross-type sequencing uses parent-child or relate, not deps). Always verify bd stats delta == nodes created and spot-check 'bd dep list' after a graph create.

RULED (PNC-1, operator 2026-06-08): InitechOS is initially built in C (i686-elf cross-compile, per ADR-0002). Turbo Initich is the Pascal self-hosting compiler / North Star (ADR-0007, pending), NOT the OS implementation language. Consequence: CLAUDE.md Law 3 ('artifact is Pascal') must be revised -> OS artifact = C; Pascal confined to Turbo Initech. The seed Pascal compiler (initech-znb, built) is re-contextualized as the genesis of Turbo Initech, not the OS bootstrap. PRD §6.7/§14 to update accordingly.

Sister repo ../dbase3-decomp (git, branch master) is the dBASE III PLUS 1.1 reconstruction knowledge base that grounds SAMIR/InitechBase M6. It is spec+ground-truth corpus, NOT code. Target is III+ 1.1 ONLY (NOT plain III, NOT IV). Adapted Laws: bytes win over docs; tag confidence [verified]/[documented]/[oracle-resolves]/[inferred]; mark III-vs-IV deltas. Layout: specs/{file-formats,language,commands,functions,full-screen,reports-labels,environment,runtime}/ (byte-verified specs), re/ (RE+MINT harness), goldens/ (real DBASE.EXE/OVL + 10 .dbf/11 .ndx/etc fixtures, gitignored), oracles/ (Harbour etc, semantic-only NOT byte authority), GAPS.md (open A-S), SOURCES.md. MINT harness is LIVE: dosbox-x runs real DBASE.EXE headless (SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy, inline ON ERROR, timeout -k, SET ALTERNATE capture). Key minted ground truth for SAMIR: .dbf header YY,MM,DD + +1/+2 terminator variants + optional 0x1A EOF; .ndx numeric keys = RAW LE IEEE-754 doubles compared ARITHMETICALLY (no sign-flip), date keys = Julian Day Number as double; C+N is ERROR #9 'Data type mismatch.' (NO auto-stringification -- that is a PLUS/IV feature); SET EXACT default OFF = directional begins-with; SET DECIMALS=2; rounding ties round toward +infinity (STR(2.5,2,0)=3, STR(-2.5,2,0)=-2); 570- trailing-minus is APP-LEVEL not a dBASE PICTURE (native: leading -, @( parens, @X DB, @C CR); 1/0 -> *-fill overflow not error; DTOS absent in III+; SET DATE AMERICAN/CENTURY OFF. The initech-os SAMIR brief docs/research/dbase-ground-truth.md is the narrower oracle-driven SUBSET of this corpus.

Bochs RFB display does NOT render VGA mode 0x13 (initech-6pj/564): even a pristine REAL-MODE fill of mode 0x13 (index 1 everywhere + DAC[1] programmed), halted before the kernel, comes back ALL BLACK over the RFB display plugin (raw framebuffer bytes 0/345600 nonzero; RFB serves the guest as 720x530 true-color 3-3-2). The OS writes the mode-0x13 framebuffer CORRECTLY -- proven 4 independent ways: (1) stage2 real-mode write/readback at 0xA0000 returns the written byte; (2) Bochs debugger write-watchpoint on 0xAF9FF fires (kernel fill reaches the high framebuffer byte); (3) host oracle test-console 8bpp 57 checks; (4) serial shows BANNER-BEGIN/text/BANNER-END through console_putc. So Bochs's RFB plugin (the only headless display in this build) cannot display chained 256-color mode 0x13 -- a Bochs RFB limitation, NOT an InitechOS bug. CONSEQUENCE: the Bochs tri-emulator gate must assert on SERIAL markers + no-triple-fault (resolution/display-independent, the rigorous Rule-5 signal), not a screendump. A visual Bochs screendump of mode 0x13 is not achievable via RFB here; mode 0x12 (640x480x16 planar) MIGHT render on RFB (untested) but needs a planar renderer. /tmp/rfb_capture.py is the (working) RFB framebuffer-capture client.

CANONICAL PALETTE DIRECTIVE (operator, 2026-06-21): FLAIR colors/chrome are GRADED AGAINST and DERIVED FROM the sister-repo goldens+specs we painstakingly built -- ../system7-decomp (specs/chrome, specs/desktop, quickdraw color, the Quadra-650 ROM clut.h=8BitStd, wctb window color table, s7-chrome.md, golden screenshots) for System 7, and ../win31-decomp (specs/gdi/system-colors, specs/chrome/color-scheme, re/win31-color-measurements) for the Win 3.1 accents. The spec/assets/palette.h INITECH_*_RGB values are MEASURED SAMPLES from the low-res preview.webp Office Space MOCK-UP frame -- they are NOT canonical and the initial FLAIR render (seafoam etc.) was WRONG because it used them. clut.h IS already system7-decomp canon (the real ROM CLUT). System 7 (+Win 3.1 accents) is canonical NOW; System 8 / Platinum becomes canonical LATER (era-layered accretion). This SUPERSEDES the committee re30.4-palette-source ruling (which preserved the preview.webp seafoam) -- re30.4 must source from the decomp canon, not palette.h.

OPERATOR DIRECTIVE (2026-06-19) -- FLAIR is the CROWN JEWEL of InitechOS corporate design. Build the FULL GUI on REAL ground truth: real MEASUREMENTS + real REVERSE-ENGINEERING / SOURCE-CODE-AS-SPEC + GOLDEN GENERATION from real era software. Every chrome metric, font strike, control, cursor, layout, and behavior must derive from actual System 7+ / Windows 3.x software run in emulators (Mini vMac / Basilisk II for Mac; dosbox-x for Win 3.1 -- already installed) OR from authoritative spec/RE sources (Inside Macintosh as published spec; open reimplementations/decomps like Advanced Mac Substitute, Executor; Wine/ReactOS GetSystemMetrics tables; Windows SDK / Petzold). Goldens are GENERATED from real software (real screenshots -> locked fixtures -> chrome/SSIM differential), NOT authored off the frame photo. The GUI must be 100 percent usable + functional, and OBVIOUSLY a bland ripoff of System 7 + Windows (the deadpan chimera, mostly-Mac + Win accents + the canon Photoshop menu bar). NO 'looks right' authoring -- Law 1 (ground truth before code) + Law 4 (look like the frame) + port-and-verify TDD against real references. NOTE: this gives the GUI APPEARANCE a real external golden (chrome/fonts/controls/cursors/layout); the ATKINSON region ENGINE internal byte-format still has no golden (QuickDraw region body is unpublished -- ADR-0005). Governs all epic k8o5 FLAIR work. Warrants a fidelity-methodology ADR (proposed ADR-0006) ratified by committee after the ground-truth research lands.

OPERATOR RULING 2026-06-20 (FCB disposition): the 40oq Appendix-A coverage capstone CERTIFIES WITH FCB STUBBED -- it asserts 'Appendix-A complete (handle API + implemented set)' with the FCB family AH=0Fh-24h (bead 509.9) documented as a known stub. InitechBase/SAMIR uses the handle API, so FCB north-star pull is low; build FCB later when the TPS Report Generator (8479.1, its flagship FCB consumer) needs it.

TOOLCHAIN (CDR-0001, operator 2026-06-08): interim OS-image toolchain is host 'gcc -m32 -ffreestanding -nostdlib' + nasm + ld (NOT i686-elf, which ADR-0002 mandates). Accepted time-limited deviation; remediate (build i686-elf cross-toolchain from source) when dev moves to a more capable device. Tracked: initech-6pm / docs/adr/CDR-0001.

ADR-BY-COMMITTEE SCOPE (operator refinement 2026-06-14): the committee-of-subagents ADR process applies to BIG FEATURES / major decisions ONLY (e.g. the DOS 5.0 scope amendment, major architecture choices), NOT every small or routine ADR. Small/routine decisions proceed normally on the main thread.

All project documents (ADRs, worklogs, specs, PRD revisions) are authored in enterprise corporate-committee 'Initech' house style -- document-control headers, defined terms, passive governance phrasing, sign-off matrices -- for period/aesthetic fidelity (ADR-0003 NFR-7, Experiential Conformance). Operator directive 2026-06-08.

FLAIR fidelity target / era axis: the Office Space 'Saving tables to disk' frame is MOST LIKELY Mac OS 8 Platinum (1997+), not System 7.0/7.1 -- the film is 1999, and the chrome reads as grayscale 3-D Platinum (Appearance Manager) rather than System 7's colored pinstripe + lavender bevel. CAVEAT: the frame is a Photoshop MOCK-UP (per CLAUDE.md), so version is partly moot -- the chrome STYLE is what matters. OPERATOR DECISION (2026-06-20): proceed with the System 7 base NOW, ACCRETE the System 8 / Platinum appearance layer LATER ('never delete, always accrete' = the InitechOS design stance). PLAN IMPLICATION: the canonical FLAIR spec must be ERA-LAYERED from the start (chrome_metrics / color tables / WDEF geometry / title-bar renderer tagged by era) so System 8 Platinum lands as additive era-deltas with no base rewrite, and red-green goldens stay valid per-era. ../system7-decomp already era-delta-tags everything (targets 7.0/7.1, notes '7.5+ adds Appearance-Manager refinements'); a future ../system8-decomp (or a platinum/ layer) would hold the Platinum goldens.

Headless Bochs in this env: only the rfb display plugin is built (no nogui/x/sdl/term). display_library:rfb blocks ~30s waiting for a VNC client and does NOT self-proceed to boot. A minimal RFB-3.3 handshake client (security type None) connecting to port 5900 unblocks it fully headless -- no xvfb/sudo/HITL. Reusable client: /tmp/rfb_unblock.py (port hold_secs). Bochs needs ata0:enabled=1 + ata0-master cyl=2,heads=2,spt=32 for the 128-sector image, com1 mode=file for serial, and -rc with 'c' to continue past the integrated debugger.

TESTING-GAP LESSON 2026-06-20: the HOST oracle test-exec-unit and the EMU gate test-exec are DIFFERENT -- test-exec-unit (in TEST_UNIT_GATES) does NOT exercise the real int 0x21 AH=4Bh do_exec path that the emu test-exec gate does. An EXEC-path / int21 / loader edit can pass 'make test-unit' (all host gates) yet break test-exec on the emulator. After ANY change to do_exec / the loader EXEC seam / int21 dispatch, re-run the EMU EXEC gates: test-exec, test-program, test-zs24-exec, test-ut6d, test-samir-boot (Rule 5). Do not commit EXEC-path changes on host-green alone.
